ACKNOWLEDGMENTS
Chapter 103. An Act relating to the acknowledgment of
instruments , the attestation of documents ,
the administration of oaths and affirma-
tions , the execution of depositions and af-
fidavits , and other notarial acts , hereto-
fore or hereafter taken before any commis-
sioned officer of the armed forces of the
United States , and providing that such in-
struments and documents executed by any
person who is a member of or actually
present with the armed forces of the Unit-
ed States or is outside the United States
for certain purposes , shall be legal , valid ,
and binding ; and providing for the form
of the instrument or document and what
proof shall be sufficient of the authority
of such commissioned officer to so act.
(House Bill No. 163 , James E. Quill ) ....

CHAPTER 2
(S. B. 14 )
AN ACT to amend and re - enact Section 133.220 , Kentucky
Revised Statutes , 1942 Edition , relating to the preparation
and delivery of tax bills for listed property and poll taxes .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , as follows :
That Section 133.220 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
1942 Edition , be amended and re -enacted , so that
said section , as amended and re -enacted , shall read
as follows , to -wit :
(1 ) The Department of Revenue shall annually
furnish to each county clerk sufficient bills and stubs
for his use in the preparation of the tax books of his
county with the exception of tax bills necessary for
listing a poll tax for taxpayers assessing no property
for taxation . The Department of Revenue shall fur-
nish these tax bills within ten days after the Ken-
tucky Tax Commission has rendered its certification .
The county shall annually furnish the county clerk
with sufficient bills and stubs for his use in the prepa-
ration of tax bills for all taxpayers liable for a poll tax
and who assess no property for taxation . The county
shall furnish the county clerk sufficient tax bills for
this purpose within ten days after the Board of
Supervisors has adjourned .
( 2 ) After receiving the forms the county clerk
shall make out for the use of the sheriff or collector a
book of tax bills and stubs . The book shall contain a
correct tax bill and stub for each taxpayer in the
county who has been listed by the county tax com-
missioner or County Board of Supervisors or by the
county clerk, and whose taxable property valuation
has been approved by the County Board of Super-
visors and equalized by the Kentucky Tax Commis-
sion. If the book is bound the cost of binding shall be
paid out of the county levy . Each tax bill and stub

CHAPTER 2 3
shall show the rate of tax upon each one hundred dol-
lars worth of property for state , county and school
purposes ; the name of the taxpayer ; the number of
acres of farm land and its value ; the number of lots
and their value ; the amount and value of notes and
money ; the value of mixed prsonal property , the
county poll ; the school poll ; the school tax ; and the
total amount of taxes due to the state , county , school
fund and other levies . The county clerk shall attest
each tax bill and stub by signing it officially . In coun-
ties containing a city of the first class the clerk , at
the time of making out the tax bill , shall cause the
bill number of each tax bill to be inserted on the same
line as name of the taxpayer on the assessment roll .
(3 ) The book of tax bills and stubs prepared in
accordance with the certification of the Kentucky Tax
Commission shall be delivered to the sheriff or col-
lector by the county clerk before the second succeed-
ing September 15 following the assessment date . The
clerk shall take a receipt showing the number of tax
bills and the total amount of tax due each taxing dis-
trict as shown upon the tax bills . The receipt shall
be signed and acknowledged by the sheriff or collec-
tor before the county clerk , filed in the county court ,
and recorded in the order book of the county court in
the manner required by law for recording the official
bond of the sheriff .
(4 ) When the county clerk has forwarded his
final recapitulation to the Department of Revenue , as
provided in KRS 133.190 , he shall immediately make
out the tax bills of all taxpayers who are liable for
a poll tax and who assess no property for taxation .
When the bills are made out , the clerk shall immedi-
ately turn them over to the sheriff for collection .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

4 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 3
(S. B. 15 )
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 133.990 , Kentucky
Revised Statutes , 1942 Edition , relating to penalties to be im-
posed on certain officers for failure to discharge their duties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , as follows :
That subsection ( 7 ) of Section 133.990 , Kentucky
Revised Statutes , 1942 Edition , be repealed and sub-
section ( 2 ) enacted which shall read as follows , to-
wit :
(2) Any county clerk who fails to make out , for
the use of the sheriff or collector , the book or books
of tax bills and stubs provided in KRS 133.220 , and
deliver same to the sheriff or collector on or before
September 15 of each year , shall pay a penalty of
$10.00 for each day's delay which must be deducted
by the Department of Revenue from such sums , as
may be due , or become due from the Commonwealth
for official duties , and the date of the receipt required
to be signed by the sheriff or collector by the pro-
visions of KRS 133. 220 shall be prima facie evidence
of the delivery of same .
(3 ) Any county clerk who , without reasonable ex-
cuse , fails to return to the Department of Revenue
copies of any books , papers or records required by
it in the manner and at the time prescribed by law ,
shall , upon conviction , be fined not less than $ 10.00
nor more than $ 100.00 for each offense .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 4
(S. B. 23 )
AN ACT providing for the taking of the sense of the people of
the State as to the necessity and expediency of calling a con-
vention for the purpose of revising or amending the Consti-
tution of the Commonwealth , and such amendments as may
have been made to same as provided by Section 258 of the
present Constitution of Kentucky .

CHAPTER 4 5��
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the sense of the people of the State as
to the necessity and expediency of calling a conven-
tion for the purpose of revising or amending the
present Constitution , and such amendments as may
have been made to the same , shall be taken as herein
provided and as provided by section 258 of the pres-
ent Constitution of Kentucky .
§ 2. When this Act is passed at the present ses-
sion of the General Assembly by a majority of all
members elected to each house of the present Gen-
eral Assembly, that is when a majority of all the
members of each house shall concur by a yea and
nay vote , to be entered upon their respective Jour-
nals , this Act shall be spread upon the respective
Journals of each house as a law .
§3. If the next session of the General Assembly
shall , in like manner concur in this Act after it be-
comes a law as herein provided , it shall provide for
having a poll opened in each voting precinct in this
state by the officers provided by law for holding gen-
eral elections at the next ensuing regular election to
be held for State officers or members of the House of
Representatives , which does not occur within ninety
days from the final passage of such law , at which time
and place the votes of the qualified voters shall be
taken for and against calling the convention , in the
same manner provided by law for taking votes in
other State elections . The vote for and against said
proposition shall be certified to the Secretary of State
by the same officers and in the same manner as in
State elections . If it shall appear that a majority
voting on the proposition was for calling a conven-
tion , and if the total number of votes for the calling
of the convention is equal to one -fourth of the number
of qualified voters who voted at the last preceding
general election in this State , the Secretary of State

6 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall certify the same to the General Assembly at its
next regular session , at which session a law shall be
enacted calling a convention to readopt , revise or
amend the present Constitution of this State , and
such amendments as may have been made thereto .
Section 4. That before any constitution agreed
upon by said convention shall take effect or become
operative , the same shall be submitted to the quali-
fied voters of this commonwealth , after at least ninety
days ' notice , and ratified by a majority of those
voting .
Passed March 13 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 5
(S. B. 26 )
AN ACT to amend Section 147 of the Constitution of Kentucky
so as to permit persons to vote by filing ballot with County
Clerk . This Act shall be known as the Blake , Swope , Moore
and Howard Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That upon concurrence of the three-fifths of all
members elected to each House , the Yeas and Nays
be taken thereon and entered in full in their respective
journals , Section One Hundred and Forty - Seven of
the Constitution be , and is amended and revised so
it shall read as follows :
The General Assembly shall provide by law for
the registration of all persons entitled to vote in cities
and towns having a population of five thousand or
more ; and may provide by general law for the regis-
tration of other voters in the State . Where registra-
tion is required , only persons registered shall have
the right to vote . The mode of registration shall be
prescribed by the General Assembly . In all elections
by persons in a representative capacity , the voting
shall be viva voce and made a matter of record ; but

CHAPTER 6 7
•
all elections by the people shall be by secret official
ballot, furnished by public authority to the voters
at the polls , and marked by each voter in private at
the polls , and then and there deposited , or any person
absent from the county of his legal residence , or from
the state , may be permitted to vote in a manner pro-
vided by law . Counties so desiring may use voting
machines , these machines to be installed at the ex-
pense of such counties . The word " elections " in this
section includes the decision of questions submitted
to the voters , as well as the choice of officers by them .
The General Assembly shall pass all necessary laws
to enforce this section , and shall provide that per-
sons illiterate , blind , or in any way disabled may have
their ballots marked or voted as herein required .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 6
(S. B. 27 )
AN ACT relating to Game and Fish , with particular reference
to the organization , personnel and functions of the state agency
to be charged with administration of the laws relating to Game
and Fish .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. The following sections of the statutes are cre-
ated to constitute a part of Chapter 150 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , and to read and be numbered
as follows :
150.021 ( 1 ) There is created an agency of the
State Government to be known as the Division of
Game and Fish , which shall constitute an independent
agency of the State Government within the mean-
ing of Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
The Division shall consist of a game and fish com-
mission , a director , and such conservation officers

8 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and other agents and employees as are provided for
in this chapter . The division shall enforce the laws
relating to wild life and exercise all necessary powers
incident thereto .
( 2 ) The word sportsmen as used in this act shall
mean a resident hunter or fishermen licensed in Ken-
tucky each year for the past two consecutive years .
150.022 ( 1 ) The Game and Fish Commissioner
shall consist of nine members , one from each Con-
gressional District , and not more than five of the
same political party .
( 2 ) The Governor shall appoint the members of
the Commission . Of the first Commission appointed ,
two members shall be appointed for terms of one
year , two for two years , two for three years , and
three for four years , and thereafter as their respec-
tive terms expire , their successors shall be appointed
for four-year terms .
(3 ) The first Game and Fish Commission ap-
pointed after the effective date of this Act shall be
appointed in the following manner : The Director of
the Division of Game and Fish in the Department of
Conservation , in the office as of the effective date
of this Act , shall within thirty days after the effec-
tive date of this Act , call a meeting of the sportsmen
in each of the Congressional Districts of the State .
Notice of the meetings shall be published at least two
times in a newspaper of state -wide circulation at least
thirty days previous to the meetings . At the meeting
in each district , the sportsmen in attendance shall
select five residents and citizens of the district , who
are well informed on the subject of wild life con-
servation and restoration , and recommend such per-
sons to the Governor for appointment to the Game
and Fish Commission . The Governor shall appoint
the members of the first Game and Fish Commission
from the names so submitted , within sixty days after
the effective date of this Act . Until such time as the

CHAPTER 6 9
first Game and Fish Commission appointed under
the provisions of this Act have met and organized ,
the Division of Game and Fish in the Department of
Conservation , as constituted on the effective date of
this Act , shall continue to function .
(4) Vacancies through expiration of terms of the
members of the commission shall be filled by appoint-
ment by the Governor from a list of five names from
each Congressional District , recommended and sub-
mitted by the sportsmen of the respective districts .
When the term of a member expires , the Director
shall call a meeting of the sportsmen in that district
not later than thirty days prior to the expiration of
the term of the member whose term expires in that
year. Notice of the meeting shall be given by publi-
cation at least two times in a newspaper having state-
wide circulation , at least thirty days previous to the
day set for the meeting . At such meeting , the sports-
men in attendance shall select and recommend to the
Governor a list of five residents and citizens of the
district who are well informed on the subject of wild
life conservation and restoration .
( 5 ) In the case of vacancies other than expira-
tions , the Governor shall fill the vacancy for the un-
expired part of the term from the names remaining
on the list last previously submitted for the district
with regard to which the vacancy exists .
(6 ) Each commissioner shall take the constitu-
tional oath of office and shall give bond in the sum
of one thousand dollars , which shall be filed in the
office of the Secretary of State . The premiums on such
bonds shall be paid out of the Game and Fish Fund .
(7) The Governor shall remove a commissioner
for inefficiency , neglect of duty , or misconduct in
office , delivering to him a copy of all charges and af-
fording him an opportunity of being publicly heard
in person or by counsel in his own defense , upon not

10 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
less than ten days ' notice . If a commissioner shall be
removed , the Governor shall file in the office of the
Secretary of State a complete statement of all charges
made against such commissioner and his findings
thereon , together with a complete record of the pro-
ceedings .
( 8 ) Each commissioner shall be entitled to reim-
bursement for actual and necessary traveling and
other expenses made by him in the discharge of his
official duties , such expense not to exceed five hun-
dred dollars per annum for each member , and to be
paid from the Game and Fish Fund .
(9 ) A majority of the commission shall constitute
a quorum for the transaction of any business , for the
performance of any duty , or the exercise of any power
of which they are invested .
( 10 ) The Division shall have its principal office in
Frankfort , Kentucky , and is authorized to purchase
all supplies , equipment and printed forms , and to
issue such notices and publications as the Director
may deem necessary to carry out the provisions of
this chapter .
150.023 ( 1 ) The members of the commission shall
meet in Frankfort within 30 days after their appoint-
ment and shall organize by electing from their mem-
bership a chairman and other officers . The commis-
sion shall thereafter meet quarterly , or as often as
may be necessary for the transaction of business ,
upon reasonable notice to each member of such meet-
ings .
(2 ) The commission shall conduct Civil Service
Examinations for all employees of the Division .
(3 ) The commission shall at all times keep a watch-
ful eye over the Division of Game and Fish , and ad-
vise the Director to take such action as may be
beneficial to the division in the interest of wild life
and conservation of natural resources .

CHAPTER 6 11
(4 ) The Director , with approval of the commis-
sion , shall establish such bureaus and subdivisions ,
and shall employ such experts , clerks and other em-
ployees as he deems necessary for the conduct of the
work of the division , and shall fix the compensation
of all employees of the division , to be paid out of the
Game and Fish Fund .
(5 ) The Director , with approval of the commis-
sion, shall authorize such scientific and other studies
as he deems necessary , and shall collect , classify and
disseminate such statistics , data and information as
in his discretion will tend to promote the objects of
this chapter .
(6 ) The Director , with approval of the commission
shall have the exclusive power to expend for the pro-
tection , conservation , propagation , and restoration of
game , birds , fish and fur bearing animals , all funds of
the state acquired for the protection , conservation ,
propagation or restoration of birds , animals , or fish ,
arising from licenses , gifts , or otherwise .
150.024 ( 1 ) The division shall have the right to
acquire such property as is necessary in the carry-
ing out of the purpose of this Act and shall have the
right of eminent domain as provided in KRS 416.010
to 416.080 inclusive .
150.061 ( 1 ) Upon the organization of the com-
mission , the commission shall appoint a director of
the Division of Game and Fish , who shall be a man
with knowledge of and experience in the requirements
for the protection , conservation and restoration of
the wild life resources of the state . The director shall
serve for an indefinite term, subject to removal by
the commission for the same causes and in the same
manner in which the Governor may remove a com-
missioner .
(2) The director shall receive such compensation
as the commission may determine , and shall be re-

12 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
imbursed for all actual and necessary traveling and
other expenses incurred by him in the discharge of
his official duties .
(3) Before entering upon the duties of his office
the director shall take and subscribe to the consti-
tutional oath of office , and shall, in addition thereto ,
swear or affirm that he holds no other public office ,
nor any position under any political committee or
party .
(4 ) The director shall execute and file with the Sec-
retary of State a bond in the sum of five thousand
dollars . The premium on such bond shall be paid from
the Game and Fish Fund .
(5 ) The director shall have general supervision
and control of all activities , functions , and employees
of the Division of Game and Fish . He shall enforce
all the provisions of the laws of the state relating to
wild animals , birds and fish , and shall exercise all
necessary powers incident thereto not specifically
conferred on the commission . The director shall make
an annual report of all receipts and disbursements
to the League of Kentucky Sportsmen .
150.071 ( 1 ) The members of the Game and Fish
Commission shall constitute , ex officio , a Civil Serv-
ice Commission for the Division of Game and Fish .
(2 ) The commission shall prescribe , propound , and
conduct such examinations as are properly commen-
surate with positions within the Division of Game
and Fish .
(3 ) The time and place for holding such examina-
tions shall be fixed by the commission , and notice
thereof shall be given at least ten days prior to the
fixed date by announcements through all leading state
papers , through press releases , and through distri-
bution in any other manner the commission may deem
necessary . Such announcements shall state the time

CHAPTER 6 13
and place , the qualifications required of applicants ,
and pertinent facts relative to positions to be filled .
(4) The commission shall , as soon after all exami-
nations as practical , grade the applicants and certify
to the director an eligible list of all applicants so ex-
amined who have made passing grade . The one pos-
sessing the highest average grade shall be listed first
and all others shall be listed numerically according to
the result of the examinations .
150.072 ( 1 ) All appointments in the Division of
Game and Fish shall be made by the director from the
three highest names on the eligible list provided for
in KRS 150.071 .
( 2) No appointment shall be made to any position
in the Division of Game and Fish save from the
eligible list provided for in subsection ( 4 ) of KRS
150.071 .
(3 ) Whenever the director shall determine an
emergency exists , he is empowered to make emer-
gency appointments . Such appointments need not
come from the eligible list . In no case shall any emer-
gency period exceed 90 days .
150.081 While retaining the right to vote as he
may please and to express privately his opinion of
all political subjects , no employee or officer of the
Division of Game and Fish , including the director ,
superintendent of hatcheries , and superintendent of
conservation officers , shall take any active part in
political management or political campaign , nor shall
he use his official authority or influence for the pur-
pose of interfering with an election or effecting the
result thereof , or for the purpose of coercing the poli-
tical action of any person or body .
150.083 ( 1 ) For violation of orders or failure to
perform duties , the director may suspend any em-
ployee or conservation officer for a period of time
not to exceed two months , or may impose a fine of

14 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
not more than two months pay, or impose both sus-
pension and fine . Conservation officers may also be
demoted for violation of orders . For a second or sub-
sequent violation of orders of failure to perform his
duties , the accused shall be dismissed by the director .
150.095 Conservation officers are authorized to
keep and bear arms in the same manner as all other
peace officers , and to exercise the use of such arms
as may be necessary in the exercise of their duties .
150.105 ( 1 ) Notwithstanding any other provi-
sions of this chapter , the director may , with the ap-
proval of the Commission , authorize conservation
officers or any other person to destroy or bring under
control in such manner as he deems necessary , any
wild animals , fish , or wild birds , protected or un-
protected , which are causing damage to persons ,
property , or other animals , fish or birds , and which in
his judgment should be eliminated or controlled to
prevent further damage .
( 2) Subsections ( 3 ) and ( 6 ) of section 150.010 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes are amended to read
as follows :
(3) " Commission" means the Game and Fish
Commission .
(6 ) " Division " means the Division of Game and
Fish .
(3 ) Sections 150.020 , 150.030 , 150.040 , 150.050 ,
150.060 , and 150.070 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes are repealed .
(4) Section 150.080 of the Kentucky Revised Sta-
tutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The director may appoint from the eligible list
provided for in subsection ( 4 ) of KRS 150.071 , as
many conservation officers as may be required to
efficiently enforce the game and fish laws in accord-
ance with this chapter .
( 2) Before discharging his official duties , each con-
servation officer shall give bond in the sum of five

CHAPTER 6 15
hundred dollars , with two or more sureties approved
by the director . The bond shall be filed in the office
of the Secretary of State .
(5) Section 150.240 of the Kentucky Revised Sta-
tutes is amended to read as follows :
The director , with the approval of the commission
may contract with any land-owner of the state , for a
specified term of years , to set aside and maintain land
as a game refuge . The contracts may be recorded in
the county clerk's office .
(6) Section 146.010 of the Kentucky Revised Sta-
tutes is amended to read as follows :
1. The Department of Conservation shall exercise
all administrative functions of the State relating to :
( a) The operation and maintenance of parks and
museums , except those allocated to the Department
of Library and Archives ;
(b ) The conservation and protection of natural
resources , except wildlife ; and
(c) The publicizing of natural resources , agri-
cultural , commercial and industrial development , and
tourist attractions .
( 2 ) The Department of Conservation may exer-
cise the right of eminent domain as provided in KRS
148.060 and 148.070 .
(7 ) Section 146.020 of the Kentucky Revised Sta-
tutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Department of Conservation shall be
headed by a Commissioner of Conservation , who
shall receive an annual salary of four thousand dol-
lars .
(2 ) The Department shall be divided into a Divi-
sion of Parks , a Division of Forestry , a Division of
Publicity , and a Division of Archeology .
( 8 ) Section 146.030 of the Kentucky Revised Sta-
tutes is repealed .

16 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(9 ) Nothing in this chapter is intended to prevent
the division from decreasing the number of em-
ployees , when in the opinion of the commission and
the director it is necessary for the efficient operation
of the Division . When the numbers are so decreased ,
the youngest in point of service shall be the first to
be released and shall be placed at the head of the
eligible list in the order of their seniority .
(10 ) For the purpose of carrying into effect the
provisions of this Act , any appropriations or funds
made or available to the Division of Game and Fish
as now constituted under the Department of Con-
servation by present session of the General Assembly ,
and any unencumbered balance of appropriations
or funds to such Division of Game and Fish as now
constituted under the Department of Conservation
for the fiscal year 1943-44 , shall be transferred to and
become available to the Division of Game and Fish
established under this Act .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 24 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 7
(S. B. 29)
AN ACT relating to the Department of Law ; repealing , amend-
ing , and re-enacting section 15.020 Kentucky Revised Statutes ;
prescribing the duties and powers of the Attorney General ;
providing for the employment and compensation of special
counsel for the Commonwealth ; and repealing sections 15.030
and 15.040 Kentucky Revised Statutes of 1942 , and also sec-
tions 12.150 to and including 12.180 Kentucky Revised Statutes
of 1942 , and chapter 106 of the Acts of 1942 ; and declaring an
emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Section 15.020 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes of 1942 is repealed , amended and re-
enacted so as to read as follows :

CHAPTER 7 17
"The Attorney General is the chief law officer of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and all of its de-
partments , commissions , agencies , and political sub-
divisions , and the legal adviser of all state officers ,
departments , commissions , and agencies , and when
requested in writing shall furnish to them his writ-
ten opinion touching any of their official duties , and
shall prepare proper drafts of all instruments of writ-
ing required for public use , and shall exerecise all
common law duties and authority pertaining to the
office of the Attorney General under the common law,
except when modified by statutory enactment . He
shall appear for the Commonwealth in all cases in
the Court of Appeals wherein the Commonwealth is
interested , and shall also commence all actions or
enter his appearance in all cases, hearings and pro-
ceedings in and before all other courts , tribunal , or
commissions in or out of the state , and attend to all
litigation and legal business in or out of the state re-
quired of him by law , or in which the Commonwealth
has an interest , and any litigation or legal business
that any state officer , department , commission , or
agency may have in connection with , or growing out
of, his or its official duties , except where it is made the
duty of the Commonwealth's Attorney or County
Attorney to represent the Commonwealth . When any
attorney is employed for any said agency , the same
shall have the approval of such agency before such
employment .
Section Two . A new section of the Kentucky Re-
vised Stautes is created , to be numbered 15.050 , and to
read as follows :
" (1) No state officer or agency shall employ or be
represented by an attorney other than the Attorney
General unless an emergency arises which , in the
opinion of the Attorney General , requires the employ-
ment of other or additional counsel to protect the
interest of the Commonwealth , or a litigation arises in

18 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
which the Attorney General has an adverse interest .
In either event the Attorney General shall in writing
set forth the reasons for the employment , and request
the Governor to employ other or additional counsel .
" (2) Before other or additional counsel is em-
ployed his fee and compensation shall be fixed by
written contract by the Attorney General and the
counsel , subject to the approval of the Governor ; and
the written request and a copy of the contract shall be
filed in the office of the Secretary of State ; copy of the
written request and of the contract shall be kept on file
in the office of the Attorney General . But in the event
that additional counsel must be employed because the
Attorney General has an adverse interest in the liti-
gation , the contract employing said other counsel
shall be made by the officer or agency involved , and the
counsel employed subject to the approval of the Gov-
ernor . A copy of such contract shall also be filed in the
office of the Secretary of State and the office of the At-
torney General .
"(3) The fees , compensations , and expenses con-
tracted to be paid to said other or additional counsel
shall be paid out of funds designated by the Governor .
Section Three . Sections 15.030 and 15.040 Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes of 1942 , and also sections 12.-
150 to and including 12.180 Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes of 1942 , and chapter 106 of the Acts of 1942 , are
hereby repealed .
Section Four . All acts and parts of acts in conflict
with this act are repealed to the extent of such conflict .
Provided , however , that nothing in this Act is in-
tended to or shall be construed to apply to any con-
tract of employment with any attorney or attorneys
heretofore made pursuant to law , nor shall any of the
provisions of this Act be construed to prohibit or to
limit the right of any attorneys now employed by the
Commonwealth or any of its Department pursuant to
J

CHAPTER 8 19
contract made under statutory authority to represent
the Commonwealth or such Department in any suits ,
actions , or proceedings authorized under such con-
tracts of employment . The provisions of this Act shall
not apply to any of such cases .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 8
(S. B. 34 )
AN ACT to make it unlawful for any two or more insurance
companies , writing the same class , or classes , of risks , and
doing business in this Commonwealth , to enter into any
arrangement , contract, agreement , understanding , combina-
tion , or association to require , coerce , or induce any agent ,
or representative , of any two , or more , of such insurance
companies within this Commonwealth to refrain from rep-
resenting any other such insurance company , or companies ,
authorized to write the same class , or classes , of risks in this
Commonwealth , and doing business herein , or to offer , or
afford , any advantage to such agent , or representative , for
refraining from representing such other insurance company ,
or companies ; or to impose upon such agent any disadvantage
by reason of his acting as representative of such other insur-
ance company, or companies , or for any insurance agent
representing two or more insurance companies , authorized to
write .the same class , or classes , of business in this Common-
wealth , and doing business herein , to enter , directly or in-
directly , into any agreement , arrangement , contract or
understanding with one or more of such companies that he
will refrain from representing any other company , or com-
panies , authorized to write the same class , or classes , of
business in this Commonwealth , and doing business herein ;
and to provide penalties for the violation of said Act.
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. It is hereby declared to be unlawful for any two
or more insurance companies , authorized to write
the same class , or classes , of risks in this Common-
wealth , and doing business herein , directly or in-
directly, to enter into any arrangement , contract ,

20 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
agreement , understanding, combination , or associa-
tion to require, coerce , or induce any agent , or repre-
sentative , of any two or more of such insurance com-
panies within this Commonwealth , to refrain from
representing any other insurance company , or com-
panies , authorized to write the same class , or classes ,
of risks in this Commonwealth , and doing business
herein , or to offer or afford any advantage to such
agent , or representative , for refraining from repre-
senting such other insurance company , or companies ,
or to impose , or threaten to impose , upon such agent ,
or representative , any penalty , or disadvantage , if
he should agree to , or does , act as representative of
such other insurance company , or companies , in this
Commonwealth , or any part thereof .
2. It shall be unlawful for any insurance agent
representing , or acting for, two ( 2 ) or more insurance
companies authorized to write the same class , or
classes , of risks in this Commonwealth , and doing
business herein , to enter , either directly or indirectly ,
into any agreement , arrangement , contract , or under-
standing with one ( 1 ) or more of such companies that
he will refrain from representing in this Common-
wealth, or any part thereof, any other insurance com-
pany , authorized to write the same class , or classes ,
of risks in this Commonwealth , and doing business
herein .
3. Nothing herein contained shall be deemed , or
construed , as declaring it to be unlawful for any in-
surance company authorized to write risks in this
Commonwealth to enter into a bona fide contract with
an agent whereby the latter agrees that he will there-
after represent said single company exclusively in
this Commonwealth , or any part thereof, or as de-
claring that it shall be unlawful for any agent to
enter into such a contract with a single insurance
company .
4. For violation of any provision of this Act , the

CHAPTER 8 21
license of the offending company , or companies , or
agent , to transact the business of insurance within
this Commonwealth shall be suspended for a period
of three ( 3 ) years . Whenever information of any such
violation shall come to the knowledge , or be brought
to the attention, of the Director of the Division of
Insurance of this Commonwealth , he shall issue and
cause to be sent by registered mail , with postage pre-
paid , to the company , or companies , and/or agent or
agents , suspected , or accused of violating same , and
to any person , who has , to the Director's knowledge ,
accused them of doing so , at their last known ad-
dresses , a notice fixing a day certain , not less than
twenty ( 20 ) , nor more than thirty ( 30 ) days from
the date of issuance of said notice , upon which the
company , or companies , or agent , accused or sus-
pected of having violated same shall appear and show
cause why its , his , her , or their license , or licenses , to
transact the business of insurance in this Common-
wealth should not be revoked for a period of three ( 3)
years , which notice shall specify with reasonable
certainty the offense charged . If, after a hearing , held
pursuant to said notice , the Director shall determine
that any company , or the agent , thus notified to
appear is guilty of violating the provisions of section
1 or 2 of this Act, he shall forthwith suspend the
license of the company , or agent , so found guilty , to
transact the business of insurance in this Common-
wealth for a period of three ( 3 ) years . The hearing
before the director hereinabove provided for shall be
public and at any such hearing any person , or cor-
poration , having lodged information of any alleged
violation with the Director , as well as the alleged
offender , or offenders , shall have the right to be
present , be represented by counsel , and introduce evi-
dence as to whether the accused was , or was not,
guilty of the offense charged . Within thirty (30 ) days
after the issuance of an order by the Director sus-

22 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pending the license of any company , or agent , to
transact the business of insurance in this Common-
wealth for a period of three years , because in his
opinion such company , or agent , has violated the pro-
visions of Section 1 or 2 of this Act, the Company , or
agent , whose license has been so suspended shall be
entitled to appeal from the decision of the Director
by suit brought in the Franklin Circuit Court , which
Court shall hear the entire matter de novo and enter
judgment in accordance with the law and the evi-
dence presented to it on the trial of said cause .
3. All Acts , or parts of Acts , in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Exceptions . None of the provisions of this Act shall
apply to assessment or cooperative associations or
companies , mutual companies , reciprocal or inter-
insurance exchanges , their attorneys in fact , or their
agents , servants or employees or representatives .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 9
(S. B. 37 )
AN ACT to provide for an amendment to Section 230 of the
Constitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , pertaining
to the withdrawal of money from the State Treasury and the
purposes to which money paid into the State Treasury may
be devoted .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 230 of the Constitution of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky be , and the same is here-
by amended , so that said section , when amended and
re -enacted , shall read as follows :
Section 230. MONEY NOT TO BE DRAWN
FROM TREASURY UNLESS APPROPRIATED ;
PUBLICATION ; HIGHWAY FUNDS .- No money
shall be drawn from the state treasury , except in

CHAPTER 10 23
pursuance of appropriations made by law ; and a
regular statement and account of the receipts and
expenditures of all public money shall be published
annually .
No money derived from excise or license taxation
relating to gasoline and other motor fuels , and no
monies derived from fees , excise or license taxation
relating to registration , operation , or use of vehicles
on public highways shall be expended for other than
the cost of administration , statutory refunds and ad-
justments , payment of highway obligations , costs for
construction , reconstruction , rights -of -way , mainten-
ance and repair of public highways and bridges , and
expense of enforcing state traffic and motor vehicle
laws .
§ 2. This amendment shall be submitted to the
voters of the state for their ratification or rejection
at the time and in the manner provided for under
sections 256 and 257 of the Constitution of Kentucky
and under provisions of Section 118.430 of K.R.S.
Passed March 9, enrolled March 11 , approved March 14 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 10
(S. B. 39 )
AN ACT relating to the Active Militia and providing for pay ,
medical treatment and funeral expenses of Active Militiamen
who may die or become disabled by injuries received when
on , or as the result of , duty as such .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. If any member of the Active Militia shall be
incapacitated from pursuing his usual business or
occupation by reason of any injury received when on
duty or assembled therefor under competent au-
thority or lawful order, which said injury shall
directly result from such service , he shall be entitled
to receive the pay provided by Kentucky Revised

24 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Statutes 37.270 and actual necessary expenses for
care , hospitalization and medical attendance during
the period of such incapacity not exceeding ninety
days from the date of receiving such injury . Where a
claim is made under this section , the Adjutant
General may cause examinations of the claimant to
be made from time to time by a medical officer or
officers designated by him , and he may direct the re-
moval of the claimant to and his treatment in a hos-
pital designated by the Adjutant General , and if the
claimant refuse to permit such examination or if he
refuse to go to such hospital or to follow the advice
given or treatment prescribed for him therein , he
shall forfeit and be barred from all right to any claim,
pay or allowance under this section .
§ 2. If the death of any member of the Active
Militia results from an injury received when on duty
or assembled therefor under competent authority or
lawful order the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall
pay his funeral expenses , but not to exceed $ 250.00 .
§ 3. The body of an officer or enlisted man , who
dies while in active field service , or on active duty
while his command is absent from its home station ,
shall , under the direction of his commanding officer ,
be promptly prepared for burial , enclosed in a suit-
able casket , and returned , at the expense of the State ,
to his late residence under a proper escort ; provided ,
however , that the cost of the casket and preparing the
body for burial shall not exceed the sum of $ 250.00 as
allowed by Section 1 of this Act . All disbursements
under Sections 1 , 2 and 3 of this Act shall be a proper
charge against the State and shall be paid from the
Treasury on properly certified and approved
vouchers .
§ 4. If any member of the Active Militia , when on
duty or assembled therefor under competent author-
ity or lawful order , shall do any act in the discharge
of his duty, the doing of which causes him to be pro-

CHAPTER 11 25
ceeded against by civil court action , it shall be the
duty of the Attorney General to represent the mem-
ber of the Active Militia in any civil proceedings so
begun . If, under the same conditions , a member of
the Active Militia is proceeded against in a criminal
action , the Adjutant General may , with the approval
of the Governor , appoint for said member of the
Active Militia , legal counsel , who shall represent said
member in any criminal proceedings so begun . The
compensation paid the legal counsel shall be fixed by
the Adjutant General, with the written approval of
the Governor , and upon proper certification shall be
paid from the appropriation for the Department of
Military Affairs as other claims against that De-
partment are paid .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 23 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 11
(S. B. 40 )
AN ACT to authorize the appointment of a nominee by banking
institutions acting in a fiduciary capacity and fixing a penalty
for the violation of the same .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Any bank or trust company when acting as execu-
tor, administrator , guardian , committee , agent , or
trustee , whether alone or jointly with an individual or
individuals , may with the consent of the individual
fiduciary or fiduciaries , if any , (who are hereby
authorized to give such consent ) cause any stock or
other securities held in any such capacity to be reg-
istered and held in the name of a nominee or nominees
of such bank or trust company , or in its own name .
Any such bank or trust company shall be absolutely
liable for any loss occasioned by the acts of any
nominees of such bank or trust company with respect
to such stock or other securities so registered . But

26 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
no liability for any loss occasioned by the acts of any
such bank or trust company , or the nominee of either
of them with respect to such stock or securities so
registered , shall be imposed upon the corporation , its
transfer agent or registrar , which registers its stock
or other securities in the name of such bank or trust
company , or the nominee of either of them , in accord-
ance with the provisions of this act .
The records of such bank or trust company shall
at all times show the ownership of any such stock or
other securities . Such stock or other securities shall
at all times be kept separate and apart from the assets
of such bank or trust company .
Any person violating any of the provisions of this
section shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and fined
not less than $50.00 nor more than $2,000.00 .
Passed February 16 , enrolled February 17 , approved February
24 , 1944 .
������� 12
(S. B. 42 )
AN ACT to make uniform the law of transfer of shares of stock
in corporations .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. Title to a certificate and to the shares
represented thereby can be transferred only ,
(a ) By delivery of the certificate indorsed either
in blank or to a specified person by the person appear-
ing by the certificate to be the owner of the shares
represented thereby , or
(b ) By delivery of the certificate and a separate
document containing a written assignment of the cer-
tificate or a power of attorney to sell , assign , or trans-
fer the same or the shares represented thereby ,
signed by the person appearing by the certificate to
be the owner of the shares represented thereby . Such

CHAPTER 12 27
assignment or power of attorney may be either in
blank or to a specified person .
The provisions of this section shall be applicable
although the charter or articles of incorporation or
code of regulations or by -laws of the corporation issu-
ing the certificate and the certificate itself , provide
that the shares represented thereby shall be transfer-
able only on the books of the corporation or shall be
registered by a registrar or transferred by a transfer
agent .
Sec . 2. Nothing in this act shall be construed as
enlarging the powers of an infant or other person
lacking full legal capacity , or of a trustee , executor
or administrator , or other fiduciary , to make a valid
indorsement , assignment or power of attorney .
Sec . 3. Nothing in this act shall be construed as
forbidding a corporation ,
(a) To recognize the exclusive right of a person
registered on its books as the owner of shares to re-
ceive dividends , and to vote as such owner , or
( b) To hold liable for calls and assessments a
person registered on its books as the owner of shares .
Sec . 4. The title of a transferee of a certificate
under a power of attorney or assignment not written.
upon the certificate , and the title of any person claim-
ing under such transferee , shall cease and determine
if, at any time prior to the surrender of the certificate
to the corporation issuing it , another person , for
value in good faith , and without notice of the prior
transfer , shall purchase and obtain delivery of such
certificate with the indorsement of the person appear-
ing by the certificate to be the owner thereof , or shall
purchase and obtain delivery of such certificate and
the written assignment or power of attorney of such
person , though contained in a separate document .
Sec . 5. The delivery of a certificate to transfer
title in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 , is

28 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
effectual , except as provided in Section 7 , though
made by one having no right of possession and having
no authority from the owner of the certificate or from
the person purporting to transfer the title .
Sec . 6. The indorsement of a certificate by the
person appearing by the certificate to be the owner
of the shares represented thereby is effectual , except
as provided in Section 7 , though the indorser or trans-
feror ,
(a ) was induced by fraud , duress or mistake , to
make the indorsement or delivery , or
(b) has revoked the delivery of the certificate , or
the authority given by the indorsement or delivery of
the certificate , or
(c) has died or become legally incapacitated after
the indorsement , whether before or after the delivery
of the certificate , or
(d ) has received no consideration .
Sec . 7. If the indorsement or delivery of a certif-
icate ,
(a) was procured by fraud or duress , or
(b) was made under such mistake as to make the
indorsement or delivery inequitable , or
If the delivery of a certificate was made
(c) without authority from the owner , or
(d) after the owner's death or legal incapacity ,
the possession of the certificate may be reclaimed and
the transfer thereof rescinded , unless :
( 1 ) The certificate has been transferred to a pur.
chaser for value in good faith without notice of any
facts making the transfer wrongful , or,
( 2 ) The injured person has elected to waive the
injury , or has been guilty of laches in endeavoring to
enforce his rights .
Any court of appropriate jurisdiction may enforce
specifically such right to reclaim the possession of
the certificate or to rescind the transfer thereof and ,

CHAPTER 12 29
pending litigation , may enjoin the further transfer of
the certificate or impound it.
Sec . 8. Although the transfer of a certificate or
of shares represented thereby has been rescinded or
set aside , nevertheless , if the transferee has posses-
sion of the certificate or of a new certificate represent-
ing part or the whole of the same shares of stock , a
subsequent transfer of such certificate by the trans-
feree , mediately or immediately , to a purchaser for
value in good faith , without notice of any facts mak-
ing the transfer wrongful , shall give such purchaser
an indefeasible right to the certificate and the shares
represented thereby .
Sec . 9. The delivery of a certificate by the person
appearing by the certificate to be the owner thereof
without the indorsement requisite for the transfer of
the certificate and the shares represented thereby , but
with intent to transfer such certificate or shares ,
shall impose an obligation , in the absence of an agree-
ment to the contrary , upon the person so delivering ,
to complete the transfer by making the necessary in-
dorsement . The transfer shall take effect as of the
time when the indorsement is actually made . This
obligation may be specifically enforced .
Sec . 10. An attempted transfer of title to a certif-
icate or to the shares represented thereby without
delivery of the certificate shall have the effect of a
promise to transfer and the obligation , if any , im-
posed by such promise shall be determined by the law
governing the formation and performance of con-
tracts .
Sec . 11. A person who for value transfers a cer-
tificate , including one who assigns for value a claim
secured by a certificate , unless a contrary intention
appears , warrants-
( a) That the certificate is genuine ,
(b) That he has a legal right to transfer it , and

30 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(c) That he has no knowledge of any fact which
would impair the validity of the certificate .
In the case of an assignment of a claim secured by
a certificate , the liability of the assignor upon such
warranty shall not exceed the amount of the claim .
Sec . 12. A mortagee , pledgee , or other holder for
security of a certificate who in good faith demands or
receives payment of the debt for which such certificate
is security , whether from a party to a draft drawn
for such debt , or from any other person , shall not by
so doing be deemed to represent or to warrant the
genuineness of such certificate , or the value of the
shares represented thereby .
Sec . 13. No attachment or levy upon shares of
stock for which a certificate is outstanding shall be
valid until such certificate be actually seized by the
officer making the attachment or levy , or be sur-
rendered to the corporation which issued it , or its
transfer by the holder be enjoined . Except where a
certificate is lost or destroyed , such corporation
shall not be compelled to issue a new certificate for
the stock until the old certificate is surrendered to it.
Sec . 14. A creditor whose debtor is the owner of a
certificate shall be entitled to such aid from courts of
appropriate jurisdiction , by injunction and otherwise ,
in attaching such certificate or in satisfying the claim
by means thereof as is allowed at law or in equity , in
regard to property which cannot readily be attached
or levied upon by ordinary legal process .
Sec . 15. There shall be no lien in favor of a cor-
poration upon the shares represented by a certificate .
issued by such corporation and there shall be no
restriction upon the transfer of shares so represented
by virtue of any by -laws of such corporation , or other-
wise , unless the right of the corporation to such lien
or the restriction is stated upon the certificate .
Sec . 16. The alteration of à certificate , whether
fraudulent or not and by whomsoever made , shall not

CHAPTER 12 31
deprive the owner of his title to the certificate and
the shares originally represented thereby , and the
transfer of such a certificate shall convey to the trans-
feree a good title to such certificate and to the shares
originally represented thereby .
Sec . 17. Where a certificate has been lost or de-
stroyed , a court of competent jurisdiction may order
the issue of a new certificate therefor on service of
process upon the corporation and on reasonable notice
by publication , and in any other way which the court
may direct , to all persons interested , and upon satis-
factory proof of such loss or destruction and upon
the giving of a bond with sufficient surety to be ap-
proved by the court to protect the corporation or any
person injured by the issue of the new certificate from
any liability or expense , which it or they may incur
by reason of the original certificate remaining out-
standing . The court may also in its discretion order
the payment of the corporation's reasonable costs and
counsel fees .
The issue of a new certificate under an order of the
court as provided in this section , shall not relieve the
corporation from liability in damages to a person to
whom the original certificate has been or shall be
transferred for value without notice of the proceed-
ings or of the issuance of the new certificate .
Sec . 18. In any case not provided for by this act ,
the rules of law and equity , including the law
merchant , and in particular the rules relating to the
law of principal and agent , executors , administrators
and trustees , and to the effect of fraud , misrepresenta-
tion , duress or coercion , mistake , bankruptcy , or other
invalidating cause , shall govern .
· Sec . 19. This act shall be so interpreted and con-
strued as to effectuate its general purpose to make
uniform the law of those States which enact it.
1

32 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 20. A certificate is indorsed when an assign-
ment or a power of attorney to sell , assign , or trans-
fer the certificate or the shares represented thereby
is written on the certificate and signed by the person
appearing by the certificate to be the owner of the
shares represented thereby , or when the signature of
such person is written without more upon the back
of the certificate . In any such cases a certificate is
indorsed though it has not been delivered .
Sec . 21. The person to whom a certificate was
originally issued is the person appearing by the
certificate to be the owner thereof, and of the shares
represented thereby , until and unless he indorses the
certificate to another specified person , and thereupon
such other specified person is the person appearing
by the certificate to be the owner thereof until and
unless he also indorses the certificate to another speci-
fied person . Subsequent special indorsements may
be made with like effect .
Sec . 22. ( 1 ) In this act , unless the context or
subject matter otherwise requires—
"Certificate " means a certificate of stock in a
corporation organized under the laws of this state
or of another state whose laws are consistent with
this act .
"Delivery " means voluntary transfer of posses-
sion from one person to another .
"Person " includes a corporation or partnership
or two or more persons having a joint or common
interest .
To " purchase " includes to take as mortgagee or
as pledgee .
"Purchaser " includes mortgagee and pledgee .
" Shares " means a share or shares of stock in a
corporation organized under the laws of this state
or of another state whose laws are consistent with
this act.

CHAPTER 13 33
"State " includes state , territory , district and in-
sular possession of the United States .
" Transfer" means transfer of legal title .
"Title" means legal title and does not include a
merely equitable or beneficial ownership or interest .
"Value " is any consideration sufficient to support
a simple contract . An antecedent or pre -existing ob-
ligation , whether for money or not , constitutes value
where a certificate is taken either in satisfaction
thereof or as security therefor .
(2 ) A thing is done " in good faith " within the
meaning of this act , when it is in fact done honestly ,
whether it be done negligently or not .
Sec . 23. The provisions of the transfer act shall
apply to certificates for shares whether issued before
or after the taking effect of the transfer act , and to
transfers made in this state whether of certificates
for shares of domestic or foreign corporations , and
also , so far as applicable , to voting trust certificates
and stock purchase or subscription warrants which
shall be transferable in the same manner and with the
same effect as certificates for shares .
Sec . 24. All acts or parts of acts inconsistent with
this act are hereby repealed .
Sec . 25. This act may be cited as the Uniform
Stock Transfer Act.
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 16
(S. B. 51 )
AN ACT to repeal , amend , and re - enact Section 69.050 of Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes of 1942 , relating to salary , commissions
and compensations of Commonwealth's Attorneys .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly ofthe Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 69.050 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes of 1942 as adopted by the General Assembly at its

CHAPTER 17 37
1942 session , be and the same is hereby repealed ,
amended , and re -enacted , so that the same when so
amended and re -enacted , shall read as follows :
" Each Commonwealth's attorney shall receive an
annual salary of $ 500 to be paid out of the State
Treasury . In addition thereto and in lieu of all taxed
fees and perquisites he shall be allowed the sum of
$4,500 per annum out of the fines and forfeitures
rendered in favor of the Commonwealth in the several
courts in his district , to be paid monthly out of the
fines and forfeitures collected and paid into the State
Treasury each month . Except as provided in KRS
150.160 he shall receive forty percent of all fines and
forfeitures recovered in prosecutions before any
county judge or magistrate and all of the fines and
forfeitures rendered in circuit court except that part
now allowed the circuit clerk and the county attorney .
When the fines and forfeitures rendered in favor of
the Commonwealth in the several courts in his district
in any year are not sufficient to allow him $4,500 and in
the succeeding year they are sufficient to exceed that
amount , he shall be paid out of the excess , if sufficient ,
the sum necessary to bring his compensation from
fines and forfeitures for the preceeding year to $4,500 .
In no case shall he receive more than $5,000 out of the
State Treasury for any one year, but the fiscal court
of any county may allow the Commonwealth's at-
torney for that county additional compensation as it
deems proper , to be paid as other claims against the
county are paid . '

CHAPTER 17
(S. B. 55 )
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 431.130 , Kentucky
Revised Statutes .

38 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 431.130 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
be amended and re - enacted so that when so amended
and re -enacted it shall read as follows :
A jury by whom an offender is tried in any Court
of this Commonwealth shall fix by its verdict a punish-
ment to be inflicted within the periods or amounts pre-
scribed by law . Prisoners sentenced to punishment
by confinement in the penitentiary shall be kept at
hard labor . If the punishment is a fine or imprison-
ment in the jail of the county , or both , the imprison-
ment shall be close confinement in the jail of the county
where the trial was held , unless otherwise provided .
The prisoner shall also be confined in the county jail
until the costs are paid , unless otherwise provided .
In any jurisdiction where there is a county workhouse
the Court may in its discretion confine the prisoner to
the county work house instead of the county jail .
Passed and enrolled March 15, approved March 20 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 20
(S. B. 61 )
AN ACT repealing and re - enacting Section 489 of the Civil Code
of Practice , being a part of Chapter 137 , Acts of 1942 .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 489 of the Civil Code of Practice (being a

40 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
part of Chapter 137 , Acts of 1942 ) is repealed and the
following is enacted in lieu thereof.
Section 489. Sale of Real Estate of Infants and
Persons of Unsound Mind .
1. Purposes of Sale. The real property or any
right , title or interest in real property of a person
under the age of twenty -one years , or of a person of
unsound mind, whether such person be a resident or
a non -resident of this State , may be sold under or by
decree of a Court of Equity of the County in which
the property or any part thereof is situated for any
purpose deemed by the chancellor to be necessary or
proper or beneficial to such person under disability .
2. Parties . The decree may be rendered on a
petition , counter -claim or cross -petition filed by the
statutory guardian of an infant , or by the committee
of a person of unsound mind , or by a next friend , or
by any party against a guardian or committee , if he
have one residing in this State , in any kind of similar
proceeding that may be maintained against a person
who is not under disability .
Except in actions brought under sub- section eight
( 8 ) hereof, if the guardian or committee of a person
under disability be a party to the action , then it shall
not be necessary for the person under disability to be
made a party personally .
If the guardian or committee of a person under dis-
ability be not a party to the action , then it shall be
necessary for the person under disability to be a party
personally and brought before the Court in the
manner authorized by this Code .
Nothing contained in this sub- section shall limit the
purposes of sale named in sub -section 1 hereof.
If the suit be filed against a guardian or committee
and he shall fail to appear and defend or show that no
good defense can be made , the court shall appoint a
guardian ad litem to do so.

CHAPTER 20 41
The3. SUBSTANCE OF THE PLEADING .
pleading seeking a sale shall file the title papers , or
copies thereof, under which the property is held ; shall
particularly describe the property and the interest
therein of the person under disability ; set forth fully
the reasons or purposes for which it is sought to have
the same sold ; and the nature and extent of all liens
upon the interest of the person , if any , so far as the
same may be known to the petitioner .
4. REFERENCE TO COMMISSIONER . The
case may be referred by order of Court to the Master
Commissioner for hearing of evidence and report on
the facts and his recommendation with respect to the
relief sought in the pleadings .
5. PROOF REQUIRED . The allegations of the
pleading shall not be taken as confessed but shall be
proved by competent evidence heard on deposition or
orally by the chancellor or commissioner . A transcript
thereof shall be filed as part of the record .
6. Payment of Proceeds - Bond . The proceeds
of any sale may be paid to the guardian or committee
provided that he shall have executed in the Circuit
Court a bond with surety to the Commonwealth for
the use and benefit of the person under disability that
he will faithfully discharge his trust and will account
for , pay and deliver to said person all money and other
property due or belonging to him when he shall reach
his majority , or if he be a person of unsound mind
when he shall be legally restored , or to his heirs or
personal representative , or in compliance with an
order of any court of competent jurisdiction . The
court shall endorse its approval on said bond , which
shall be recorded on the order book .
7. Judgment - Guardian or Committee May Make
Sale. Subject to the above conditions , judgment may
be rendered and executed in the same manner and on

42 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the same terms as judgments in similar cases where
no person under disability is a party .
Provided , that (a ) the Court may in its discretion
authorize the guardian or committee of such person
or the Commissioner of the Court to make said sale
at public auction upon such terms and conditions as
the court may order , and to convey title thereto ; or
(b) if the land or interest to be sold be of an appraised
value of less than $1,000 , the court may in the judg-
ment authorize such fiduciary to make the sale
privately and convey title ; subject , however , to the
approval of the chancellor before consummation .
8. Private Sale- Approval of Court . Without
previous judicial proceeding or judgment the
guardian of an infant or the committee of a person of
unsound mind may sell privately or at public auction
and convey any interest in real estate owned by a
ward for such reasons and purposes and upon such
terms and conditions as he shall deem best for the
interest of said ward . Provided , however , that no
such sale shall be consummated until same shall have
been approved by the judge of the Circuit Court in the
County where the land or a portion thereof is situated
in a suit in equity filed by the guardian or committee
against said infant or person of unsound mind . The
petition shall set forth the reasons and purposes of
the sale and the terms thereof .
If the person under disability be brought before the
Court by service of summons , a guardian ad litem
shall be appointed for such person . Proof shall be
taken only on notice to said guardian ad litem or , if
the person under disability be constructively sum-
moned , on notice to his warning order attorney . Proof
may be taken by depositions or orally before the chan-
cellor and thereafter transcribed and filed in the
record of the case . The guardian ad litem or warning
order attorney shall make a defense or file a report

CHAPTER 21 43
stating that after careful examination of the case he
is unable to make a defense .
If the chancellor shall deem the sale advisable and
the terms and price satisfactory , he shall enter an
order approving the same ; provided , however , that
the judgment shall not be entered until the guardian
or committee shall have executed a bond with ap-
proved surety in an amount not less than the sum due,
or to become due , his ward from said sale providing
that he will faithfully discharge all his duties as such
guardian or committee and will comply with the
judgment and orders of the court , and account for ,
pay and deliver the proceeds of the sale or property
in which it shall be invested to his ward or other
person entitled thereto . The bond shall be approved
by the court and recorded in the order book . Provided
if the share of the infant in the proceeds does not
exceed $ 100.00 , exclusive of interest , no bond shall be
required of the guardian . This proceeding may be
tried and judgment rendered in accordance with the
provisions of Section 23.150 , Ky . Rev. Stats .
9. Court to Protect Rights . The court shall at all
times enter such orders as will conserve and protect
the rights of the person under disability .
10. Proceedings Not Void-Exceptions . No de-
fect or error in the proceeding shall render the judg-
ment or sale void unless the same is of the nature and
character that would render the proceeding void if all
parties were sui juris .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

§2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is created , to read as follows :
Any county clerk shall receive for record and
record any certified copy of any matter in reference
to bankruptcy which any Act of Congress of the
United States may provide for as being necessary to
be filed in the county wherein lands of a bankrupt are
situated in order to be notice of said bankruptcy . Such

for recording each such certified copy , to be paid by

48 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) Except as provided in KRS 29.090 , the circuit
judge of each county shall , at the first regular term of
circuit court each year, or, in the case of courts of
continuous session , at some time during the month of
January each year , appoint , as jury commissioners
for one year, three intelligent and discreet house-
keepers of the county over twenty -one years of age ,
resident in different portions of the county , having
no action in court requiring the intervention of a jury ,
and who have not served as jury commissioners in
the past year . They shall be sworn in open court to
faithfully discharge their duty. If any commissioner
cannot serve or cannot complete his service , the judge
may appoint another person of like qualifications in
his place .
( 2 ) The commissioners shall meet at such time ,
promptly after their appointment , as will enable them
to complete their duties not less than five days before
the date specified by KRS 29.130 for drawing jury
lists for the next term of court , or , in the case of courts
of continuous session , not less than five days before
the first Monday of the month following the month in
which they are appointed . The commissioners shall
remain in session not longer than five days , except
that in counties having a population of more than
fifty thousand they may remain in session ten days .
They shall hold their meetings in a room designated
by the judge . While engaged in making lists of juries ,
selecting the names and writing , depositing or draw-
ing them from the drum , no person shall be permitted
in the room with them except that the jury com-
missioners of courts of continuous session may select
a typist to aid them in the performance of their duty ,
who shall be sworn in open court to faithfully dis-
charge his duties and who shall be in attendance on
the commissioners in session . Such typist shall be
compensated at the same rate as the commissioners ,

CHAPTER 23 49
and his compensation shall be allowed and paid by the
fiscal court .
§3 . Section 29.070 is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The jury commissioners shall take the last
returned tax commissioner's book for the county and
from it shall carefully select from the intelligent ,
sober , discreet and impartial citizens , resident house-
keepers in different portions of the county , over
twenty -one years of age , the following number of
names of such persons :
(a ) In counties having a population of ten thou-
sand or less , not less than one hundred and sixty -two
nor more than two hundred .
(b ) In counties having a population of more than
ten thousand and not more than twenty thousand , not
less than one hundred and sixty -two nor more than
three hundred .
(c) In counties having a population of more than
twenty thousand and not more than fifty thousand ,
not less than five hundred nor more than six hundred .
(d) In counties having a population of more than
fifty thousand and not more than one hundred thou-
sand , one thousand .
(e) In counties having a population of more than
one hundred thousand , and its circuit court divided
into branches , two thousand for each division of the
court requiring the services of a jury .
( 2) They shall place the name , and in counties
containing a city of the first , second or third class ,
the address , of each person so selected , on a small slip
of paper , each slip being as near the same size and
appearance as practicable . In counties having courts
of continuous session in which the commissioners
have employed a typist , the typist shall type the names
on the slips ; in other counties the commissioners shall
write the names on the slips in plain handwriting .
Each slip shall be by them enclosed in a small case
and deposited , unsealed , in a revolving drum , but

50 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
before depositing any of the slips in the drum they
shall carefully examine its contents and remove and
destroy any slips found therein.
(3 ) The slips of paper upon which are written the
names of persons placed by the commissioners upon
the lists of grand and petit jurors shall be destroyed
by the commissioners as soon as the names are re-
corded on the list.
§4 . Section 29.080 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) When the commissioners have completed the
filling of the drum, they shall lock the drum and de-
liver it and the key , and all slip cases not used , to
the judge of the circuit court for which the drum has
been filled, who shall deliver the drum and the slip
cases to the circuit clerk in open court and at the time
administer to him and his deputies the following oath :
"You do solemnly swear that you will not open this
drum except in open court under the direction of the
judge of this court, and that you will not open the
sealed envelopes containing the lists of petit ( or
grand and petit jurors , as the case may be ) , for the
session of this court to be held in the month of
in the year of until the time fixed by law ;
that you will not , directly or indirectly , converse with
any one selected as a petit juror concerning any suit
for trial in this court , at its next term , unless by leave
of court . " If the clerk subsequently , in vacation , ap-
points a deputy he shall administer to him a like oath .
(2 ) No person, except as authorized by statute ,
shall open , unlock or break into , or in any way will-
fully injure the drum or lock , or willfully open or
break the seal of an envelope containing jury lists .
§5 . Section 29.090 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Circuit courts divided into two divisions shall
have four commissioners , two to be appointed by the

CHAPTER 23 51
judge of each division . They shall meet together and
shall have only one drum , except as provided in sub-
section ( 2 ) of this section .
( 2 ) In counties having circuit courts of continu-
ous session in which the court is authorized to hold
part of its sessions in a city of the second class not
the county seat , the court shall have one drum for use
at the county seat and another for use at the city of
the second class . The commissioners shall meet at

(4) No grand jury shall be made or grand jurors
drawn for any other than the criminal branch of a

§6. Section 29.130 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) At each term of circuit court , of circuit courts

the drum , reopen it and draw therefrom the names of
not less than thirty nor more than thirty -six persons ,
in the discretion of the judge , to act as petit jurors ,
record their names on paper , certify and sign it.
Sessions of a court having continuous session held at

52 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the county seat of a county in which there is a city of
the second class at which part of its sessions are auth-
orized to be held shall be deemed terms of court within
the meaning of this section .
(2 ) In courts of continuous session , except the
courts in counties containing a city of the first class ,
the judge of the court, or if the court has branches
the judge of the criminal branch , shall , in open court ,
on a day one week next before the day upon which
they shall be summoned to appear , draw from the
drum a sufficient number of names to procure twenty-
four persons qualified to act as grand jurors , record
the names of the twenty-four qualified upon paper ,
certify and sign it . In such courts the judge of the
court , or if the court has branches the judge of each
division requiring the services of a petit jury , shall,
in open court , every two weeks , on a day one week next
before the day upon which they shall be summoned to
appear , draw from the drum the names of not less
than thirty nor more than thirty-six persons to act
as petit jurors , record their names on paper, certify
and sign it . In such courts the names of all persons
drawn as grand and petit jurors shall be publicly an-
nounced when drawn , and entered of record in the
order drawn .
(3 ) In courts of counties containing a city of the
first class , the judge of the criminal branch shall , on a
day one week next before the day on which they shall
be summoned to appear , draw from the drum a suffi-
cient number of names , in the discretion of the judge ,
to procure a sufficient number of persons qualified to
act as grand jurors , and shall make a written record
thereof , which he shall certify and sign . In such courts
the judge of each division requiring the services of a
petit jury shall , every two weeks , on a day one week
next before the day upon which they shall be sum-
moned to appear , draw from the drum a sufficient

CHAPTER 23 53
number of names , not to exceed sixty , in the discretion
of the judge , to procure twenty -four persons qualified
to act as petit jurors . The clerk of the court or the
official stenographer , as directed by the court , shall

(4) In courts of continuous session , if the day on
which the drawing is prescribed to be done is a holi-
day, the judge shall draw the names on the following
day .
(5 ) The judge shall destroy the slips on which
are written the names placed on the lists as soon as
the names are recorded on the lists . If in drawing the
grand jurors the name of a person not having the
qualifications of a grand juror is drawn , it shall be

the name of a person temporarily disqualified is
drawn , it shall be replaced in the drum, but if the name
of any person permanently disqualified for any jury

(6) From the list of names of persons qualified to
act as grand jurors , the next grand jury shall be
