fied to act as petit jurors , not less than twenty -four
nor more than thirty jurors shall be selected , in the

$7. Section 29.140 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) In courts having terms , the judge shall place

ately after drawing the names from the drum the

54 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
judge shall lock the drum . The judge shall deliver
the lists and the drum to the clerk, at the same time
administering to the clerk and his deputies the oath
prescribed in subsection ( 1 ) of KRS 29.080 .
(2 ) In courts of continuous session the procedure
shall be the same as prescribed in subsection ( 1 ) of
this section for courts having terms , except as follows :
(a ) In counties other than those containing a city

)
AN ACT
toprovide jurisdiction and supervision over privately
owned airports and landing
-fields
inthe Kentucky Aeronautics
Commission
:
toprovide further for the condemnation
oflands
for airports and landing
-fields
, and for landing strips along
highways
: and for the improvement
ofairports by the ex-
tension
ofrunways across certain inferior county roads and
passways
; and for the regulation thereof
.

56 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
"
that the word " airport " , as the word is used herein ,
shall be synonymous , with " landing field ", or " avia-
tion field " , or "landing strip ", and shall indicate and
mean any place constructed for the landing or dis-
patch of machines or aircrafts in the navigation of
the air, and which has been approved and designated
for such purposes by The Kentucky Aeronautics Com-
mission .
SECTION 2. Where airports have heretofore or
may hereafter be established by any individual , part-
nership , or corporation organized for the purpose of
establishing the same, such individual , partnership ,
or corporation may , if he or it so desires , file an ap-
plication with the State Aeronautics Commission ,
submitting to the jurisdiction thereof , and agreeing
to comply with all the requirements , rules and regu-
lations of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission ,
authorized by Chapter 183 Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , with respect to publicly owned airports , and
thereupon the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission
shall cause said airport to be inspected , and if found
to conform to the standards of public airports estab-
lished under said Chapter , the Kentucky Aeronautics
Commission shall then approve said application ; and
said airport shall be deemed to be established , and
to be within the jurisdiction of said Kentucky Aero-
nautics Commission , to the same extent as public air-
ports under said Chapter are so subject .
SECTION 3. The Kentucky Aeronautics Com-
mission , with the consent of the owner of any such
airport , may , by resolution , reciting that any lands or
property adjoining an airport is needed for purposes
of establishing and maintaining an airport and facili-
ties to provide for transportation by air , of passen-

CHAPTER 24 57
gers , express and mail , and to provide an airport and
facilities for the use of the State or Federal Govern-
ment, direct the condemnation of property . Where-
upon , condemnation proceedings shall be conducted
in the name of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commission
at the expense of the individual , partnership , or cor-
poration owning or operating said airport , and the
procedure for condemnation shall conform to the pro-
cedure set out in KRS 416.120 , but the Commission
shall have no liability for the result of such proceed-
ings , and all cost , expense , damages , and compensa-
tion awarded shall be paid by the owners or oper-
ators of such airport seeking to acquire title to said
lands .
SECTION 4. Any airport operator within the
jurisdiction of the Kentucky Aeronautics Commis-
sion, shall have the right to extend and improve any
runway of such airport , over and across any County
Road , or pasway not heretofore designated by the
Kentucky State Highway Department as a part of
the Primary Road System of the Commonwealth , in
such manner as not unreasonably to obstruct by such
construction , the passage of vehicles on such road or
passway , and in the event any such runway is so ex-
tended or constructed , the public using such road shall
be entitled to the same protection in the use of said
road as in cases where public roads cross railroads ;
the operators of said airport at its expense shall re-
store to its former condition , as nearly as may be , any
such road across which it has constructed its runway ,
to the full width of said runway , and at least 100 feet
on each side thereof .
An automatic signal by bell or whistle upon said
road , or passway , installed at least 50 rods from the
said runway , which device shall ring or sound con-
tinuously or alternately , while any aircraft is using
any part of said runway , shall be provided by the

58 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
operator of said airport , and at its expense , it shall
cause signal boards to be placed and constantly main-
tained on any such road or passway one hundred feet
from the place where its runway crosses any such
road, which boards shall be substantially supported ,
and shall be placed so as to be seen easily by travelers ,
and not to obstruct travel , and shall bear on each side
in capital letters at least five inches high , the words
"DANGER , AIRPORT CROSSING , STOP , LOOK
AND LISTEN FOR AIRCRAFT " ; provided that
nothing herein shall allow the construction of any
runway over any hard surfaced or improved highway,
nor any road constituting a part of the Primary Road
System of the State .
SECTION 5. Any airport Operator may condemn
land adjoining any public road or highway in the
State for landing strips , and the procedure in such
cases shall be the same as hereinabove provided , with
respect to the condemnation of lands for airports .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 26
(S. B. 84 )
AN ACT consenting to the establishment of an independent
school district on the Fort Knox Military Reservation and for
the issuance of Motor Vehicle License Plates and Operators'
Licenses to residents of said reservation in consideration of
the State of Kentucky being released from all responsibility
for the establishment and maintenance of said school district ,
and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That , for the purpose of this Act only,
the Fort Knox Military Reservation , by and with the
consent of the War Department of the United States ,
be and the same is hereby set apart, established and
created a separate school district , to be designated
and known as the " Fort Knox Independent School
District . "
Section 2. That the Commanding Officer of the
Fort Knox Military Reservation , is hereby author-
ized and empowered to establish , maintain and con-
duct , a grade and high school for the said Fort Knox
Military Reservation .
Section 3. That whereas , with the advent of war in
1941 , the population of Fort Knox increased until

62 ACTS OF THE General ASSEMBLY
there are at the time of this enactment , more than
five hundred children within grade and high school
age living on the Fort Knox Military Reservation ,
and whereas there is no State or Federal law provid-
ing for the education of said children , and whereas
there is no adjacent school district within practical
reach of said children which has adequate housing
or instructional facilities for the education of said
children, and whereas construction of further facili-
ties for such purpose , in view of construction priori-
ties , would in current time be impossible , and with
view to normal times , impracticable and uneconomi-
cal , and whereas to meet the emergency and to the
relief of the said school districts adjacent to said Res-
ervation, by agreement with the proper officials of
the State of Kentucky , for the purpose of obtaining
revenue to establish , maintain and conduct a grade
and high school on said Reservation , the Commanding
Officer of said Fort Knox Military Reservation has
been authorized to issue to residents of said Military
Reservation an operator's license for privately owned
motor vehicles and to issue to resident owners of pri-
vately owned motor vehicles license plates for such
vehicles , said license plates containing the number
of the plate , the year in which issued , the word " Ken-
tucky" and the words " Fort Knox " , said agreement
as herein described is hereby ratified and confirmed
and the State of Kentucky hereby consents that said
agreement shall continue in effect until formally abro-
gated , and all motor vehicle license plates and all
motor vehicle operator's licenses issued by the Com-
manding Officer of the said Fort Knox Military Reser-
vation pursuant to said agreement , shall be treated
and considered as lawful operator's licenses and law-
ful motor vehicle license plates of the State of Ken-
tucky and shall entitle the holders thereof to the same
rights and privileges as those operator's licenses and

CHAPTER 27 63
motor vehicle license plates issued by the various
counties of the State of Kentucky .
Section 4. All Acts and parts of Acts in conflict
with this Act are hereby repealed .
Section 5. Whereas , War conditions require that
immediate steps be taken to provide adequate school
service at the Fort Knox Military Reservation , an
emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall take
effect from and after its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Passed March 9 , enrolled March 10 , approved March 14 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 27
(S. B. 88 )
AN ACT repealing , amending and re - enacting KRS 44.050 re-
lating to when motor vehicles may be purchased out of state
funds ; and providing that the Governor , Lieutenant - Governor
when acting as Governor , the Department of Highways and
its employees , and the head and the inspectors of the Depart-
ment of Mines and Minerals , and the Adjutant General of the
Department of Military Affairs may purchase and use pas-
senger motor vehicles out of state funds ; and providing that
the Department of Military Affairs may purchase truck motor
vehicles for the use of the department from state funds ; and
providing that Department of Military Affairs may acquire
motor vehicles by gift or transfer from the Federal Govern-
ment .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth ofKentucky :
Section 44.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Motor vehicles , when may be purchased out of the
state funds . (1 ) No state officer or member of a
board or commission created by the laws of this State
shall purchase or contract to purchase any motor pas-
senger vehicle on behalf of or on account of the State ,

64 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or for the use of any State officer or any member ,
agent or employee of any board or commission creat-
ed by the laws of this State , or for the use of any
person engaged in performing services for this state ,
except vehicles necessary for the use of the Governor ,
the Lieutenant Governor when acting as Governor ,
the Department of Highways and its employees , and
the head and the inspectors of the Department of
Mines and Minerals , and the Adjutant General of the
Department of Military Affairs , nor shall any pay-
ments be made for the purchase of such vehicles , ex-
cept those permitted by this section , out of state funds
or money paid into the State Treasury .
(2) No person except the Governor , the Lieuten-
ant Governor , the Department of Highways and its
employees , and the head and inspectors of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals , and the Adjutant
General of the Department of Military Affairs , shall
use or operate any passenger motor vehicle owned by
the State .
( 3) The Department of Military Affairs is hereby
authorized to purchase from state funds , motor trucks
to be used in the carrying out of the functions of this
department and it is further provided that the De-
partment of Military Affairs may acquire motor ve-
hicles by gift or transfer from the Federal Govern-
ment for use in said department .
Whereas , the present National Emergency requires
that the Adjutant General be able to travel to all
parts of the state in the most expeditious manner ,
now, therefore, an emergency hereby is declared by
the General Assembly to exist and this Act shall be-
come effective immediately upon its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 28
( S. B. 91 )
AN ACT relating to charters of the cities of the second and third
classes providing for the maintenance and operation of , the
creation of a depreciation fund , the fixing of rates and the
transfer of surplus funds derived from the operation of ,
municipally owned water works and electric light , heat and
power plants .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
( 1 ) At the time or before or after the issuance of
revenue bonds for the acquisition , extension or main-
tenance of a system of water works or electric light ,
heat and power plants in cities of the second and third
classes , which bonds do not represent the general ob-
ligation of the city, and city legislative body shall, by
ordinance , set aside and pledge the income and rev-
enue of any such municipally owned utility into a
separate and special fund to be used and applied in the
payment of the cost thereof, and in the maintenance ,
operation and depreciation thereof , and for the pur-
poses hereinafter set out . The ordinance shall defi-
nitely fix and determine the amount of revenue neces-
sary to be set apart and applied to the payment of
the principal and interest of the bonds , and the por-
tion of the balance of the income and revenue to be
set aside as a proper and adequate depreciation ac-
count , and the portion to be set aside for the reason-
able and proper operation and maintenance of the
utility , and may provide that the surplus not needed
for the purposes aforesaid shall be paid over to and
become a part of the general funds of such city . The
rates to be charged for services from the utility shall
be fixed and revised from time to time by the board
appointed to operate the utility by and with the ap-
proval of the legislative body of the city so as to be
sufficient to provide for the payment of interest upon
all bonds and to create a sinking fund to pay the prin-

66 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cipal thereof when due ; to provide for the operation
and maintenance of the utility and an adequate depre-
ciation account ; and such rates may be fixed as will
furnish a fair and reasonable return to the municipal-
ity on the fair value of the used and useful property of
the utility.
Nothing in this Act shall apply to electrical plants
acquired or operated under Provisions of Section
KRS 96.550 to 96.900 .
( 2) Whereas this act is of vital importance to the
administration of municipally owned water works
and light , heat and power plants in the State of Ken-
tucky and will clarify the present fiscal management
of such plants , an emergency is hereby declared to
exist and this act shall become effective immediately
upon its final passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed March 10, enrolled March 13 , approved March 14 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 29

monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That sub -section ( 3 ) of Section 202.120 ,
Kentucky Revised Statutes , be amended and reenact-
ed so that as amended and reenacted it shall read as
follows :
" (3) Within thirty days after such commitment ,
if the superintendent and clinical director , or a staff
physician acting in the latter capacity , of the institu-

the person is insane ( psychotic ) , they shall so certify

physicians to make the examination as provided in

or award of contracts therefor ) and shall include the

CHAPTER 31
(S. B. 96 )
AN ACT to repeal and re - enact Sections 162.431 , 162.435 , 162.440 ,
162.450 , 162.460 and 162.480 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
prepared by the Statute Revision Commission of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , 1942 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky that
Sections 162.431 , 162.435 , 162.440 , 162.450 , 162.460 ,
and 162.480 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes pre-
pared by the Statute Revision Commission of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , be and they are hereby
amended and re -enacted so that said Sections as
amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
162.431 : In addition to other taxes now levied ,
each city of the second class shall levy , and the County
Fiscal Court of a county containing a city of the sec-
ond class shall levy annually such tax of not less than
one cent nor more than five cents on each one hundred
dollars ' valuation of property assessed by the city
and/or the count , as shall be requested by the Board
of Education of the independent school district em-
bracing the city, or Board of Education of a county
containing a city of the second class , at the same time
the regular tax rate is fixed by the Board of Educa-
tion , to provide a fund for the Board of Education
for the purchase of sites for school buildings , for the
erection of school buildings and the complete equip-

72 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ping thereof , and for the major alteration and en-
largement of existing buildings and the complete
equipping thereof . In addition to , or in lieu of , the
above special tax , the board of education of any city
of the second class and/or of a county containing a
city of the second class may pay into this special fund ,
from time to time , all or any part of the receipts from
the sale of land or property no longer needed for
school purposes , and may from time to time pay into
this special fund all or any part of any balances re-
maining in the general fund at the end of any fiscal
year over and above the amount necessary for dis-
charging obligations for the fiscal year in full . Any
additional tax levied under this section shall come
within the present permissible maximum school tax
levy of school districts affected , and within such per-
missible maximum school tax levies as the General
Assembly may hereafter determine .
162.435 : The Board of Education shall cause to be
made annually an audit of the building fund by a cer-
tified public accountant and/or by an accountant ap-
proved by the State Department of Education .
162.440 : The Board of Education of any city of
the second class or of a county containing a city of
the second class may , by resolution , establish a fund
to be known as the " Insurance Fund " after written
approval of the plan to administer said fund has been
secured from the Superintendent of Public Instruc-
tion . The resolution shall state and fix the maximum
limit of the fund . The fund shall be maintained sep-
arate from the other funds and moneys of the board ,
and shall be used exclusively for replacing or repair-
ing any injury or destruction to any of the buildings
owned by the board or to their contents when caused
by fire , tornado , windstorm , cyclone , casualty , explo-
sion , riot or flood , but not when caused by wear and
tear or the natural processes of decadence or deter-
ioration .

CHAPTER 31 73
162.450 : The Board of Education of a city of the
second class , or of a county containing a city of the
second class , may raise the maximum limit of the in-
surance fund from time to time as it deems best . Until
the amount in the fund equals the maximum limit , the
Board of Education shall each year , from the rev-
enues under its control, set apart to the fund a sum
equal to from one-twentieth to one-tenth of the max-
imum limit of the sum . When any portion of the fund
is used , payments to restore the fund shall at once be
begun and be continued until the restoration is com-
plete . When the fund is , for any reason , below the
maximum limit , the interest derived from the invest-
ment thereof shall be accumulated and added to the
fund ; otherwise the interest may be transferred to
the general funds of the board.
162.460 : The insurance fund shall be kept on de-
posit with the treasurer of the Board of Education ,
unless by order of the board it is invested in United
States , State, County , or City bonds that are not pay-
able from assessments , and are registered , if prac-
ticable . If the bonds are coupon bonds they shall be
kept deposited in a safe deposit vault and be opened
only by the business manager or secretary of the
board in the presence of a member of the board
authorized to represent it . Every vote upon the use
or investment of any portion of the fund shall be by
call of the yeas and nays and the record shall show
how each member voted .
162.480 : When an injury occurs to any building
or its contents from fire , tornado , windstorm , cyclone ,
casualty , explosion , riot or flood , the business di-
rector or secretary of the Board of Education shall ,
within thirty days , thereafter , prepare and file with
the board a sworn written proof of loss , showing in
detail the items of injury , and in detail an estimate
of the extent of the financial loss and whether and to

74 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
what extent the same is covered by insurance , with
the names of the companies , the number of the policies
and names of the agents . Before the Board of Edu-
cation may appropriate any portion of the insurance
fund , a committee appointed by the board shall re-
port to the board in writing , answering in detail the
following questions and making such recommenda-
tions as they deem best :
1. What is the entire loss on the building ? When
and what caused it ?
2. What deduction should be made for wear and
tear and the natural processes of decadence or de-
terioration ?
3. What portion of the loss proposed to be made
good from this fund resulted from causes covered
by this fund ?
4. Will it be practicable to make the restoration
from the general fund and the proceeds of any insur-
ance policies without assistance from this fund ?
5. If assistance is needed from this fund , how
much ? How will the portion so used be returned to
the fund ?
6. Does or does not the committee recommend an
appropriation from this fund to aid in the restora-
tion proposed ? If so , how much ?
WHEREAS it is necessary that county school
budgets be prepared and submitted to the Fiscal
Courts on or before March 20 for the fiscal year be-
ginning July 1 ,
NOW , THEREFORE , an emergency is hereby de-
clared to exist , and this act shall take effect from and
after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 32 75
CHAPTER 32
(S. B. 97)
AN ACT amending Section 422.090 , Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky that :
Section 422.090 , Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
amended by repealing said Section and re -enacting it
so that as re-enacted , it shall read as follows , to -wit :
( 1 ) All discharge papers , given , executed or de-
livered to any person in the military or naval service
of the United States , which evidence is discharge
from the service of the United States and show the
unit or part of the department to which he was at-
tached and from which he was discharged may be
recorded in the office of the county clerk of the county
in which the person discharged is a resident . Upon
the presentation of such discharge papers the county
clerk shall record them , without charge therefor , in a
suitable book which he shall provide for that purpose .
( 2 ) A certified or attested copy of such recorded
discharge is admissible evidence in all proceedings in
which such discharge may come in question or in
which it might be used as legal evidence of any fact .
( 3) It shall be the duty of each county court clerk
to index alphabetically the name of each person whose
discharge papers are recorded as provided in this
Act and to keep such index as a permanent record
in such office . The index may be bound with the book
in which the discharge papers are recorded , or may
be a separate bound index , to be kept with the book in
which the discharge papers are recorded .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

76 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 33
(S. B. 98)
AN ACT amending and re -enacting Section 202.020 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , relating to lunacy inquests held by
county courts.
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 202.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
The Circuit Courts shall have exclusive jurisdiction
of all inquests concerning the condition of the mind
or mental faculty of persons , except that when no cir-
cuit court is in session in the county , inquests of in-
sane persons may be held by a circuit judge or by the
county court. But in no case shall an inquest upon an
idiot or a person convicted of crime and confined in
the penitentiary or reformatory , be held except in
circuit court , and if the inquest is upon a convict it
shall be held only at a regular term of the circuit
court . When the inquest is held by the county court a
fee of $3.00 shall be allowed the court for holding
said inquest and this fee shall be paid by the county
in which the commitment is made , upon a certified
copy of an order of allowance made by the court hold-
ing the inquest .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 34
(S. B. 103 )
AN ACT relating to Inheritance Taxes .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 2 ) of Section 140.030 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 2 ) The proceeds payable under any life insur-

CHAPTER 34 77
ance policy on the death of the assured ( other than a
United States Government Life Insurance policy or
National Service Life Insurance policy issued by or
through the Federal Government ) , whether payable
to a designated beneficiary or to the assured or his
estate , shall be taxable as a part of the legacy as the
distributable share of the beneficiary , except that the
proceeds of an insurance policy payable to a desig-
nated beneficiary other than the assured or his estate ,
not to exceed ten thousand dollars in the aggregate ,
shall be tax-free . The tax -free portion shall be divided
among the beneficiaries in proportion to the proceeds
payable to each of them . The proceeds payable under
any United States Government Life Insurance policy
or National Service Life Insurance policy issued by
or through the Federal Government , whether payable
to a designated beneficiary or to the assured or his
estate , shall be tax-free , and shall not be considered
in determining the value of any taxable transfer .
$ 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Sta-
tutes is created to be numbered KRS 140.015 and to
read as follows :
The payment of any gratuity pay , death compen-
sation or other award or benefit , by the Federal Gov-
ernment , to the surviving spouse or heirs of any
person by reason or arising out of service by such
person in the armed forces of the United States in
time of war shall not be considered a taxable trans-
fer within the meaning of this chapter , and the amount
paid shall not be considered in determining the value
of any taxable transfer .
§ 3. Whereas , it is essential that immediate relief
be provided for the surviving spouse and children of
the persons losing their lives in the service of our
country , an emergency is declared to exist and this
Act shall take effect upon its passage and approval by
the Governor .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

78 •ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 35
(S. B. 105 )
AN ACT repealing , amending , and re - enacting Section 23.230 ,
Kentucky Revised Statutes of 1942 so as to authorize the
appointment of a special judge where a vacancy occurs or
exists .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That section 23.230 Kentucky Revised Statutes of
1942 be and the same is hereby repealed , amended ,
and reenacted , so as to read as follows :
" When , from any cause , a judge of any circuit
court fails to attend , or being in attendance cannot
properly preside in an action pending in the court ,
or if a vacancy occurs or exists in the office of circuit
judge, or if either party files with the clerk of the
court his affidavit that the judge will not afford him
a fair and impartial trial , or will not impartially de-
cide an application for a change of venue , the parties ,
by agreement , may elect one of the attorneys of the
court to preside on the trial or hear the application ,
or hold the court for the occasion . If any party to the
action is a nonresident defendant , who has not en-
tered his appearance nor been summoned , or is an
infant defendant , the attorney appointed to defend
for such nonresident or the guardian ad litem for
such infant may agree with the other parties to the
action upon an attorney having all the qualifications
of a circuit judge to try the action . Any special judge
so selected shall have all the powers and be subject
to all the responsibilities of the regular judge of the
court."
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 36
( S. B. 111 )
AN ACT to amend Section 92.285 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , relating to Taxation of Insurance Companies .

CHAPTER 36 79
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 92.285 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The legislative body of each city which is au-
thorized to impose and collect license fees or taxes
upon insurance companies for the privilege of en-
gaging in the business of insurance shall file with the
Director of the Division of Insurance a copy of all
ordinances and amendments thereto which impose
any such license fee or tax .
(2 ) Any license fee or tax imposed by such a city
upon any fire insurance company shall be based upon
a percentage of the premiums received by the com-
pany within the preceding calendar year , for insur-
ance on property located within the corporate limits
of the city , less all premiums returned to policy-
holders . A fire insurance company entering a city
during a license year shall at the end thereof promptly
pay to such city the full additional tax due on such
percentage basis .
(3 ) Any license fee or tax imposed by such a city
upon a life insurance company may be based upon
premiums received , and if so based shall be applied
to premiums from new policies written within the
preceding calendar year upon the lives of persons
residing within the corporate limits of such city .
(4 ) Any license fee or tax imposed by such a city
upon any insurance company other than those speci-
fied in subsection ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) shall be based upon a
percentage of the premiums received by such com-
pany within the preceding calendar year on risks
located within the corporate limits of the city on those
classes of business which such company is authorized
to transact , less all premiums returned to policy-
holders , provided , however , that any license fee or
tax imposed upon premium receipts on a percentage

80 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
basis shall not include premiums received for in-
suring employers against liability for personal in-
juries to their employees , or death caused thereby ,
under the provisions of the Workmen's Compensa-
tion Act .
( 5 ) The license fees or taxes provided for by sub-
sections ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) and ( 4 ) of this section shall be due
at the beginning of the tax year . The city may collect
a fee of five dollars from any company whose fee or
tax on the percentage of premium basis is less than
five dollars .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 37
( S. B. 116 )
AN ACT to repeal and re - enact Sections 341.510 and 341.530 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes , relating to Unemployment
Compensation .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Section 341.510 , sub - section ( 1 )
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes be repealed and
re -enacted , so that when re -enacted the same shall
read as follows :
( 1)"WITHDRAWALS FROM TRUST FUND .
Money shall be requisitioned from the State's ac-
count in the Unemployment Trust Fund solely for the
payment of benefits and in accordance with regula-
tions prescribed by the Commission . The Commis-
sion , through the State Treasurer acting as its fiscal
agent , shall from time to time requisition from the
Unemployment Trust Fund such amounts , not ex-
ceeding the amounts standing to this State's accounts
therein as it considers necessary for the payment of
benefits for a reasonable future period . Provided ,
however , that out of contributions which become pay-

CHAPTER 37 81
able under this Chapter on and after April 1 , 1944 ,
the Commission may also in like manner requisition
from the Unemployment Trust Fund such funds as
may be necessary to defray the cost of administration
of this Chapter for a reasonable future period dur-
ing which the Social Security Board does not , for the
same period , make certification for payment of this
State of funds for the assistance of this State in the
administration of this Chapter as provided in Title
III of the Federal Social Security Act (49 Stat . 620 )
as amended ; any funds so requisitioned by the Com-
mission shall not exceed the total amount the Social
Security Board shall have withheld from certification
with respect to such State for the same period , nor
an aggregate total amount of $ 1,260,000.00 in any
event , and shall when received be deposited by the
Treasurer in the Unemployment Compensation Ad-
ministration Fund created by Section 341.240 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes and become a part of said
Fund , subject to the limitations of KRS 341.620 and
other provisions of the chapter with respect to ex-
penditure therefrom . During the period for which
funds are so requisitioned to defray the cost of ad-
ministering this chapter and deposited in the Unem-
ployment Administration Fund as above provided ,
no assessment shall be levied on employers under the
provisions of Section 341.590 Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes .
Section 2. That Section 341.530 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes be repealed and re -enacted , so that
when re -enacted the same shall read as follows :
"Employer reserve account . ( 1 ) The Commission
shall maintain within the fund a separate reserve
account for each subject employer , and shall , except
as provided in paragraph two hereof and KRS
341.560 , credit to such account the total amount of
all contributions paid by the employer on his own

82 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
1
behalf. The liability of each such account shall be
limited to the payment of benefits to eligible workers .
" (2) KRS 341.270 to the contrary notwithstand-
ing , on and after April 1 , 1944 , no employer's con-
tribution rate shall be less than three tenths of one
per cent and the first three tenths of one per cent shall
be withheld from credit to an employer's reserve
account until the end of the tax paying period in
which the total so withheld from all employers in the
aggregate reaches $1,260,000.00 , whereupon the mini-
mum contribution rate fixed by this paragraph shall
become inoperative . Any balance so withheld in
excess of $1,260,000.00 shall be credited to the pooled
account as defined in KRS 341.550 .
" ( 3 ) The benefits paid to an eligible worker for a
week of unemployment shall be charged propor-
tionately against the accounts of those of his subject
employers from whom he has base year or base period
wages . The proportion so used shall be that ratio
which is obtained by dividing the base year or base
period wages of a worker from such employer by the
total base year or base period wages of such worker ,
adjusted to the nearest multiple or one per cent . For
the purpose of this sub - section a fractional part of
a cent shall be disregarded unless it amounts to one-
half cent or more , in which case it shall be increased
to one cent .
" ( 4 ) Each subject employer's reserve account
shall, unless terminated or exhausted as of the com-
putation date ( as defined in sub - section ( 5 ) of KRS
341.270 ) be charged with all benefits paid to eligible
workers which are chargeable to such reserve account
under sub -section (3 ) of this section . A subject em-
ployer's reserve account shall be deemed to be termi-
nated if he has ceased to be subject to this chapter ,
and his account has been closed and any balance
remaining therein has been credited to the fund's

CHAPTER 38 83
pooled account or to a successor's account as pro-
vided in KRS 341.540 .
‘ ( 5 ) Any subject employer may at any time make
voluntary payments , additional to the contributions
required under KRS 341.260 and 341.270 to his re-
serve account , according to the general rules estab-
lished by the Commission . "
Section 3. Declaration of emergency . Whereas
the taking effect of the provisions of this Act is neces-
sary prior to April 1 , 1944 , in order to have available
administrative money for the Unemployment Com-
pensation Commission by July 1 , 1944 , an emergency
is hereby declared to exist and this Act shall become
law upon its enactment by the General Assembly and
approval by the Governor .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 38
(S. B. 118 )
AN ACT relating to veterans of the World Wars and amending
Section 164.480 and repealing Section 164.510 , Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 164.480 Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended and re -enacted so that said sec-
tion as amended and re -enacted shall read as
follows :-
"Every person who was engaged in any branch of
the military or naval service of the United States ,
prescribed and classified by the United States War
Department as a part of such service , during the wars
that were declared on April 6, 1917 , and any wars
declared thereafter , respectively , and who at the
time of his enlistment was a resident of Kentucky ,
is entitled to a free scholarship in any State higher

84 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
educational institution for white or colored persons
that he chooses for a period required for the comple-
tion of the course of study selected by him . The
scholarship includes free tuition , matriculation and
other fees , room rent, fuel and lights , and the ad-
vantages and privileges of the institution , but does
not include free board . ”
Provided , however , that if the Federal Government
provides for the education of veterans of any of the
wars herein referred to , then this Act shall not apply
to such veterans .
Section 2. Section 164.510 Kentucky Revised
Statutes be and the same is hereby repealed .
WHEREAS , many persons are now being dis-
charged from the various branches of the armed
forces and desire to resume their education but are
financially unable to defray the expenses incident
thereto , an emergency is hereby declared to exist and
this Act shall become immediately effective after its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 39
(S. B. 119 )
AN ACT to amend Sections 122.080 and 122.090 , Ky. Rev. Stat .,
relating to contests of elections .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Sections 122.080 and 122.090 , Ky . Rev. Stats . , are
hereby amended to read as follows :
122.080 (1596a -12) . Procedure in contest of election
under KRS 122.070 ; trial ; judgment . A contest in-
stituted under KRS 122.070 shall proceed as equity
actions . Upon return of the summons properly exe-
cuted to the office of the Circuit Clerk , he shall imme

CHAPTER 39 85
diately docket the case and notify the presiding judge
of the court that the contest has been filed . The
judge shall proceed to a trial of the cause without
delay . In courts having more than one judge , the
judge who shall try the case shall be determined by
lot . The court may be convened at any time, irre-
spective of the regular term , for the purpose of trying
the case or any proceeding therein and shall complete
the same as soon as practicable . The action shall have
precedence over all other cases.
The evidence in chief for the contestant shall be
completed within 30 days after service of summons ;
the evidence for the contestee shall be completed
within 25 days after filing of answer , and evidence
for contestant in rebuttal shall be completed within
7 days after the contestee has concluded ; provided
that for cause the court may grant a reasonable ex-
tension of time to either party .
All ballots , stub books and other papers concern-
ing which there is any ground for contest may be
removed to the court in which the action is pending .
If it appears from an inspection of the whole record
that there has been such fraud , intimidation , bribery
or violence in the conduct of the election that neither
contestant nor contestee can be judged to have been
fairly elected , the circuit court , or the Court of Ap-
peals , on appeal , may adjudge that there has been no
election . In that event the office shall be deemed
vacant , with the same legal effect as if the person
elected had refused to qualify . If one of the parties
is adjudged by the court to be elected to the office ,
he shall , on production of a copy of the final judg-
ment , be permitted to qualify or be commissioned .
122.090 ( 1596a - 12 ) Appeal to Court of Appeals
from judgment in contest under KRS 122.070 ; costs .
Either party may appeal to the Court of Appeals
from a judgment of the circuit court in a contest in-

86 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
stituted under KRS 122.070 , by giving bond before
the clerk of the circuit court , with good surety , con-
ditioned for the payment of all costs and damages
the other party may sustain by reason of the appeal ,
and by filing the record in the office of the Clerk of the
Court of Appeals within thirty days after final judg-
ment in the circuit court . The case shall be heard
and determined in the Court of Appeals as speedily
as possible , and shall have precedence over all other
cases .
The Court in its discretion may issue its mandate
forthwith after rendering its decision , but it shall be
without prejudice to the right of the losing party to
file a petition for rehearing or the power of the Court
to recall the mandate should it be sustained . Pro-
ceedings in the trial court upon a return of the case
shall be as prescribed by Section 761 of the Civil Code
of Practice except that if the judgment be reversed
and the case remanded for trial or further action ,
the proceedings shall continue in like manner as when
originally filed beginning ten days after the filing of
the mandate with notice to the adverse party or his
attorney .
The unsuccessful party shall pay all costs in both
courts .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 40
(S. B. 121 )
AN ACT providing for the establishment of a juvenile court in
all cities of the third class which are not county seats and
which are larger than the county seat of the county in which
such third class city is located , except in counties wherein
there are circuit courts of continuous session and which are
more than ten miles from the county seat when the distance
is measured along the usually traveled road between the third
class city and the county seat of the county in which such

CHAPTER 40 87
third class city is located ; providing for the establishment ,
construction and maintenance of detention homes in such third
class cities by such cities ; providing for a judge of the juvenile
court in such third class cities ; providing for the employment
of probation officers in such cities ; and providing for the levy-
ing annually by such third class cities of a sufficient tax for
the establishment , construction and maintenance of such de-
tention home , which detention home shall not be connected
with a jail , courthouse or city hall .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In any county having therein or that may
hereafter have therein a city of the third class or a
portion of a city of the third class not the county seat
and being more than ten miles from the county seat ,
the distance to be measured from the courthouse of
such county seat to the city limits of such third class
city or portion of such third class city , except in
counties wherein there are circuit courts of continu-
ous session along the most convenient and usually
traveled highway , there is hereby established a
Juvenile Court and the governing authority of such
third class city may but shall not be compelled to
establish , construct and maintain a detention home
in such city and if such home is established shall levy
annually a sufficient tax to establish , construct and
maintain such detention home .
§ 2. The Judge of the Police Court of said city
shall act and serve as Judge of this Juvenile Court ,
and such Court shall have exclusive jurisdiction with-
in the limits of the city of all dependent , neglected
and delinquent children as defined in Section 199.010
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes . The City Prose-
cuting Attorney shall be Clerk of the Juvenile session
of the Police Court , and he shall attend all sessions
of the court and shall keep a complete and indexed
record of its proceedings . For his services as such
Clerk the governing authority of the city may make

88 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
him a reasonable annual allowance to be paid out of
the city treasury . The Judge of the Police Court may
appoint a probation officer if he deems it necessary
or advisable , who shall serve at the pleasure of the
Judge and who may be a volunteer probation officer ,
or the city may pay him a reasonable compensation
for his services not to exceed Fifteen Hundred
($ 1500.00 ) Dollars annually payable in equal monthly
installments out of the City treasury .
§ 3. The provisions of chapter 199 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , dealing with dependent ,
neglected and delinquent children and Juvenile
criminals , shall apply in full force to all proceedings
had in the Juvenile Courts established under this
Act, except as inconsistent with the foregoing pro-
visions of this Act, and they are adopted as part of
this Act , and if in the enforcement of this law the
Juvenile Judge should commit some delinquent child
to the Reform School the expense of maintaining the
child shall be paid by the County in the manner now
provided by law under sections 198.080 and 198.090
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
$ 4. The tax levying authority of such third class
city shall make an annual levy of taxes in the manner
now provided by law , and within the constitutional
limits of taxation provided in such city , to defray
such expenses as are incident to the establishment ,
operation and maintenance of this Juvenile Court
and detention home , if such detention home is estab-
lished by the city .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 41
(S. B. 122 )
AN ACT relating to drainage districts , amending KRS 267.390 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 41 89
Section 267.390 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1) Except as otherwise provided in this chapter ,
all improvements made under this chapter , as well-
as all public ditches or drains established or con-
structed under preexisting laws , shall be under the
control and supervision of the board having jurisdic-
tion of the proceedings for the establishment and con-
struction thereof . The board shall keep all such im-
provements open , free from obstruction and in good
repair . For that purpose the board may employ men
and teams , and lease , rent , hire or purchase ma-
chinery .
( 2 ) For the purpose of providing funds to pay
the compensation of the employes of the board, out-
side of that portion to be paid out of the original as-
sessment on any district , and for the purpose of pay-
ing the expense of repairing , keeping open and look-
ing after the various improvements under the charge
of the board , the land in each district established un-
der this chapter , as well as the land assessed for each
public ditch or system of drainage established under
preexisting laws , shall be assessed in proportion to
the original assessment made against the land for
the original improvement , except that where a district
has been established diverting the course of water,
the board may , in order to relieve land taxed and from
which water has been diverted , use funds of the dis-
trict in maintaining the original watercourse .
(3) ( a) Except as provided in paragraph ( b ) of
this subsection , no assessment in any one year for the
purposes prescribed in subsection (2 ) of this section
shall exceed ten percent ofthe original assessment
against the land , and no more than one assessment
shall be made in any one year .
(b) In counties having seventy -five or more sep-
arate drainage districts , when a petition signed by a

90 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
majority of the land owners in any drainage district
is filed with and approved by the drainage commis-
sioner , the latter may levy a maintenance assessment
not exceeding 30 % of the original assessment against
the land . The number of assessments which may be
levied shall be limited as follows : Where the assess-
ment is 10 % but not over 15 % , no more than one
assessment may be levied during any eighteen month
period ; where the assessment is 16 % but not over
20% no more than one assessment may be levied dur-
ing any two year period ; where the assessment is 21 %
but not over 25 % no more than one assessment may be
levied during any two and one -half year period ; and
where the assessment is 30% but not less than 26%
no more than one assessment may be levied during
any three year period.
(c) No assessment shall be made until the board
has prepared an assessment roll , in duplicate , stating
the name of the owners of the land to be affected , and
the amount proposed to be assessed against each tract
of land . Both the assessment roll and the duplicate
shall be signed by the president and secretary of the
board . The original shall be kept on file in the office
of the board and the duplicate shall be filed with the
collector of the assessment . These additional assess-
ments shall have the same effect as original assess-
ments and be collected in the same manner . They
shall be kept separate and deposited with the treas-
urer , who shall keep the accounts for each district
separate . Each warrant drawn shall specify out of
which account it shall be paid .
(4) The board may compromise and settle , upon
such terms and conditions as it deems proper , any
maintenance assessment made , levied or assessed
prior to February 17 , 1936 , and may do everything
necessary to release and discharge any claim , demand
or lien upon land established , made or claimed prior

CHAPTER 43 91
to February 17 , 1936 , under or by reason of any such
maintenance assessment .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 42
(S. B. 123 )
AN ACT providing for the maintenance of county and municipal
airports by the Department of Highways .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
The Department of Highways shall furnish men ,
machinery , materials and equipment for the main-
tenance of the runways only of any and all airports
heretofore or hereafter established by cities , counties ,
or cities and counties jointly , that have paved run-
ways and are approved by the Civil Aeronautics Ad-
ministration or its legal successor and by the Depart-
ment of Highways of the State of Kentucky . The
maintenance work shall be performed by the Depart-
ment of Highways when and as requested by the gov-
erning boards of the respective airports , as and when
required to maintain same in serviceable condition
according to Civil Aeronautics standards - except
when such airports are under lease to the Federal
Government or to a private corporation .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 43
(S. B. 124 )
AN ACT to repeal Section 28.450 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , and to enact in lieu thereof new provisions relating
to compensation or reporters for Circuit Courts of continuous
session in counties having a population of less than 150,000 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

26 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY92
4444444
I. That Section 28.450 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes be and the same is hereby amended so that
said section as so amended shall read as follows , to-
wit :
" Reporters for Circuit Courts of continuous ses-
sion in counties having a population of less than 150 , -
000 shall receive for their services in taking steno-
graphic notes a salary of not less than $1800.00 nor
more than $2400.00 per annum , to be fixed by the fiscal
court and paid in monthly installments by the County.
They shall receive for making transcripts for a per-
son other than the county or State $ .15 (fifteen cents )
per one hundred words ; the pages shall average thir-
ty lines each . In civil actions involving less than
$500.00 they shall receive $5.00 per day for taking
stenographic notes of said actions and $ .15 (fifteen
cents ) per hundred words for the transcript thereof ,
which shall be paid forthwith by the party in whose
behalf the report or transcript is ordered , and shall
be taxed as part of the costs of the action . In crimi-
nal cases the fee for making transcripts shall be paid
by the county in which the Court is held when the
transcript is made upon the motion of the Common-
wealth . If the transcript is made upon motion of the
defendant in a criminal case , he shall pay the fee
therefor , unless the court directs the reporter to file
a transcript upon the motion of a party suing in forma
pauperis , in which event the fee shall be paid by the
county .
For duplicate copies of transcripts , reporters for
circuit courts of continuous session in counties having
a population of less than 150,000 shall be allowed one-
third the amount they are allowed for the original ,
to be paid for in the same manner as the original .
II. All acts and parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
""
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

(S. B. 133 )

monwealth of Kentucky :

is amended to read as follows :

CHAPTER 48
(S. B. 143 )
AN ACT relating to cooperative marketing associations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 272.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The articles of incorporation of each associa-
tion shall state :
(a ) The name of the association ;
(b ) The purpose for which it is formed ;
(c ) The place where its principal business will be
transacted ;
(d ) The term for which it is to exist , not exceeding
fifty years ;
( e) The number of directors , their term of office ,
and the names and addresses of the incorporating
directors ;
(f) If organized without capital stock , whether the
property rights and interest of each member are equal
or unequal ; and if unequal , the general rules appli-
cable to all members by which the property rights and
interests of each member shall be determined ; and
provision for the admission of new members who may
all share in the property of the association with the
• old members ; and
(g ) If organized with capital stock , the maximum
amount of stock and the number of shares into which
it is divided and the par value ; and if the stock is
divided into preferred and common stock , a statement
of the number of shares of stock to which preference

96 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
is granted and the number to which no preference is
granted and the nature and definite extent of the
preference and privileges granted to each . No spe-
cific amount of the capital stock authorized shall be
required to be subscribed before the corporation shall
be permitted to transact business with others than
its stockholders ; the board of directors of the corpor-
ation may determine the amount of capital stock to
be issued as the business of the corporation may jus-
tify or demand , from time to time , within the amount
of the total authorization .
( 2) The articles shall be subscribed by the incor-
porators and acknowledged by one of them before an
officer authorized by this state to take acknowledge-
ments of deeds . A certified copy of the articles of
incorporation shall , in addition to being filed with the
Secretary of State , be filed with the Dean of the Col-
lege of Agriculture of the University of Kentucky .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944.
CHAPTER 49
( S. B. 144 )
AN ACT to amend Section 67.180 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , relating to the purchase of liability and compensation
insurance by counties , by extending its provisions to counties
containing cities of the fourth , fifth and sixth classes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
-Sec . 1. Section 67.180 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The fiscal court of each county containing a
city of the second , fourth , fifth or sixth class may , in
its discretion , for the protection of the public and its
employees , appropriate county funds to purchase
policies of insurance of all kinds deemed advisable ,
covering vehicles operated by the county , and com-

CHAPTER 50 97
pensation insurance covering employees of the coun-
ty receiving injuries arising out of and in the course
of employment .

tained against the county only for the purpose of ob-
taining a judgment which when final shall measure
the liability of the insurance carrier to the injured

and this Act shall take effect from and after its pas-
sage and approval by the Governor .

CHAPTER 52
(S. B. 147 )
AN ACT to amend and re-enact Sections 97.470, 97.430 , Kentucky
Revised Statutes , 1942 Edition , pertaining to the Secretary
and Treasurer of Boards of Park Commissioners in Cities of
the Second Class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 97.430 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes 1942 Edition , be and the same is hereby amended
and re -enacted so that when so amended and re -enact-
ed said section shall read as follows :
" 97.430 (3235b -6 ) Compensation of Secretary and
Treasurer ; members not to receive pay or hold other
office .

100 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The secretary and treasurer of each board may re-
ceive an annual salary of not over five hundred dol-
lars , to be fixed by the board and paid out of the fund
appropriated for park purposes . The members of
the boards shall receive no compensation for their
services as members , but a member who is elected
secretary and treasurer of his board may receive the
compensation authorized by this section for his serv-
ices as such . No member of either board may hold
any office , appointment or employment under the city
or any department or institution of the city ."
Section 97.470 Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1942
Edition , is amended to read as follows :
“ 97.470 ( 3235b -12 ; 3235b -13 ) Appropriations for
park purposes ; separate funds for white and colored
park boards.
(1 ) The city may appropriate a sufficient sum to
provide for the purchase , care and improvement of
public parks , and to meet the expenses of the boards
of park commissioners , and may maintain public
parks out of the city funds to the same extent as it
is authorized to maintain other public property .
( 2 ) Two funds in whatever amounts the city legis-
lative body deems sufficient for the care and mainten-
ance of public parks may be annually appropriated
for that purpose . The funds may be directed by the
legislative body to be held by the treasurer of the
city in separate and distinct accounts , one for the use
of the white park board and the other for the use of
the colored park board , or the legislative body may
direct that the funds be held by the secretary and
treasurer of each park board in a separate account ,
in which event a secretary and treasurer shall be ap-
pointed by each of such park boards . The city treas-
urer or the secretary and treasurer of the park board
shall pay out money from such accounts only upon
an order issued by the board of park commissioners

CHAPTER 53 101
for which the funds have been appropriated , which
order shall be signed by the secretary and treasurer
and countersigned by its president , and after a bill for
the withdrawal has been approved by the board at
session assembled . In no event shall the city treas-
urer or the secretary and treasurer of a park board
honor in any one year orders for a greater sum than
the amount appropriated for park purposes for that
board.
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 53
(S. B. 150 )
AN ACT relating to escheated , abandoned , and unclaimed prop-
erty .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 1 ) of section 393.110 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) It shall be the duty of all state and National
banks , trust companies , or other persons , and courts
of this Commonwealth or the agents thereof , whether
holding estates or property as bailee , depository ,
debtor , trustee , executor , liquidator , administrator ,
distributor , receiver or in any other capacity coming
within the purview of KRS 393.060 to 393.100 , to re-
port annually to the department as of July 1 , all prop-
erty held by them declared by this chapter to be pre-
sumed abandoned . The report shall be filed in the
office of the department on or before September 1 of
each year for the preceding July 1 , and shall give the
name of the owner , his last known address , the amount
and kind of property , and such other information as
the department may require for the administration
of this chapter . The report shall be made in dupli-

102 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cate ; the original shall be retained by the department ,
and the copy shall be mailed to the sheriff of the coun-
ty where the property is located or held . It shall be
the duty of the sheriff to post said copy on the court-
house door or the court house bulletin board , and also
to publish said copy in at least two issues of a news-
paper published in the county seat or , if no newspaper
is published in the county seat , then in a newspaper of
general circulation in the county . The list shall be
published within thirty days of its receipt by the sher-
iff . The cost of the publication shall be paid by the
state . The sheriff shall immediately certify in writ-
ing to the department the dates when the list was post-
ed and published . The list shall be posted and pub-
lished as required on or before October 1 of the year
when it is made , and such posting and publishing shall
be constructive notice to all interested parties and
shall be in addition to any other notice provided by
statute or existing as a matter of law .
§ 2. Section 287.410 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is repealed .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 55
(S. B. 155 )
AN ACT empowering and directing the Treasurer of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky to invest funds in his hands belonging
to boards , institutions , and agencies not supported in whole
or in part by taxation .
WHEREAS : There are several boards , institutions and agencies
of the Commonwealth supported by private funds , contribu-
tions and fees collected in the furtherance of their purposes ,
and not supported in whole or in part by money raised by
taxation , which now or may have to their credits in the
Treasury of the Commonwealth surplus funds , arising in the
manner stated ; and
WHEREAS : It is deemed advisable , as well as patriotic , to in-
vest such surplus funds in War or Victory bonds or other
bonds of the United States Government ;
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
( 1 ) The Treasurer of Kentucky is hereby em-
powered to invest surplus funds of any board , insti-
tution or agency of the Commonwealth which is not in
whole or in part supported or maintained by means
of monies raised by taxation , and whose operating
funds are accumulated solely by means of contribu-

104 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tions , gifts , private funds or fees collected in the oper-
ation of its functions , in War or Victory bonds , or
other bonds of the United States of America .
( 2) Such surplus funds shall not be so invested un-
less and until the authorized head , or the governing
body of such board , institution or agency shall give
direction in writing to the Department of Finance
that such investment shall be made , and when so given
the Department of Finance shall by its warrant direct
that such investment be made on behalf of the particu-
lar board, agency or institution .
(3 ) The Treasurer shall be custodian of such
bonds as are purchased , to be held under the same
duties , restrictions and liabilities as are now imposed
by law as to public funds . Provided that such bonds
are to be earmarked for the use and benefit of the par-
ticular board, agency or institution , and may be sold
or redeemed by the Treasurer , upon the written re-
quest of the head or governing body of such board ,
institution , or agency entitled to the funds , the pro-
ceeds to be administered , cared for and withdrawn as
provided by Section 41.290 Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , or as may hereafter be provided by law .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944.

CHAPTER 60
(H. B. 8 )
AN ACT relating to the operation of school buses and other
motor vehicles.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 186.600 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
of 1942 is amended to read as follows :
No person under the age of eighteen shall drive a
motor vehicle while it is in use as a school bus for the
transportation of pupils to and from school . All
drivers and all operators of school buses shall meet
the requirements of law and the rules and regulations
of the State Board of Education for school bus
drivers and operators . Any violation of the rules and
regulations of the State Board of Education for school
bus drivers and operators shall be subject to the
penalty prescribed for violation of this section . No
person under the age of twenty -one shall drive any
other motor vehicle while it is in use as a public pas-
senger carrying vehicle .

The provisions of this bill shall apply for the dura-

CHAPTER 61
(H. B. 9 )
AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Fund for the
purpose of supplementing the salaries of teachers in common
schools :
WHEREAS , due to the present emergency there is a great short-
age of teachers in the Commonwealth of Kentucky because
of the low salaries and the attraction of more lucrative paying
positions in war and private industries . Now , therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
There is hereby appropriated out of any sums in
the General Fund not already appropriated the sum
of $3,000,000 for the purpose of supplementing the
salaries of teachers employed in the public elemen-
tary and secondary schools of Kentucky for the school
year beginning July 1 , 1943 , and ending June 30 , 1944 .
The funds so appropriated shall be distributed to each
school district by the Superintendent of Public In-
struction as a supplement to the common school fund
appropriated in Item 18 ( e ) of Part One -A of Chapter
1 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1942 for the
1943-44 school year . They shall be distributed in
accordance with the law .
In making the distribution the Superintendent of
Public Instruction shall use the official census figures
for the state and for each school district used in the
distribution of the common school fund for the
1943-44 school year .
The funds received by each board of education from
this appropriation shall be so applied that each
teacher of the district shall receive the same amount

CHAPTER 62 111
of increase on the monthly basal salary schedule for
the school year 1943-44 .
The appropriation provided herein shall be exempt
from all provisions of law relating to teacher retire-
ment , Section 161.220 to and including Section
161.710 , Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1942 edition .
" Teacher , " as defined in this Act , shall mean class-
room teachers , princpals , supervisors , or other cer-
tificated employees included in the official salary
schedule as adopted by the school district and ap-
proved by the State Board of Education for the
1943-44 school year.
WHEREAS , the funds appropriated by this Act
supplement the salaries of teachers for the school year
1943-44 , and
WHEREAS , if the effective date of the Act is de-
layed many of the school terms will end before the
funds can be distributed as required by the Act .
NOW, THEREFORE , an emergency is hereby de-
clared to exist and this Act shall become effective upon
its approval by the Governor .
Passed January 31 , enrolled February 1, approved February 1,
1944 .

CHAPTER 63
(H. B. 20 )
AN ACT requiring all employers to state specifically the amount
and the purpose of any deductions from employes ' salaries
or wages .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. All employers who employ ten or more and
pay their employes by check or otherwise , making de-
ductions from the salaries and wages due said em-
ployes , shall state specifically the amount for which
the deductions are made , and each such employer at
the time of payment of salary or wage to each employe
shall furnish the employe a statement giving the
amount of each deduction and the general purpose for
which the deduction is made .
§ 2. Any person who fails to comply with Section
1 of this Act shall be fined not less than ten dollars , nor
more than one hundred dollars , for each offense and
each day that such failure continues shall be deemed a
separate offense .
Passed February 17 , enrolled February 18 , approved February
24 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 64
(H. B. 24 )
AN ACT amending and re -enacting KRS 179.420 relating to the
plans of the Department of Highways and the fiscal courts
for the use of funds allocated for the county roads and bridges .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 179.420 of KRS is amended and re -enacted
to read as follows :
( 1 ) The funds apportioned the several counties
under the provisions of KRS 179.410 shall be ex-

CHAPTER 64 113
pended for the construction and maintenance of
county roads by the Department of Highways in ac-
cordance with previously agreed plans prepared
jointly by the fiscal courts of the several counties and
the Department of Highways , or prepared by one or
the other and jointly approved . The plans shall take
into consideration the mileage of county roads in the
particular county , the condition and needs of such
roads and the manner in which the funds shall be
expended for the fiscal year to which the agreement
applies , giving priority to the maintenance of county
roads and bridges heretofore constructed . The plan
for each county shall be formulated and agreed to an-
nually, and , under any circumstances , even though a
plan has not been formulated and agreed upon, the
Department of Highways is empowered to use such of
the funds allotted for the county roads and bridges as
may be required for the proper maintenance of such
roads and bridges , which , in the absence of an agreed
plan , shall not exceed 25% of the allotment to the
county for the fiscal year , and such maintenance cost
shall be charged to the allotment of the particular
county affected .
( 2 ) If 30 days after the beginning of the fiscal year
of the Department of Highways in which the funds
were apportioned , the department and the fiscal court
of any county have failed to agree on a plan , the funds
so apportioned to such county , less the funds , if any ,
expended or obligated for the maintenance of there-
tofore constructed county roads and bridges shall be
expended by the Department of Highways for the con-
struction or reconstruction of the county roads and
bridges according to the priority schedule designated
by the fiscal court , but the amount of money to be ex-
pended on each of the roads or bridges so designated
and the type of roads or bridges and the material used

114 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall be in accordance with the specifications of the
Department of Highways .
(3) Any funds apportioned to any county , less the
funds , if any , expended or obligated for the construc-
tion , re-construction or maintenance of county roads
or bridges in any county shall be held for the use and
benefit of said county , and shall not be deducted from
the apportionment to that county in any subsequent
year , and shall not be expended in any other county or
used for any other purpose .
Passed March 6 , enrolled March 7 , approved March 8 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 65
(H. B. 28 )
AN ACT relating to the State Fair .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 247.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The State Fair Board
(a ) Shall have the custody and control of the State
Fair Grounds, including the buildings and equipment ,

( b ) May hold an annual fair on the grounds , or

hibition of agricultural , mechanical , horticultural ,
dairy , forestry , poultry , livestock , mineral and all
other industrial interests of the State ; except that if
during the National Emergency the State Fair Board
is unable to find a suitable location within a county
containing a city of the first class , the Fair during
such National Emergency may be held at any other
place selected by the State Fair Board , and

CHAPTER 66 115
(c) Prepare premium lists and establish rules of

(2 ) It may take and hold property by deed , gift ,

(3 ) Wherever the term " State Fair Grounds " is

Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 66
(H. B. 30 )
AN ACT repealing , amending , and re -enacting Section 68.090 ,
Kentucky Revised Statutes , so as to provide for an exception
to the limitation on ad valorem taxes for the financing of
tubercular institutions in legally established tubercular dis-
tricts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 68.090 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes be repealed , amended , and re -enacted so that
when so repealed , amended , and re -enacted , it shall
read as follows , to - wit :
"For county purposes the fiscal court of each
county may levy a poll tax on each male resident of the
county who is of the age of twenty -one years or over
and is not exempt under KRS 142.020 , and shall levy
an ad valorem tax on all property subject to county
taxation . This section does not authorize the fiscal
court to levy a tax to pay any railroad bonded in-
debtedness or any interest thereon . The poll tax
shall not exceed one dollar and fifty cents on each
person taxed , and the ad valorem tax shall not exceed
fifty cents on each one hundred dollars ' worth of
property taxed ; except that , if an additional tax is
required to pay the principal or interest on indebted-

116 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ness of the county or a taxing district therein incurred
prior to the adoption of the Constitution , the fiscal
court may levy such additional tax as is authorized by
law in force prior to the adoption of the Constitution .
"It is further provided that in any county which
has under the law now in force established itself as a
tubercular district the fiscal court shall levy a tax
sufficient to maintain a tubercular institution and to
provide for the salaries of the medical staff thereof
and any other necessary expenses incident thereto
taking into consideration any federal , state , or pri-
vate financial assistance which may be given to such
institution or which by existing law or that hereafter
enacted such institution may be entitled to receive .
Such levy shall be an ad valorem tax on all property
subject to county taxation and shall not exceed ten
cents on each one hundred dollars ' worth of property
so taxed and such ten cents shall be an additional tax
to the fifty cents ad valorem tax limitation herein-
before provided for . "
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
