CHAPTER 68
(H. B. 33 )
AN ACT relating to the licensing of operators of motor vehicles.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 186.460 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Except as provided in subsection (3 ) of this
section , every applicant for an instruction permit or
an operator's license shall apply in person to the cir-
cuit clerk of the county in which he resides and make
application upon a form furnished by the department .
The applicant shall make oath to the circuit clerk as
to the truthfulness of the statements contained in the
form .
( 2 ) Every application shall contain the full
name , date of birth , sex, race , and resident address of
the applicant and briefly describe him . It shall also
state whether the applicant has previously been li-
censed as an operator and if so , when and by what
state or county , whether any license has ever been
cancelled , suspended , revoked , or refused , and if so ,
the date of and reason for the cancellation , suspen-
sion , denial, revocation or refusal , and such other
information as may be required .
T

118 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(3) Any licensed operator whose license has not
been suspended or revoked may procure an annual re-
newal of his license either by applying in person at the
office of the circuit clerk or by sending to the circuit
clerk by another person or by mail his license certifi-
cate for the previous year, together with the license
fee and a three cent postage stamp . Upon receipt of
any application for renewal by mail , accompanied by
the fee and stamp , the circuit clerk shall issue a new
license to such person and forward it to him by mail .
(4 ) No person whose license has been revoked
shall apply for or receive a new license until the ex-
piration of one year from the date of revocation of the
former license , nor shall any person whose license is
suspended apply for or receive any new license until
the expiration of the period for which the license was
suspended irrespective of whether or not the license
was suspended for a period longer than that for which
the license was issued .
§ 2. Section 186.500 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Every license certificate shall bear a distinguishing
number , the name , age , resident address and a brief
description of the licensee , the signature of the li-
censee, which shall be affixed in the presence of the
circuit clerk or his deputy , except as provided in sub-
section ( 3 ) of KRS 186.460 , and such other informa-
tion as the department may reasonably require .
Passed February 24 , enrolled February 25 , approved March 8 ,
1944 .
CHAPTER 69
(H. B. 34)
AN ACT relating to cemeteries for cities of the fifth and sixth
classes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 69 119
1. Cities of the fifth and sixth class may establish ,
purchase or otherwise acquire , accept , condemn , main-
tain and operate cemeteries to bury the dead , and
other necessary appurtenances including personal
property necessary for the successful conducting of
such cemeteries , within or without the corporate
limits of such cities under the provisions of this Act,
except that no cemetery already in existence may be
condemned .
2. Any city establishing any cemetery authorized
by this Act and desiring to maintain and operate same
under the provisions hereof shall by ordinance pro-
vide for the appointment of a board of commissioners
to operate , manage and control such project , said
board of commissioners to be known by whatever title
the city may designate in the ordinance under which it
is created . The board shall consist of five persons , to
be appointed by the Mayor to serve for terms of three
years and until their successors are appointed , except
that the members first appointed shall be so appointed
that the term of one member shall expire annually
thereafter . Vacancies shall be filled in the same man-
ner as original appointments and for the unexpired
term . Members shall serve without pay . The mem-
bers of the board shall be citizens and legal voters of
the city , and if any member during the term of his
office shall become a candidate for , or be elected to ,
any public office , he shall automatically vacate his
membership from the board and another person shall
be appointed in his place ; Provided , however , that
this provision shall not prevent a member of the board
from serving as a member of any other appointive
office or commission of the city , county , state or fed-
eral government . The board shall provide rules , reg-
ulations and by -laws for the management of the
cemetery , and out of the revenue derived from the
cemetery , it shall pay all operating expenses , provide

120 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for such repairs , additions and improvements as may
be necessary to be made thereto and provide a fund
for payment of any incidental or emergency expense
which may arise and set up a fund to provide for the
payment of any debts created in connection with the
establishment and maintenance of the cemetery .
Cemetery revenue shall be used only for the upkeep ,
improvement or extension of the cemetery , and the
retirement of cemetery bonds . The board may fix the
charges for lots sold in the cemetery to individuals
and for maintenance , care , upkeep , and use of such
lots as it deems necessary and advisable .
4. All properties , both real and personal , which
the city may acquire for the establishment and main-
tenance of a cemetery , shall be exempt from taxation
to the same extent as other public property used for
public purposes . The proceeds of all income and sales
of cemetery lots shall also be exempt from taxation .
5. Any city which establishes a cemetery as pro-
vided by this Act may acquire by gift , condemnation ,
devise , bequest , grant , or purchase solely from the
funds provided under the authority of this Act , such
lands , structures , rights -of -way , franchises , ease-
ments or other interests in land , or personal property ,
necessary for the establishment and maintenance of a
cemetery , and upon such terms , prices , and considera-
tions as may be considered by the city to be reasonable
and can be agreed upon between the city and the owner
thereof . Title to all property acquired for such pur-
poses shall be taken in the name of the city . When-
ever property shall be needed by any city of the fifth
or sixth class for cemetery purposes , either within or
beyond the boundary of such city , in the county in
which such city is located , same may be condemned by
the city in accordance with the provisions of Section
87.090 , Kentucky Revised Statutes , except that no
existing cemetery may be condemned .

CHAPTER 69 121
6. For the purpose of defraying the cost of pur-
chasing , maintaining , establishing , erecting , or acquir-
ing any cemetery and necessary appurtenances
thereto , including personal property , under the provi-
sions of this Act , any city may borrow money and
issue negotiable bonds which shall be payable solely
out of the revenue from the cemetery . All bonds under
the provisions of this Act shall be issued to bear in-
terest at a rate not exceeding 6 % per annum , payable
semi-annually , and shall be executed in such a man-
ner and be payable at such times , not exceeding thirty
years from the date of issuance , and at such place as
the city legislative body shall determine . Any city
acquiring a cemetery may at any time provide for the
addition to and improvement of the cemetery by an
additional issue of bonds , subject to the procedure re-
quired for the issuance of original bonds , or the city
may , at the time of issuing bonds for the acquisition ,
provide for additional bonds for additions and per-
manent improvements to be placed in escrow and to be
negotiated from time to time as necessary . The bonds
placed in escrow shall , when negotiated , have equal
standing with bonds of the same issue , and if addi-
tional bonds are issued in lieu of bonds placed in
escrow , such additional bonds shall have equal stand-
ing with bonds of prior issue .
7. Any and all such bonds shall have in the hands
of bona fide holders all the qualities of negotiable in-
struments under the law merchant , and shall not be
subject to taxation . If the officers whose signatures
or countersignatures appearing on the bonds or cou-
pons shall cease to be such officers before delivery of
the bonds , the signatures or countersignatures shall
nevertheless be valid and sufficient for all purposes
the same as if they had remained in office until such
delivery . The bonds when issued shall be payable
solely from the revenue funds derived from the oper-
ation , management and control of the cemetery , and

...122 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
•
shall not constitute an indebtedness of the city within
the meaning of the provisions and limitations of the
Constitution of this commonwealth . It shall be plainly
stated on the face of each bond that same has been is-
sued under the provisions of this act and does not
constitute an indebtedness of the city within the mean-
ing of any constitutional provisions or limitations .
8. All money received from any bonds issued pur-
suant hereto shall be applied solely to the purchase ,
establishment , erection and acquisition of the ceme-
tery and necessary appurtenances thereto , provided
the money may be used also to advance the payment
of interest on the bonds during the first three years
following the date of such bonds . And provided fur-
ther that the expenses of operation and maintenance
thereof may be advanced from these funds , or the city
may pay from its general funds the cost of any of the
aforesaid property , or maintenance thereof. There
shall be and there is hereby created a statutory lien
upon the cemetery and necessary appurtenances , in-
cluding personal property , so acquired by the city to
and in favor of the holders of the bonds , and each of
them , and to and in favor of the holders of the coupons
of the bonds . The cemetery , together with appur-
tenances and personal property , established , erected ,
or acquired , shall remain subject to these statutory
liens until the payment in full of the principal and in-
terest of the bonds . A majority of the holders of the
bonds or coupons , may , either at law or in equity by
suit, action , mandamus or other proceedings protect
and enforce the statutory lien hereby conferred and
may by suit or other proceedings enforce and compel
performance of all duties required by this act , includ-
ing the making and collecting of sufficient fees , the
segregation of the income and revenue , and the appli-
cation thereof .
9. Any city already owning property may dedicate
same for cemetery purposes , to be managed , operated

CHAPTER 70 123
and controlled in accordance with the provisions of
this act .
10. Any authority in which is vested the power to
provide, conduct and maintain a cemetery , may accept
any grant or devise of real estate , or any bequest , or
gift of money , or personal property , or any donation ,
the principal or income of which is to be used for ceme-
tery purposes .
11. All funds derived by the cemetery shall be de-
posited to the credit of the commission in some bank
to be designated as depository by the city legislative
body of the city , to be drawn upon by the Chairman of
the board of commissioners and countersigned by the
treasurer of the city.
12 . Whereas this act provides a method of acquir-
ing municipal cemeteries , and whereas several mu-
nicipalities desire to establish same immediately but
may not have an opportunity to do so unless this act
becomes a law , now , therefore , an emergency is de-
clared to exist , and this act shall become of full force
and effect from and after its passage and approval by
the Governor .
Passed March 3 , enrolled March 7 , approved March 11 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 70
(H. B. 38)
AN ACT amending Section 256.010 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 256.010 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) " Lawful Fence " means : (a ) A strong and
sound fence , four feet high , so close that cattle can-
not creep through , made of rails , or plank, or wire and
plank , or iron , or hedge , or stone or brick ; or
( b) A ditch three feet deep and three feet broad ,
with a hedge two feet high or a rail , plank, stone ,

124 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
smooth or barbed wire or brick fence two and one -half
feet high on the margin of the ditch , if the fence is so
close that cattle cannot creep through ; or
( c ) A well-constructed gate four feet high so close
that cattle cannot creep through , made of wood slats
and wood framing or made of metal slats and framing
either or both , forming a part of a fence otherwise
lawful and entering upon a public road or highway or
entering upon a private or public road or passway
over the land of another adjacent owner ; or
(d ) A cattle guard not less than eight ( 8 ) feet wide
and not less than six ( 6 ) feet across with a pit not less
than two ( 2 ) feet six ( 6 ) inches deep with iron pipes
not less than two ( 2 ) nor more than six ( 6 ) inches in
diameter , iron rails or wooden rails not less than
two ( 2 ) nor more than four ( 4 ) inches across the sur-
face exposed to traffic , and not less than five ( 5 ) inches
apart , constituting a part of a fence otherwise lawful
and entering upon a public road or highway or enter-
ing upon a private or public road or passway over the
land of another adjacent landowner .
Provided , however , the definition or description of
a lawful fence or cattle guard in this subparagraph ( d )
shall not apply to the term " cattle guard " as used in
Section 256.150 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
(2 ) As used in this chapter , unless the context
requires otherwise , " railroad " means the person who
owns a right of way and owns or controls a railroad
in this state that has been in operation for five years .
Passed February 29 , enrolled March 1, approved March 8 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 71
(H. B. 52 )
AN ACT authorizing agreements consolidating oil and gas leases
to be executed by trustees under court appointment in cases
where contingent future interests are involved .

CHAPTER 71 125
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. A trustee appointed by any circuit court
of this state in cases where contingent future interests
in land are involved may enter into a consolidation
agreement , as herein defined , subject to approval
by the circuit judge as provided in Section 3 hereof.
The detailed provisions of such agreement may be
such as the court approves , consistently with the pro-
visions of Section 2 hereof.
Sec . 2. A consolidation agreement , within the pro-
visions of this act , is an agreement which provides
for the consolidation of an oil and gas lease , or some
portion thereof , with some other oil and gas lease or
leases , or portions thereof, so that the oil and gas
leases or portions thereof embraced in such agree-
ment shall be operated and developed for oil and gas
purposes as one unit lease , and all royalties which
may accrue from production on such consolidated
area shall be treated as an entirety and shall be di-
vided among and paid to the owners ( or their lawful
representatives ) of the royalties in such area upon
an acreage basis , so that each shall receive a share of
such royalties calculated upon the basis of the ratio
between each owner's interest in royalty and the
entire interest in the royalties embraced in the con-
solidated area .
Sec . 3. (a) Upon the execution of such a consoli-
dation agreement , the trustee shall file with the Clerk
of the Circuit Court in the county in which the land
covered by the lease to be consolidated hereunder , or
the greater portion thereof, lies , a petition for an
order approving such consolidation agreement
setting forth a copy of the consolidation agreement
and a statement of facts constituting the grounds
relied upon to secure the approval of such agreement ,
and shall submit with the petition the affidavits of

126 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
not less than three ( 3 ) disinterested owners of real
estate in the county wherein the proceedings here-
under are brought , as to whether such agreement will
be advantageous or beneficial to the beneficiaries for
whom such trustee is acting , which affidavit shall be
filed and preserved as records of the circuit court .
( b) No agreement permitted by this act shall be
valid or effective until the judge of said court ap-
points a guardian ad litem to represent the benefi-
ciaries for whom such trustee is acting and whose
interests are sought to be consolidated hereunder .
The guardian ad litem shall file an answer and the
affidavits of at least three witnesses , proving the
advantages or disadvantages of such agreement , and
shall make such recommendation to the judge relating
to the agreement as he believes to be most beneficial
to the persons on whose behalf he is acting . He shall
receive for his services a reasonable compensation ,
to be allowed by the judge and taxed as costs .
(c) The judge shall , either in term time or in
vacation , proceed in a summary manner to approve
or disapprove the consolidation agreement , and if
he approves it he shall endorse his approval thereon .
The order of the judge approving such agreement ,
whether made in term time or in vacation , shall be
entered on the Civil Order Book of the circuit clerk's
office of the county in which the proceedings here-
under are brought.
Passed March 9 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 74
(H. B. 56 )
AN ACT relating to motor vehicle operators ' licenses .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 186.420 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
1

CHAPTER 74 131
(1 ) No person need obtain an operator's license
to operate a road roller , road machinery or any trac-
tor or implement of husbandry temporarily drawn
or propelled on the highways .
( 2 ) Every person in the service of the Army ,
Navy or Marine Corps of the United States , when
furnished with an operator's permit from the United
States , shall be exempt from an operator's license ,
when operating an official vehicle in the course of his
service .
(3) During the remainder of the period of Na-
tional Emergency and six months thereafter which
was declared to exist by proclamation of the Presi-
dent of the United States dated May 27 , 1941 , any
person in active service in the armed forces of the
United States shall be permitted to operate a motor
vehicle without the operator's license required by
KRS 186.410 .
(4) Any person in the armed forces of the United
States who has in his possession a Kentucky opera-
tor's license which was issued on or after May 26 ,
1941 , may have such license extended without cost
for the remainder of the National Emergency which
was declared by proclamation of the President of the
United States dated May 26 , 1941 , and six months
thereafter , by presenting in person or forwarding
such license together with return postage to the
Circuit Court Clerk .
( 5 ) Six months after the expiration of the Na-
tional Emergency which was declared to exist by
proclamation of the President of the United States
dated May 27 , 1941 , subsection ( 3 ) of this section shall
stand repealed .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 76
(H. B. 60 )
AN ACT authorizing oil , gas and mineral leases and providing
for the management of funds and income received therefrom
where contingent future interests are involved .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. LEASES -CONTINGENT FUTURE IN-
TERESTS .— Where lands , or any estate or interest
therein, are subject to any contingent future interest ,
legal or equitable , by way of remainder , reversion , or
possibility of reverter , upon the happening of a condi-

CHAPTER 76 133
tion subsequent , or otherwise , created by deed , will ,
or otherwise , and whether a trust is involved or not ,
and it is made to appear that it will be advantageous
to the present and ultimate owners of said lands or
estate or interest therein , the court shall have the
power , pending the happening of any contingency and
the vesting of such future interest or interests , to
declare a trust in said lands or estate or interest
therein , appoint a trustee therefor , and to authorize
such trustee to sell , execute and deliver a valid oil ,
gas , coal or other mineral lease covering said lands
or estate or interest therein .
Sec . 2. JURISDICTION - The circuit court of the
county wherein the land sought to be leased , or some
portion thereof , is situated shall have jurisdiction of
the proceedings authorized under this act .
Sec . 3. INSTITUTION OF PROCEEDINGS.-
The proceedings provided for hereby may be insti-
tuted upon the petition of any one or more of the
parties who have a present interest or a contingent
interest in the land or estate or interest therein .
Sec . 4. PARTIES- All of the persons in being
who have any present or contingent interest in the
lands or estate or interest sought to be leased shall
be made parties to the proceedings herein authorized ,
with any infant or infants being represented either
by next friend or statutory guardian or guardian ad
litem , or in the case of constructive service of sum-
mons by a warning order attorney appointed as in
other cases . ( Any person of unsound mind shall be
represented by his committee or by guardian ad litem ,
or, in the case of constructive service of summons as
in civil actions generally, by a warning order attor-
ney appointed as in other cases . ) If the court specif-
ically finds that the welfare or interest of any person
or persons not in being requires special representa-
tion , the court may appoint a trustee ad litem to

134 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
represent such unknown parties not in being or each
separate class thereof, and such trustee ad litem
shall file such pleadings or answer and take such steps
as he deems proper , and such unknown persons will
be fully bound by the proceedings hereunder . Other-
wise , and in the absence of such finding by the court ,
it shall not be necessary to make parties any persons
not in being , either as " Unknown Defendants ' or
otherwise , but the persons in being who are parties
shall stand for and represent the full title and whole
interest in said lands or estate or interest therein , and
all parties not in being who might have some con-
tingent or future interest therein , and all persons ,
whether in being or not in being , having any interest ,
present , future or contingent , in the property sought
to be leased , will be fully bound by the proceedings
hereunder . It shall be permissible , however , to make
defendants any unknown persons who might have
any interest in the land sought to be leased , under
the style of " Unknown Defendants . "
Sec . 5. ALIGNMENT OF PARTIES AND
PROCESS . (a ) Parties to the proceedings here-
under may be aligned either as plaintiffs or defend-
ants , but there shall be one or more parties plaintiff
and one or more parties defendant . (b) In the pro-
ceedings authorized hereunder any defendant who
does not enter his appearance may be served with
process as provided by law with reference to civil
actions generally.
Sec . 6. DECREE .-Should the court appoint a
trustee pursuant to Sec . 1 hereof, the court shall , in
its decree , require such trustee to give a bond in favor
of the owners of the property which is to be leased
and shall fix the amount of such bond . The decree of
the court shall further provide for all the terms and
provisions of the lease which the trustee is authorized
to make . It shall be discretionary with the court as

CHAPTER 76 135
to whether or not the trustee's sale of lease shall be
public or private , and the decree shall provide there-
for, and if a public sale be authorized the court shall,
in its decree , fix and determine the notice to be given .
Such sale shall be for cash , payable on confirmation
of sale . No appraisement shall be required .
Sec . 7. SALE , REPORT OF SALE , AND CON-
FIRMATION OF SALE .- The trustee shall pro-
ceed , conformably to the provisions of such decree ,
to sell the lease authorized thereby , and after making
such sale shall make a report thereof to the court.
Upon filing of such report , the court may hear evi-
dence as to whether or not the sale price is reasonable ,
and , if the court be satisfied with the sale and the
amount received therefor , such sale shall be con-
firmed by the court and the lease executed by the
trustee approved by the court on the face thereof .
—
Sec . 8. SEPARATE GUARDIAN'S OR COM-
MITTEE'S LEASES UNNECESSARY . If a
trustee's lease is granted hereunder , even though
one of the parties who has an interest or possible
interest in the property covered by such lease is a
minor or a person of unsound mind , no separate
guardian's lease or committee's lease shall be neces-
sary .
Sec . 9. FUNDS -MANAGEMENT .-All funds
received by any trustee appointed under this act shall
be held , managed , invested or otherwise dealt with by
such trustee under and pursuant to the direction of
the court , as the rights and interests of the parties
and the equities of the case may require , for the bene-
fit of the persons entitled , or who may become en-
titled, thereto , according to their respective rights
and interests .
Sec . 10.-EFFECT OF PARTIAL INVALIDITY .
The invalidity of any portion of this act shall not
affect the validity of any other portion thereof.
Passed March 9 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

136 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 77
(H. B. 65)
AN ACT to amend subsection ( 2 ) of section 199.990 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes so as to increase the penalty for
violating section 199.320 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 2 ) of section 199.990 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 2 ) (a ) Except as provided in paragraph ( b )
of this subsection , any person who violates KRS
199.320 shall be fined not more than five hundred dol-
lars or imprisoned in the county jail or workhouse
not more than twelve months , or both ;
(b) Instead of the penalty imposed by paragraph
(a ) of this subsection , the court may suspend the
sentence and release the defendant on probation for
one year upon his recognizance , with or without sure-
ties in such sum as the court directs . The recogni-
zance shall provide that the defendant shall make his
personal appearance in court whenever ordered to do
so , and shall provide and care for the child as directed
by the court, and shall further comply with the orders
of the court If the court is satisfied by information
or proof , under oath , that the defendant has violated
the terms of the order , it may revoke the order and
sentence him under the original conviction . Unless
so sentenced , the defendant shall be discharged at the
end of the year, and the conviction shall become void ;
(c) County courts shall have exclusive jurisdic-
tion of all prosecutions under KRS 199.320 .
Passed January 28 , enrolled January 31 , approved February 2 ,
1944 .

2) He shall give all necessary time
tothe busi-

; shall

inall rate litigation and hearings

138 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
office of the Attorney General when his time is not
required by the Railroad Commission .
(3 ) The Assistant Attorney General shall receive
a salary fixed by the Railroad Commission , with the
approval of the Attorney General , in a sum not to
exceed Four Thousand Five Hundred Dollars
($4,500.00 ) per annum . His salary and necessary
expenses shall be paid by the Railroad Commission
out of its appropriation , in monthly installments .
He shall be entitled to the services of a stenographer
at a salary of not to exceed Two Thousand One
Hundred Dollars ( $2,100.00 ) per annum , to be deter-

Attorney General .
Section 2. For the purpose of paying the com-
pensation of the Assistant Attorney General and
stenographer , and expenses provided for in this Act ,
there is appropriated to the Railroad Commission out
of the General Expenditure Fund for each of the
fiscal years 1944-1945 , and 1945-1946 , the sum of
Seven Thousand Dollars ($7,000.00 ) , this to be in
addition to any other appropriation made to the Rail-
road Commission of Kentucky for such years .
Section 3. WHEREAS , it is now impossible for
the Attorney General's Office with its present number
of Assistants to investigate , advise , prepare plead-
ings , attend hearings , prepare briefs , and participate
in oral arguments that are now pending before the
Interstate Commerce Commission , in which hearings
and litigation the Railroad Commission and the ship-
pers and rail carriers of Kentucky are interested , and
demand immediate attention , an emergency is de-
clared to exist , and this bill shall become a law when
properly passed by the General Assembly, and signed
by the Governor .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 79 139
CHAPTER 79
(H. B. 74 )
AN ACT relating to licenses for operators of.motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 186.410 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Every person except those exempted by KRS
186.420 , 186.430 and 186.450 , before operating any
motor vehicle upon a highway , shall annually pro-
cure an operator's license . The license shall cost
$1.00 , of which ten cents shall be retained by the
circuit court clerk as compensation for his services
in indexing the records of operators ' licenses issued
in his county as provided in KRS 186.495 , and of
which fifteen cents shall be retained by the circuit
court clerk to defray expenses of administering KRS
186.400 to 186.640 . Five cents shall be forwarded to
the department by the clerk for the purpose of print-
ing necessary forms and expenses of administering
KRS 186.400 to 186.640 , and the remaining seventy
cents and other monies not otherwise distributed ,
derived from the provisions of KRS 186.400 to
186.640 , shall be paid into the State Treasury .
2. A new section of the statutes is created , to be
numbered KRS 186.495 , and to read as follows :
The circuit clerk of each county shall maintain an
alphabetical index of all persons to whom operator's
licenses have been issued in his county , which index
shall be sufficient to enable the prompt locating of the
complete license record of each of such persons .
Such index shall at all times be available for inspec-
tion by the State Highway Patrol and other officers
charged with the duty of enforcing highway laws .
Passed March 3, enrolled March 7 , approved March 10 , 1944 .

140 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 80
(H. B. 79 )
AN ACT relating to Insurance , amending sections 299.010 ,
299.310 , 299.380 , 299.420 and 299.400 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 299.310 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Twenty -five or more persons residing in any
one or more adjoining municipalities , or in any
county , or in not more than ten adjoining counties ,
who collectively own property of the value of fifty
thousand dollars or more , may organize a company
for the purpose of cooperative or assessment insur-
ance against loss or damage of property by fire , wind
or lightning , or the explosion of natural or artificial
gas , or hail , or explosion , except explosion by steam
boilers or flywheels , or riot , or riot attending a strike ,
or aircraft , or smoke , or vehicles , by making and
acknowledging a certificate setting forth their inten-
tion to form such a company , the counties or munici-
palities in which it intends to do business , its corpo-
rate name , and the place where its principal office
is to be located . Every person insured in such a com-
pany who signs an application for insurance as re-
quired by the certificate of incorporation or the by-
laws of the company shall thereby become a member .
(2) No money shall be collected by any person on
behalf of the company until two of the members or
organizers have given joint bond to the director in
the sum of ten thousand dollars , conditioned that all
money so collected will be used as directed by law ,
and that the affairs of the company will be conducted
according to law . The bond shall be held by the di-
rector for the benefit of the members of the company
until the company has become legally incorporated

CHAPTER 80 141
and its affairs have been examined by an expert
accountant , appointed by the director , and found to
be in due and regular form , and immediately there-
after the bond shall be cancelled .
(3 ) No company shall be formed under KRS
299.310 to 299.470 for the purpose of transacting any
business of insurance other than as prescribed in
those sections , and no company shall insure against
any loss other than the ones permitted by those sec-
tions . Any company heretofore organized or operat-
ing under these sections may issue and grant insur-
ance against any or all of the hazards named herein .
§ 2. Section 299.400 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Any company may collect such membership
fees or policy fees as are provided for in its by -laws ,
and any portion of the fees not expended for agents '
commissions and the expenses of writing insurance
shall be placed in the reserve fund of the company .
( 2 ) The directors or executive committee of the
company may declare , levy and issue assessments in
such amounts and at such times as in their judgment
is for the best interests of the company . When the
reserve fund equals one and one -half percent of the
insurance in force against fire , and two and one -half
percent of the insurance in force against windstorm ,
and one-half of one percent of the insurance in force
against any other hazard , then no assessment shall
be made for a greater sum than the amount of losses
and expenses sustained and accrued since the last
previous assessment was issued . All assessments
shall be made pro rata upon all property at such time
insured, according to its classification .
(3) If at any time the total liabilities of a com-
pany exceed its actual cash assets to an amount equal
to fifty cents for each one hundred dollars of insur-
ance held by the company , the secretary shall notify

142 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the officer whose duty it is to call a meeting of the di-
rectors or executive committee , and that officer shall
immediately call the directors or executive committee
in session . The directors or executive committee
shall, within thirty days , declare , levy and issue an
assessment sufficient to pay all the liabilities of the
company that are in excess of its actual cash assets .
§ 3. Subsection ( 4 ) of section 299.010 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(4 ) " Company ", as used in KRS 299.310 to
299.470 , means any corporation organized under
KRS 299.310 and 299.320 for the purpose of transact-
ing , and any company heretofore organized under
any similar law of this state or under the general
corporation laws of this state that is transacting , the
business of insurance against loss or damage of
property by fire , wind or lightning , or by the explo-
sion of natural or artificial gas , or hail , or explosion ,
except explosion by steam boilers or flywheels , or
riot, or riot attending a strike , or aircraft , or smoke ,
or vehicles , upon the cooperative or assessment plan .
4. Subsection ( 1 ) of section 299.380 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The directors of every company shall cause
to be issued policies of insurance signed by their
president and secretary , agreeing in the name of the
company to pay all losses or damages , not exceeding
the amount insured , caused by the risk insured
against, as provided by and during the time named
in the policy . Every policy shall have attached there-
to a printed copy of the bylaws .
5. Section 299.420 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
If the amount of the deposit notes and cash belong-
ing to a company is not sufficient to pay the amount
due at any one time to the policyholders on account of
losses occasioned by the risks insured against , the

CHAPTER 81 143
limit of liability of each policyholder on any assess-
ment that may be required to meet the deficit shall
not exceed the following rates and amounts : Upon
each policyholder of a company having less than five
hundred thousand dollars of insurance in force , ten
dollars for each one hundred dollars of insurance he
has in the company ; upon each policyholder of a com-
pany having over five hundred thousand dollars but
less than one million dollars insurance in force , five
dollars for each one hundred dollars insurance he
has in the company ; upon each policyholder of a com-
pany having over one million dollars but less than
two million dollars insurance in force , three dollars
for each one hundred dollars insurance he has in the
company ; and upon each policyholder of a company
having over two million dollars of insurance in force ,
two dollars for each one hundred dollars insurance
he has in the company .
Passed February 17 , enrolled February 18 , approved February
24 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 81
(H. B. 86)
AN ACT enabling fiscal courts of counties containing cities of
the first and third class , in their discretion to purchase policies
of insurance, of all kinds , on motor vehicles and other vehicles
operated by the county, and compensation insurance to protect
employes of the county against injuries received in the course
of employment ; provided , the insurance carrier , by its policy
of insurance , waives the right to contest or deny liability on
the ground of county governmental function or capacity , and
to pay the premiums on all policies so purchased out of the
county funds .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That the fiscal court , in each county , having therein
a city of the first or third class , may , in its discretion,

144 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for the protection of the public and its employees ,
appropriate county funds to purchase policies of in-
surance , of all kinds , deemed advisable , covering
motor vehicles and other vehicles operated by the
county , and compensation insurance covering em-
ployees of the county receiving injuries arising out
of and in the course of employment ; provided , the
insurance carrier , by its policy of insurance , waives
therein the right to contest or deny liability , by
denying the liability of the county because of its
governmental capacity and immunity on that account ,
and , provided , said policy of insurance shall bind the
company to pay any final judgment rendered against
the county for loss , or damage to property of any
person or death or injury of any person .
Passed February 17 , enrolled February 18 , approved February
24, 1944 .
CHAPTER 82
(H. B. 90 )
AN ACT to repeal and re - enact Sections 342.005 , 342.315 , 342.185 ,
and 342.435 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , relating to
Workmen's Compensation , and to provide compensation for
disability or death due to the disease of silicosis .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. That Section 342.005 , Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , be repealed and re -enacted so that the same ,
when re-enacted , shall read as follows :
342.005 . Persons to Whom Chapter Applies . This
act shall apply to all employers having three or more
employees regularly engaged in the same occupation ,
or business , and to their employees , except that it
shall not apply to domestic employment , agriculture ,
steam railways , or such common carriers other than
steam railways for which a rule of liability is provid-

CHAPTER 82 145
ed by the laws of the United States . Provided , how-
ever , it shall apply to the operators of threshing ma-
chines used in threshing or hulling grain or seeds . It
shall affect the liability of the employers subject
thereto to their employees for a personal injury sus-
tained by the employee by accident arising out of and
in the course of his employment , or for death result-
ing from such accidental injury ; provided , however ,
that personal injury by accident as herein defined
shall not include diseases except where the disease is
the natural and direct result of a traumatic injury by
accident , nor shall they include the results of a pre-
existing disease but shall include injuries or death due
to inhalation in mines of noxious gases or smoke , com-
monly known as ' bad air ' , and also shall include injur-
ies or death due to the inhalation of any kind of gas .
Any employers and employees who are by the provi-
sions of this section excepted from the provisions of
this Act , including employers having less than three
employees , may subject themselves thereto by joint ,
voluntary application to the board , in writing , for
such period as may be stated in the application , which
shall be irrevocable during such period and effective
thereafter until a written revocation be filed with the
board or the employment be terminated , and any em-
ployers and their employees may , with respect to the
disease of silicosis caused by the inhalation of silica
dust, in like manner voluntarily subject themselves
thereto as to such disease .
2. That section 342.315 , Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , be repealed and re -enacted so that the same ,
when re -enacted , shall read as follows :
342.315 . Board to appoint physicians or surgeons
to examine employee ; fees and expenses ; examina-
tion for occupational disease .
(1 ) The board , or any member thereof , may , upon
the application of either party or upon its own mo-

146 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion , appoint a disinterested and duly qualified phy-
sician or surgeon to make any necessary medical ex-
amination of the employee and to testify in respect
thereto . Said physician or surgeon shall be allowed
traveling expenses and a reasonable fee to be fixed by
the Board , but not exceeding fifteen dollars ( $ 15.00 )
for each examination and report , but the Board may
allow additional reasonable amounts in extraordi-
nary cases . The fees and expenses of such physician
or surgeon shall be paid out of the maintenance fund .
( 2 ) The Workmen's Compensation Board , with
the approval of the Governor , shall appoint a Medical
Commitee composed of three disinterested and duly
qualified physicians who are experienced in the diag-
nosis and/or treatment of occupational diseases in-
cluding silicosis , two of whom shall be qualified radi-
ologists , whose headquarters shall be in the City of
Louisville ; said Medical Committee shall be appoint-
ed for terms of four years , except that , of the original
Committee , one member shall be appointed for a term
of one year , one for two years , and one for three years ,
respectively , commencing April 1 , 1944. Upon the
expiration of the term of each member , a successor
shall be appointed for a term of four years . Claims
for compensation for disability or death from silicosis
shall be referred to the Medical Committee by the
Board as hereinafter provided . Said physicians shall
examine the employee and certify their findings to the
Workmen's Compensation Board , which findings
shall state ( 1 ) in what stage , if any , the employee is
suffering from the disease of silicosis ; ( 2 ) whether
the disability of the employee is temporary , partial
or total , and ( 3 ) the extent , if any , of the impairment
of capacity to work of the employee . The physicians
so appointed shall be allowed necessary traveling ex-
penses and reasonable fees , to be fixed by the Board ,
but not to exceed a fee of twenty dollars ( $20.00 ) to

CHAPTER 82 147
each physician for making such examination and re-
port , plus a reasonable allowance for expenses in-
curred in making such examinations ; such fees and
expenses so approved by the Board shall be paid out
of the maintenance fund .
3. Occupational diseases ; general provisions .
(a) Wherever used in this act " silicosis " shall
mean the characteristic fibrotic conditions of the
lungs caused by the inhalation of dust of silica .
(b) No compensation shall be payable for silicosis
if the employee , at the time of entering into the em-
ployment of the employer by whom the compensation
would otherwise be payable , falsely represented him-
self, in writing , as not having previously been dis-
abled , laid off, or compensated in damages or other-
wise , because of such disease , or failed or omitted
truthfully to state to the best of his knowledge , in
answer to written inquiry made by the employer , the
place , duration , and nature of previous employment
or, to the best of his knowledge , the previous status
of his health .
(c) Where silicosis is aggravated by any other
disease or infirmity , not itself compensable , or where
disability or death from any other cause , not itself
compensable , is aggravated , prolonged , accelerated
or in anywise contributed to by silicosis , the compen-
sation payable shall be reduced and limited to such
proportion only of the compensation that would be
payable if silicosis were the sole cause of the disability
or death as such silicosis , as a causative factor , bears
to all the causes of such disability or death , such re-
duction in compensation to be effected by reducing the
number of weekly or monthly payments or the
amounts of such payments as under the circum-
stances of the particular case may be for the best
interest of the claimant or claimants .

148 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(d ) No compensation for death from silicosis shall
be payable to any person whose relationship to the
deceased , which , under the provisions of this act
would give right to compensation , arose subsequent
to the beginning of the first compensable disability
save only to afterborn children of a marriage existing
at the beginning of such disability .
(e ) This section shall not apply to cases of silicosis
in which the last injurious exposure to the hazards
of such disease occurred before this section shall have
taken effect .
(f) Where compensation is payable for silicosis
the employer in whose employment the employee was
last injuriously exposed to the hazards of such dis-
ease, and the insurance carrier , if any , on the risk
when such employee was last so exposed under such
employer , shall be liable therefor ; the amount of the
compensation shall be based upon the average wages
of the employee when last so exposed under such em-
ployer ; and the notice of injury and claim for com-
pensation , as herein required , shall be given and made
to such employer ; provided , however , that the only
employer and insurance carrier liable shall be the last
employer in whose employment the employee was last
injuriously exposed to the hazards of the disease dur-
ing a period of sixty ( 60 ) days or more after the ef-
fective date of this act , and the insurance carrier , if
any , on the risk when the employee was last so ex-
posed under such employer .
(g ) The employer shall not be liable for any com-
pensation for silicosis unless such disease shall be
due to the nature of an employment in which the haz-
ards of such disease actually exist and are character-
istic thereof and peculiar to the trade , occupation ,
process , or employment , and is actually incurred in
the course and arising out of his employment and un-
less disablement or death results within three years

CHAPTER 82 149
after the last injurious exposure to such disease in
such employment , or , in case of death , unless death
follows continuous disability from such disease , com-
mencing within the period above limited , for which
compensation has been paid or awarded or timely
claim made as provided herein , and results within
seven (7 ) years after such last exposure . Compen-
sation shall not be payable for disability or death due
to silicosis unless the employee shall have been ex-
posed to the inhalation of dust of silica in his employ-
ment for a period of not less than two ( 2 ) years in this
State , provided no part of such period of two years
shall have been more than five ( 5 ) years prior to the
last exposure .
(h) Except as herein otherwise provided , proce-
dure with respect to notice of disability or death , and
as to the filing of claims and determination of claims ,
and the compensation payable for disability or death ,
shall be the same as in cases of accidental injury or
death under the general provisions of this act . Unless
written notice of the first distinct manifestation or
knowledge of the existence of silicosis shall be given
to the employer in whose employment the employee
was last injuriously exposed to the hazards of such
disease or to the Workmen's Compensation Board
within ninety ( 90 ) days after such manifestation or
knowledge , and , in case of death unless also written
notice of such death shall be given by or on behalf of
those claiming to be dependents of such deceased em-
ployee , to the employer or the Workmen's Compen-
sation Board within ninety ( 90 ) days after the death
of such employee , and unless claim for disability , or
for death , as the case may be, shall be made upon the
employer sought to be held liable therefor within one
( 1 ) year after disablement or death , respectively , all
rights to compensation for disability or death from
such silicosis shall be forever barred ; provided , how-

150 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ever , that notice or claim shall be deemed waived in
case of disability or death where the employer or in-
surance carrier voluntarily makes compensation pay-
ments therefor or , within the above time limits , has
actual knowledge of the incurrence of the disease , or
of the death of the employee , and its cause , and , pro-
vided further , that where compensation payments
have been made and discontinued , and further com-
pensation is claimed , whether for disability or death
from silicosis , the claim for such further compensa-
tion shall be made within one ( 1 ) year after the last
payment of compensation .
(i) If on a claim for compensation for silicosis any
medical question shall be in controversy the Board
shall refer the case to the Medical Committee selected
by the Board for investigation and report . No award
shall be made in any case until the Medical Committee
shall have duly investigated the case and made its re-
port with respect to all medical questions at issue .
The date of disablement , if in dispute , shall be deemed
a medical question .
(j ) The Medical Committee , upon reference to it
of a claim for silicosis , shall notify the claimant or
claimants and the employer to appear before the Med-
ical Committee at a time and place stated in the notice ,
not to exceed 30 days from date claim is referred to
the Committee . If the employee be living , he shall
appear before the Medical Committee at the time and
place specified then or thereafter to submit to such ex-
amination including clinical and X-ray examination
as the Medical Committee may require . The claim-
ant and the employer or his insurance carrier shall
each be entitled , at his own expense , to have present
at all examinations conducted by the Medical Com-
mittee , a physician admitted to practice in the state
who shall be given every reasonable facility for par-
ticipating in every such examination . If a physician
•

CHAPTER 82 151
admitted to practice medicine in the State shall cer-
tify that the employee is physically unable to appear
at the time and place designated by the Medical Com-
mittee , such Committee shall , on notice to the parties ,
change the place and time of examination to such
other place and time as may reasonably facilitate the
examination of the employee . Proceedings shall be
suspended and no compensation be payable for any
period during which the employee may refuse to sub-
mit to such examination .
(k) The Medical Committee shall , as soon as prac-
ticable after it has completed its consideration of the
case , report in writing its findings and conclusions on
every medical question in controversy . If the date
of disablement is controverted and cannot be exactly
fixed scientifically, the Medical Committee shall fix
the most probable date , having regard to all the cir-
cumstances of the case . The Medical Committee shall
also include in its report a statement indicating the
physician or physicians , if any , who appeared before
it , and what, if any , medical reports and X -rays were
considered by it.
( 1 ) The Medical Committee shall file its report
with the Board , which shall send a certified copy
thereof to the claimant and to the employer and his
insurance carrier , if any . The decision or award in
the case shall conform to the findings and conclusions
in such report in so far as restricted to medical ques-
tions ; provided , however , that any such findings and
conclusions may be set aside , reversed or modified
by the Board upon a review of the Medical Commit-
tee's award or decision , in case an application for re-
view by the Board is made within ten ( 10 ) days from
the date of the award , subject , however , to the fol-
lowing special provisions : No such finding or con-
clusion of the Medical Committee shall be subject to
review unless specific objection thereto shall be filed

152 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
with the Board by a party in interest within the time
limited in which to apply for review of an award or
decision . If objection is so filed , notice thereof shall
be given to the Medical Committee , whereupon such
Committee shall delegate one or more of its members
to appear at the hearing or hearings on review to sub-
mit to examination and cross -examination in respect
to the findings and conclusions objected to . On each
review , evidence to controvert findings or conclusions
of the Medical Committee shall be limited to the ex-
amination and cross -examination of the member or
members of such Committee appearing on such re-
view and of the physician or physicians who appeared
in the case before the Medical Committee on behalf
of the claimant or employer . Upon such review no
finding or conclusion of the Medical Committee upon
a medical question shall be set aside , reversed , or mod-
ified unless proved to be manifestly erroneous or un-
reasonable , or due to fraud , undue influence , inadvert-
ence or mistake of fact or law . But , with the consent
of the Medical Committee , its report in any case may ,
upon review, be remanded to it for reconsideration .
Every decision by the Board that sets aside , reverses ,
affirms , or modifies a finding or conclusion by the
Medical Committee shall be subject to review by the
courts , upon appeal , on all questions , whether of law
or fact .
(m ) Upon the filing of a claim for compensation for
death from silicosis where an autopsy is necessary
accurately and scientifically to determine the cause
of death , such autopsy shall be ordered by the Board
and shall be made under the supervision of the cor-
oner . The Board may designate a duly licensed phy-
sician , who is a specialist in such examinations , to
perform or attend such autopsy and to certify his
findings thereon . Such findings shall be filed with
the Board . The Board may also exercise such author-

CHAPTER 82 153
ity on its own motion or on application made to it at
any time , upon the presentation of facts showing that
a controversy may arise in regard to the cause of
death or the existence of silicosis . All proceedings
for compensation shall be suspended upon refusal of
a claimant or claimants to permit such autopsy when
so ordered , and no compensation shall be payable dur-
ing the continuation of such refusal . No report of
an autopsy , or testimony as to facts disclosed thereby ,
shall be received in evidence unless such autopsy shall
have been conducted in accordance with this subsec-
tion or unless representatives of all parties shall have
participated therein or been given reasonable oppor-
tunity so to do .
(n) In case of disability or death from silicosis
complicated with tuberculosis of the lungs , compen-
sation shall be payable as for uncomplicated silicosis ,
provided , however , that the silicosis was an essential
factor in causing such disability or death . In case of
disability or death from silicosis complicated with
any other disease , or from any other disease compli-
cated with silicosis , the compensation shall be reduced
as provided in section 3 ( c) hereof .
(0) Where an employee , though not actually dis-
abled from silicosis , is found by the Medical Commit-
tee to be affected by silicosis to such a degree as to
make it unduly hazardous for him to continue in an
employment involving exposure to the hazards of
such disease , the Board may order that he be removed
from his employment , in which case or in case he has
already been discharged from such employment and
is unemployed , he shall be entitled to compensation
(subject , however , to the same conditions relative to
exposure prior to removal or discharge from employ-
ment as are in this section relative to exposure prior
to disability or death ) until he can obtain steady em-
ployment in some other suitable occupation in which

154 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
there are no hazards of such disease ; provided , how-
ever , that such compensation shall in no case be pay-
able for longer than twenty -six ( 26 ) weeks immedi-
ately following the date of removal or discharge and
unless application therefor is made within such pe-
riod ; but neither a claim for nor receipt of compen-
sation under the provisions of this sub- section shall
bar the employee from any right to compensation for
actual disability from silicosis if such disability re-
sults not later than within the time limited in sub -sec-
tion (g ) hereof, except that any compensation paid
under this sub -section shall be credited upon any com-
pensation which may become payable for actual dis-
ability . Compensation payable under this sub - sec-
tion shall not be barred nor offset by any compensa-
tion for unemployment which the employee may, for
the same period , be entitled to under the provisions of
any other law of this State .
4. That Section 342.185 , Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , be repealed and re -enacted so that the same
when re -enacted , shall read as follows :
342.185 . Employer to be notified of accident ; who
may make claim ; time limit . No proceeding under
this act for compensation for an injury or death shall
be maintained unless a notice of the accident shall
have been given to the employer as soon as practicable
after the happening thereof and unless a claim for
compensation with respect to such injury shall have
been made within one year after the date of the acci-
dent , or, in case of death , within one year after such
death , whether or not a claim has been made by the
employee himself for compensation . Such notice and
such claim may be given or made by any person claim-
ing to be entitled to compensation or by some one in
his behalf. If payments of compensation as such have
been made voluntarily the making of a claim within
such period shall not be required , but shall become

CHAPTER 82 155
•
requisite following the suspension of such voluntary
payments .
5. That Section 342.435 , Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , be repealed and re -enacted so that the same when
re -enacted , shall read as follows :
342.435 . Annual report to be made by the board ;
printing of report ; distribution of information . An-
nually on or before the 15th day of December the
Board shall make a report to the Governor for the
preceding fiscal year , which shall include a statement
of the number of awards made and of claims rejected
by it, a general statement of the causes of accident
leading to the injuries for which awards were made
or rejected claims based , and a detailed statement of
the disbursements from and unpaid expenses charge-
able against the maintenance fund and its condition ,
together with any other information which the Board
deems proper to call to the attention of the Governor ,
including any recommendations it may have to make ,
and it shall be the duty of the board to publish and
distribute among employers and employees such gen-
eral information as to the business transacted by the
department as may be useful and necessary .
The annual report shall not exceed ten thousand
copies . All printing of the department shall be.done
by the contractor or contractors for public printing ,
subject to such provisions of the general laws gov-
erning public printing as may be applicable thereto .
6. Whereas , in various industries of this State
there exists a hazard of silicosis and , under the pres-
ent provisions of the Workmen's Compensation Act ,
the employers and employees engaged in such indus-
tries cannot elect to extend the coverage of the act to
disability or death resulting from silicosis , an emer- .
gency is declared to exist and this act shall become
effective upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor .
Passed March 8 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 20 , 1944 .

156 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 83
(H. B. 93)
AN ACT relating to the number and compensation of deputies
of circuit court clerks and county court clerks in counties con-
taining a population of 75,000 or more .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 28.300 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes be repealed , amended and re -enacted to read as
follows :
Deputy Clerks in counties containing a population
of 75,000 or more .
(1 ) In each County containing a population of 75 , -
000 or more , the number of deputies allowed to Circuit
Clerks and County Clerks , and the compensation al-
lowed to each deputy , shall be fixed as provided in
subsections ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) , ( 4 ) and ( 5 ) of this section , by an
order entered upon the order book of the Circuit
Court and County Court , and signed by a majority of
the judges of said Courts . A certified copy of the
order , and of any subsequent changes made therein ,
shall , as soon as entered , be forwarded to the Depart-
ment of Finance .
(2) In Counties containing a population of 75,000
or more and up to 90,000 , the salary of the chief deputy
circuit court clerk shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding $ 2,500 per annum , and the
salaries of the other deputy circuit clerks shall be
fixed at a reasonable amount not exceeding $2,000
per annum .
In counties containing a population of 90,000 or
more and up to 200,000 , the salary of the chief deputy
circuit court clerk shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding $3,000 per annum , and the sal-
aries of the other deputy circuit court clerks shall
be fixed at a reasonable amount not exceeding $2,000
per annum .
In counties having a population of 200,000 or over

CHAPTER 84 157
the salary of the chief deputy circuit court clerk shall
be fixed at a reasonable amount not exceeding $3,000
a year , and the salary of one deputy circuit court clerk
who shall act as cashier shall be fixed at a reasonable
amount not exceeding $ 2,500 a year , and the salary
of each of the other deputies in the office of the circuit
court clerk shall be fixed at a reasonable amount not
exceeding $2,000 a year .
(3) In Counties containing a population of 75,000
or more and up to 90,000 , the salaries of the first four
deputy county clerks shall be fixed at reasonable
amounts , which need not be the same, and none of
which shall exceed $ 2,500 per annum . The salaries
of each of the other deputy county clerks in such
counties shall be fixed at a reasonable amount not
exceeding $2,000 per annum .
(4) In counties containing a population of 90,000
or more and up to 200,000 , the salaries of the first
four county clerks shall be fixed at reasonable
amounts , which need not be the same , and none of
which shall exceed $3,000 per annum . The salaries
of each of the other deputy county clerks in such coun-
ties shall be fixed at a reasonable amount not exceed-
ing $ 2,000 per annum .
( 5 ) In counties having a population of 200,000 or
over the salaries of the first four deputy county clerks
shall be fixed at a reasonable amount not exceeding
$3,000 a year and the salary of each of the other dep-
uties in the office of the county clerk shall be fixed at a
reasonable amount not exceeding $2,000 a year .
Passed February 17 , enrolled February 25 , approved March 8 ,
1944 .
CHAPTER 84
(H. B. 94 )
AN ACT to amend Section 67.110 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , relating to the Compensation of Justices and Com-
missioners for serving on Fiscal Courts and committees
thereof.

158 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. Section 67.110 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Each county commissioner serving on a fiscal
court may be paid out of the county treasury the fol-
lowing compensation for his services :
(a) In counties containing a city of the first or sec-
ond class , a salary of Three Thousand Dollars
( $3,000.00 ) per annum , payable monthly .
(b) In other counties , the same compensation per
day as justices of the peace receive under subsections
(2 ) and ( 3 ) of this section .
( 2) Each justice of the peace serving on a fiscal
court shall be paid for his services out of the county
treasury six dollars for each day of actual attendance
of the meetings of the fiscal court .
(3) County commissioners or justices of the peace
shall not receive any other compensation for their
services , or any compensation for serving on commit-
tees of the fiscal court, except that in counties where
the fiscal court controls and supervises the county
roads through a committee of its members the justices
of the peace serving on that committee shall be paid
out of the county treasury six dollars for each day
of actual service .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 86
(H. B. 98 )
AN ACT relating to primary elections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 1 ) of 119.050 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Except as provided in KRS 119.070 and
119.075 , any person who is qualified under KRS
119.200 to vote in any primary election for the candi-
dates for nomination by the party at whose hands
he seeks the nomination , shall have his name printed
on the official ballot of his party for an office to which
he is eligible in that primary , upon filing , with the
proper officer at the proper time , a notification and
declaration .
2. A new section of the statutes is created , to
be numbered KRS 119.075 and to read as follows :
( 1 ) The provisions of KRS 119.050 shall not ap-
ply to any eligible person serving in the Armed Forces
of the United States while this country is at war . The
name of such person shall be printed on the official
ballot of his party for an office for which he is eligible
in any primary election when an application has been
filed on his behalf by two reputable electors of the
party at whose hands he seeks the nomination .
(2 ) Such application shall be accompanied by a
statement signed by the proposed candidate author-
izing the action of the electors .

162 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(3 ) The application and the candidate's state-
ment , herein mentioned , shall be filed in good faith
and with the proper officer at the proper time , as pro-
vided for other candidates in primary elections .
(4) The form of such application shall be similar
to that of the affidavits required of electors in behalf
of other candidates as provided in KRS 119.050 .
(5) No certificate of nomination shall be given to
any such successful soldier , sailor or marine candi-
date until he has caused to be filed with the Clerk of
the County Court , in the county of his legal residence ,
a statement containing such information as is re-
quired , in section 119.070 , of successful candidates for
judicial nominations .
Passed February 14 , enrolled February 18 , approved February
24, 1944 .
CHAPTER 87
(H. B. 102 )
AN ACT to amend Section 91.320 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
1942 , ( First Edition ) so as to permit the joint taking of sched-
ules or lists of taxable property by the city assessor of cities
of the first class and county tax commissioner , or their depu-
ties , simultaneously , in the office of the county tax commis-
sioner, and empowering either such officer , or his deputies , to
administer oaths to taxpayers returning such lists or schedules ,
whether the property be located in the county outside the city ,
or in the city , and declaring an emergency and providing for
notices .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 91.320 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes , 1942 , ( First Edition ) is hereby amended
and as amended reads :
(1) Every person owning or holding taxable prop-
erty, either in his own right or as a fiduciary or agent ,
shall return to the assessor or his assistant a true list
of all such property , stating the value of the personal

CHAPTER 87 163
property , upon blanks prepared for that purpose by
the assessor , in the form prescribed by ordinance , and
shall make oath before the assessor or his assistant .
However , if it be deemed expedient , the city asses-
sor may mail , by postal card , appropriate notice to
the last known address of the taxpayer and by agree-
ment with the county tax commissioner receive the
return of the said true list or schedule of all such tax-
able property in the office of the county tax commis-
sioner simultaneously with the taking of such list or
schedule for State and county purposes by the county
tax commissioner , and under such arrangement the
city tax assessor shall provide for such purpose a
sufficient number of deputies to expeditiously per-
form such duties and they shall perform in the office
of the county tax commissioner during the period
necessary to complete said duties . If such arrange-
ment is made , both the city assessor and the county
tax commissioner , and the deputies of each and either
are hereby empowered to administer any oath to the
taxpayer , provided by law in connection with the
return of any list or schedule , whether the property
be located in the county outside the city or in the
corporate limits of the city . The method of payment
of salaries to said deputies engaged in said work shall
not be affected by such arrangement .
If the office of the county tax commissioner is put
to any additional expense by reason of the taking of
such lists or schedules for city property , such addi-
tional expense shall be paid by the city .
Nothing in this act shall be construed as an accept-
ance by the city of the assessment of the county tax
commissioner on property located in the city , but for
the sole purpose of convenience and economy .
( 2 ) If any such person fails to return a true list
under oath , the assessor may , according to the best
information he can obtain , assess the property .
(3 ) The police court shall , at the instance of the as-

164 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sessor , enforce by rule or process of contempt the re-
turn , under oath , of the list required by this section .
(4 ) The assessor shall each day make diligent
search among the conveyances and probated wills
recorded in the office of the county clerk and among
the confirmations of sales in the courts , and shall also
make personal inquiry in his yearly rounds about
deaths among the owners of lands or improvements
in the city .
(5 ) The assessor shall keep in alphabetical order
a register of all transfers of real estate . All pur-
chasers of real estate in the city shall furnish the as-
sessor information thereof within two days after the
conveyance has been lodged for record . The assessor
shall notify such purchasers of any taxes that remain
unpaid and are a lien on the property bought .
Because of the shortage of manpower and the
necessity for elimination of unnecessary duplication
in the assessing functions of the city assessor and
county tax commissioner , an emergency is declared
and this act shall become effective immediately after
its approval by the Governor .
Passed February 7 , enrolled February 10 , approved February
16 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 88
(H. B. 103 )
AN ACT pertaining to registration for elections in cities of the
first class , amending KRS 117.310 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 117.310 Kentucky Revised
Statutes 1942 is hereby amended and as amended
reads :
117.310 Comparison of voter's signature with reg-
istration record ; voting record .
( 1 ) Prior to every primary , special , or regular ,

CHAPTER 88 165
election in a city of the first class , the County Clerk
shall have prepared for each precinct of the city a
Comparative Signature Book which shall be in per-
manent form and contain sufficient blank sheets of
non -detachable type to afford reasonable space for
as many signatures as there are registered voters in
the precinct . The County Clerk shall follow the in-
structions of the Board of Registration Commission-
ers with respect to the exact form and makeup of the
Comparative Signature Books and they shall be fur-
nished and paid for in the same manner as other elec-
tion paraphernalia . The County Clerk shall deliver
the Comparative Signature Books along with other
election paraphernalia to the clerks of election of the
several precincts who shall sign a receipt therefor .
Before a vote is cast by any person , at any primary ,
special , or regular , election in a city of the first class ,
he shall sign his name in the Comparative Signature
Book , unless he signed his registration record by mak-
ing his mark , and the officers of election shall compare
the signature of every such person with the signature
of that person on the registration record form in the
precinct register . The challengers of the respective
political parties present shall also have the right to
compare the signature at the time the comparison is
made by the officers of election . If the comparison
of signatures creates a doubt in the minds of two of
the election officers as to whether the person applying
to vote is the same person as the registrant , no vote
shall be cast by him unless he otherwise satisfactorily
establishes his identity as the registrant . When the
polls close , the precinct sheriff and judge of the op-
posite political faith shall forthwith convey the Com-
parative Signature Book to the place where the Coun-
ty Clerk receives other election paraphernalia . The
Board of Registration Commissioners shall have at
that place , plainly identified , a duly authorized rep-
resentative to whom the sheriff and judge aforesaid

166 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall deliver the Comparative Signature Book and
secure a receipt therefor . The Board shall retain the
Comparative Signature Books for such length of time ,
not less than six (6 ) months , as it deems necessary .
( 2 ) If any person applying to vote at an election
states that he cannot sign his name , the judges of
election shall immediately swear him as to his name
and his inability to write , and thereupon the judges
and the challengers of the respective political parties
shall examine the registration record in the precinct
registrar , and if it is shown by that record that the
person of that name signed his name on the registra-
tion record , said person shall not be permitted to cast
a vote , unless his inability to sign his name at the date
of the election is due to some physical disability aris-
ing subsequent to his registration . The election of-
ficers shall make and return to the board a record
sheet of each person so sworn , showing the disposi-
tion made of each case . The record sheets shall be
provided as other election paraphernalia .
( 3 ) The clerk in each precinct shall, immediately
after a person has cast his vote at every primary , spe-
cial , and regular , election , indicate at the proper place
on the registration record that such person voted at
the election .
Passed February 14 , enrolled February 18 , approved February
24 , 1944 .
