. The dog license collector appointed under
KRS 258
:370 shall serve for the remainder of the
term
ofthe sheriff requesting his appointment and
upon the termination of his term of office the succeed-

210 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ing sheriff of the county shall collect the license fees
as prescribed in this chapter unless said succeeding
sheriff requests the appointment of a collector as
provided in KRS 258.370 . The fiscal court may
remove the dog license collector at any time by ma-
jority vote of the members of the court , with or with-
out cause , and appoint a successor .
Section 258.390 . Bond of license collector . The
collector of dog licenses appointed under KRS
258.370 shall execute bond in the sum of $2,500.00 to
the State of Kentucky , for the use and benefit of the
county appointing him , to secure it against any loss
of money collected by him that may be in his posses-
sion belonging to the county , and such bond as may
be required by the fiscal court to indemnify the county
against any damage on account of the acts of said
collector .
Section 258.400 . Compensation of license collec-
tor ; disposition of license monies . The collector of
dog licenses appointed under KRS 258.370 shall be
entitled to twenty percent of the amount collected
from dog licenses , which shall be his total compensa-
tion for issuing the license . He shall, however , be
entitled to and receive the fees and costs stipulated
in KRS 258.140 for the services therein mentioned in
addition to his fees for issuing the licenses . He shall
make a report to the County Clerk on the first day of
each month showing the number of licenses issued
and pay to the county treasurer funds which he has
collected , less the twenty percent which he shall
retain for himself and six percent of the total amount
collected which he shall pay to the Commissioner of
Agriculture Labor and Statistics . The county
treasurer shall credit the balance to the livestock
fund .
Section 258.410 . Duties of dog license collector .
When a dog license collector is appointed under KRS

CHAPTER 115 211
258.370 he shall perform the functions required under
this chapter of the Kentucky Revised Statutes to be
performed by the sheriff , and shall have all the powers
vested in the sheriff by the provisions of Chapter 258
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1942 Edition in
connection therewith . After the appointment of the
dog license collector as provided in KRS 258.370 the
sheriff of the respective county shall be relieved of
the duties imposed by the provisions of this chapter ,
and the sheriff shall not thereafter receive any sum
in fees , commission , or compensation from the
issuance or sale of dog licenses in the respective
county , or for any of the services mentioned in
Chapter 258 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1942
Edition , it being the intention of the General Assem-
bly in the passage of this act to provide an alternate
method for the collection of dog licenses , and the ad-
ministration of the provisions of Chapter 258 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1942 Edition .
Passed March 9 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 115
(H. B. 190 )
AN ACT relating to probate law and procedure , including pro-
visions concerning trusts and the estates of decedents and
persons under disability .
WHEREAS , the laws of the State of Kentucky relating to probate
procedure were amended by the General Assembly at its 1942
Session , and that the same are in need of further revision for
the purpose of simplifying and expediting the administration
of estates in the interest both of creditors and beneficiaries and
for the purpose of authorizing sales by fiduciaries in cases
where such authority does not exist or is doubtful , NOW
THEREFORE ,
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Notice to Creditors ; Publication . At least
once each month and not more than once each week ,

212 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the clerk of each county court shall cause to be pub-
lished in a newspaper having general circulation in
the county a notice containing all of the appointments
of administrators and executors made during the
previous month , or since the last preceding publica-
tion , which shall be substantially as follows :
"Notice to Creditors . Administration has been
granted by the county court upon the following
estates :
deceased ,
Administrator or Executor ,
Address
All persons having claims against said estates are
notified to present them to the Administrator or
Executor , verified according to law , not later than
(Said date to be fixed by the clerk as
three months after the publication of the notice . ) ”
The actual cost of such notice or the proportionate
part thereof , if it contains more than one appoint-
ment , shall be charged to each fiduciary by the clerk
as costs . The court may in its discretion dispense
with such publication when the gross assets of an
estate are insufficient to pay preferred claims . The
court may also dispense with publication in cases
where the gross assets of an estate , including real and
personal property , do not exceed $ 500.00 in value , in
which case the fiduciary , within one month after his
appointment , shall be required to post three written
notices in prominent places , including the courthouse
of the county of his appointment , setting forth the
fact of his appointment and notifying creditors of the
decedent to present their claims to him by a desig-
nated date , which shall be three months after the date
on which the notices are posted .
§ 2. Sections 386.080 , 395.200 , 395.210 and 396.120
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , relating to sales of
personal property of decedents , are repealed , and in

CHAPTER 115 213
lieu thereof is enacted the following : to be numbered
KRS 395.200 .
Sale of Personal Property . ( 1 ) At any time after
the appointment of a fiduciary , whenever it shall be
necessary to secure authority to sell personal proper-
ty belonging to an estate , the county court , when
satisfied that it would be for the best interests of the
estate , may authorize such fiduciary to sell , at public
or private sale , at a fixed price or for the best price
obtainable , and for cash or on such terms as the court
may determine , any part or all of the personal proper-
ty belonging to the estate , including dividend -paying
and interest -bearing securities , except :
(a ) Such tangible personal property as has been
set apart to the surviving spouse as exempt .
(b) Property specifically bequeathed , when sale
of such property is not necessary for the payment of
debts , provided that such property may be sold with
the consent of the person entitled thereto .
(c ) Property as to which distribution in kind
has been demanded prior to the sale by the surviving
spouse or other beneficiary entitled to such distribu-
tion in kind , or property which the court may deter-
mine to be suitable for distribution in kind .
(d) Property , the sale of which is contrary to the
wish of the testator, as shown by his will , unless the
court finds that the sale is necessary to pay debts ,
funeral and burial expense , and costs of administra-
tion , or that the retention of it will probably cause
great loss to the estate .
( 2 ) If the court directs any of such personal
property to be sold at public sale , the court shall
direct the nature and extent of the advertising of such
sale . Nothing herein shall limit or qualify the powers
granted by will to executors and trustees .
§ 3. Section 387.280 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :

214 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Amounts of $ 100.00 or Less Due to Persons under
Disability . Whenever a person under disability
having no guardian or committee is entitled to receive
a sum not exceeding $ 100.00 , exclusive of interest , in
any action wherein real estate has been sold or in the
settlement of any estate or from any other source , the
court in which the action is pending , or , if the sum
does not derive from such action , the county court ,
may order the sum to be paid to the person having
custody of such ward . Before entering the order , the
court shall be satisfied by affidavit or oral testimony
that the ward is in the custody of the person to whom
it is proposed to pay the money , and the latter , upon
withdrawal of the money , shall be under obligation
as trustee to expend it for the support , maintenance
or education of the ward . When the order is made
no bond shall be required of the person having cus-
tody of the ward and the purchaser of the real prop-
erty may pay the share into court , and no lien shall
remain on the property therefor and the money may
be withdrawn by the person mentioned in the order
without his giving bond . A release executed by the
person to whom the court has ordered the sum paid
shall have the same effect as a release by a duly ap-
pointed guardian or committee .
§ 4. Section 395.001 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Definition of Fiduciary . The term " fiduciary ” as
used in Kentucky Revised Statutes 25.175 , 25.195 ,
25.200 , 25.205 , 395.005 , 395.105 , 395.145 , 395.200 ,
395.255 and 395.990 means any person , association , or
corporation ( other than assignee or trustee for an
insolvent debtor or a guardian under the Uniform
Veterans ' Guardianship Act ) appointed by, or under
the control of, or accountable to , the county court ,
including executors , administrators , administrators
with the will annexed , testamentary trustees , cura-
tors, guardians and committees .
Passed February 28 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 117
(H. B. 194 )
AN , ACT to amend Section 132.630 Kentucky Revised Statutes
1942 ( 1st Edition ) and especially Section 2 thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. That Section 132.630 Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes 1942 ( 1st Edition ) and especially Section 2 there-
of be amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The county tax commissioner in counties con-
taining a city of the first class shall annually , in Jan-
uary , report to the Department of Finance under oath
the amount received by him on account of his official
duties or position from all sources during the pre-
ceding year , and the amount paid out by him for dep-
uties or assistants , giving the amounts paid to each
and for expenses of his office . If it appears from this
statement that he received as compensation on ac-
count of his office , from all sources , more than five
thousand dollars after the payment of his deputies
and assistants and all the expenses of his office , he
shall , with such statement , pay to the Department of
Finance the amount so received in excess of five thou-
sand dollars .
( 2 ) The annual salary of the deputy county tax
commissioners , appraisers , transfer clerk and drafts-

220 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
men shall be fixed by the County Judge by an order
entered of record . The salary of the Chief Deputy
shall not exceed three thousand three hundred dollars
per annum , that of the first three deputies shall not
exceed two thousand seven hundred and fifty dollars
per annum , that of six appraisers shall not exceed
two thousand five hundred dollars per annum , that of
the other deputies and draftsmen shall not exceed
two thousand two hundred dollars per annum , and
that of the transfer clerk shall not exceed one thou-
sand two hundred dollars per annum . The total
amount allowed for salaries for the county tax com-
missioner , his deputies and appraisers , draftsmen
and transfer clerk , and all other expenses of the office
including transportation expense for himself and
deputies , shall not exceed in the aggregate the com-
pensation allowed by KRS 132.590 . A copy of the
order of the County Judge fixing the salaries , as
well as a copy of any change made therein, shall be
filed with the Department of Revenue by the County
Clerk , when made .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 118
(H. B. 199 )
AN ACT relating to the operation of games of chance and gamb-
ling devices at the State Fair .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. It shall be unlawful for any person to open ,
cause to be opened , carry on , conduct or operate as
owner , proprietor or employe any gambling device or
game of chance within the confines of the grounds of
the Kentucky State Fair. Any person who violates
any provision of this section shall be fined not less
than fifty dollars nor more than five hundred dollars

CHAPTER 119 221
for each offense . Each day in which a gambling de-
vice or game of chance is so operated shall be con-
sidered a separate offense .
§ 2. Section 247.160 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The State Fair Board shall have exclusive
control of concessions , exhibitions , shows , entertain-
ments and attractions at any place on the State Fair
Grounds , but the board shall not allow the operation
of any gambling device or game of chance therein .
It may delegate such control to any of its employes
or agents , or to an executive committee .
(2) The State Fair Board shall have a prior lien
upon the property of any concessionaire , exhibitor or
person , immediately upon its coming or being brought
on the grounds , to secure existing or future indebted-
ness .
( 3 ) Any designated employe or agent of the
State Fair Board may sell the property to satisfy the
indebtedness , after giving ten days ' notice to the
owner or agent of the owner or , if notice cannot be
given to the owner , after a notice is posted for ten
days in the office of the State Fair Board on the
grounds, announcing that the property is to be sold .
The State Fair Board , through its designated agent ,
may bid and buy in the property offered for sale for
the use and benefit of the State Fair.
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 119
(H. B. 202 )
An ACT relating to individuals exempt from poll tax .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 142.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :

222 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) All citizens of this state who are sixty-five
years of age or older are exempt from all poll taxes .
(2 ) Any honorably discharged soldier, sailor or
marine who has served ninety days or more in
the military or naval forces of the United States , who
was a resident of this state at the time of his induction
into service , and who is totally disabled as evidenced
by pension certificate or award of compensation , is
exempt from all poll taxes .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 120
(H. B. 205 ) .
AN ACT abolishing the Division of Professional Regulation in
the Department of Business Regulation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 286.020 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes of 1942 is amended to read as follows :
The department shall have and exercise all the ad-
ministrative functions of the state except as other-
wise provided by law in relation to :
(1) The supervision of motor transportation for
hire ;
(2) The supervision of banks and kindred finan-
cial institutions ;
( 3 ) The regulation of insurance practices .
§ 2. Section 286.080 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 is repealed .
§ 3. Any appropriation made by the present Gen-
eral Assembly for the salary of the secretary and oth-
er expenses of operation of the Division of Profes-
sional Regulation , by way of assessments against the
funds of professional boards , is intended to be re-
pealed by this Act .
Passed February 29 , enrolled March 1, approved March 11 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 121 223
CHAPTER 121
(H. B. 206 )
AN ACT relating to the taxation of the shares of stock of cor-
porations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 136.030 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The individual stockholders of a corporation
shall not be required to list their shares for ad val-
orem taxation so long as the corporation pays taxes
to the State of Kentucky on at least seventy -five per-
cent of its total property , wherever located , provided ,
however , that bonds and obligations of the United
States of America and its possessions and bonds and
obligations of the State of Kentucky , its instrumen-
talities and its political subdivisions and their instru-
mentalities shall not be considered on the computa-
tion of the total property of the corporation , wher-
ever located , nor in the computation of the amount of
property upon which the corporation pays ad valorem
taxes to the State of Kentucky . In order to obtain
this exemption the stockholder must furnish satisfac-
tory proof to the Department of Revenue that at least
seventy -five percent of the total property of the cor-
poration as hereinabove specified is taxed in the State
of Kentucky .
( 2 ) Every corporation organized under the laws
of this state , or doing business in this state , whose
stock is liable to assessment and taxation under sub-
section ( 1 ) of this section , except banks , trust com-
panies , or combined banks and trust companies doing
business in this state and domestic life insurance com-
panies , shall by August 1 of each year make a true and
correct report to the Department of Revenue , verified
by its president, secretary , treasurer or other chief

224 ACTS OF THE GENERAL Assembly
officer , giving the name and addresses of residents of
this state who hold its shares of stock and outstanding
bonds as of July 1 previous thereto , and also the
transfer of any of its stocks or bonds by residents of
this state to nonresidents within thirty days previous
to July 1 .
§ 2. Whereas the present statute relating to the
taxation of corporate stock deters corporations from
investing in war bonds , and thereby impedes the war
effort , an emergency is declared to exist , and this Act
shall take effect upon its approval by the Governor .
Passed March 13 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 122
(H. B. 213 )
AN ACT relating to local health units .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In any county containing a city of the second
class the fiscal court of the county and the legislative
body of the city of the second class may , by joint ac-
tion , establish a city -county health department . The
department when established shall be governed by a
city-county board of health composed of five members ,
one of whom shall be either the mayor or city manager
of the city , whichever is appointed by the city legis-
lative body , one of whom shall be the county judge ,
and three of whom shall be physicians appointed in
like manner as physician members are appointed on
county boards of health and to hold office as provided
in KRS 212.020 .
§ 2. The expense of creating and establishing the
city -county department of health shall be paid by the
city of the second class and by the county in such pro-
portion as may be agreed upon between the city legis-

CHAPTER 122 225
lative body and fiscal court at the time of establishing
the department . After the department has been es-
tablished the annual expense of maintenance shall be
borne in the same proportion , or as may be agreed
upon between the city legislative body and fiscal
court , and the city legislative body and fiscal court
shall each make an annual levy sufficient to produce
the necessary amount .
3. The city- county board of health may appoint
a health officer , who shall serve for a term of four
years , subject to removal for cause , and may employ
such assistant health officers and other employes as
are necessary for the work of the department . The
compensation of the health officer , assistant health
officer and other employes shall be fixed by the board ,
subject to the approval of the city legislative body
and fiscal court. The health officer must have the
qualifications set forth in subsection ( 2 ) of KRS
212.180 .
4. The city -county board of health and depart-
ment of health shall have such powers , duties and
privileges as are given by law to county boards and
departments of health .
§ 5. The health officer appointed by the city - coun-
ty board of health shall have such powers and duties
as are vested by law in health officers of county de-
partments of health .
§ 6. Upon the establishment of a city -county de-
partment of health under this Act , the property of
the city board of health of the city of the second class
and the poperty of the county board or department of
health shall be transferred to the city-county depart-
ment , and the city-county board of health shall as-
sume control of any institutions formerly under the
control of the city or county board or department of
health .

226 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 7. The city -county department of health shall
have jurisdiction over the entire county , including all
cities within the county .
§ 8. Any city -county department of health estab-
lished under this Act shall be entitled to the same
state aid as is provided for county and district health
departments under KRS 212.120 , upon notice of the
establishment of the department being given to the
State Board of Health as provided in KRS 212.120 .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 123
(H. B. 218 )
AN ACT relating to child labor ; and declaring an emergency to
exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. During the duration of the National Emer-
gency which was declared to exist by proclamation of
the President of the United States , dated May 27,
1941 , and for six months thereafter , KRS 339.110
shall be suspended in so far as it prohibits the work-
ing of a person under sixteen years of age more than
eight hours on Saturday , or after six o'clock in the
evening on Saturday ; providing that such person
shall not be employed or permitted to work more than
ten hours on any one Saturday nor later than nine
o'clock in the evening on Saturday . The provisions
of this act shall apply only to retail establishments
which employ ten persons or less at any one place of
business .
Section 2. Whereas , the present labor conditions
are such in regard to the shortage of labor in the oper-
ation of business establishments , an emergency is de-
clared to exist, so therefore , this act shall be effective

CHAPTER 124 227
on its passage by both houses of the General As-
sembly and the signature of the Governor .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 124
(H. B. 219 )
AN ACT relating to Game and Fish .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 150.010 of the Ken- .
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) "Angling " means taking fish by hook and line
in hand , rod in hand , set line or trot line , but does not
include the use of grappling hooks , naked hooks ,
snatch hooks , gang hooks , gigs and nets of any kind ,
or the taking of fish where the bait or hook is not vol-
untarily taken into the mouth by the fish , or the taking
of fish by any means prohibited by KRS 150.445 .
§ 2. Section 150.170 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Except as provided in subsections ( 2 ) , ( 3)
and (4) of this section :
(a ) No person sixteen years of age or older shall
use a gun or take wild animals or wild birds , or take
fish by angling or buy the pelts of wild animals for
commercial purposes , without first having secured a
license therefor as provided in this chapter ;
(b) No person under sixteen years of age shall
take wild animals except by trapping or buy the pelts
of wild animals for commercial purposes or take wild
birds having first procured a license therefor as pro-
vided in this chapter ; and
( c ) It is the specific purpose of this chapter to
prohibit the taking of any wild life or wild animals
protected or unprotected , or the angling in any

228 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
stream or body of water , whether public or private ,
without the securing of the license provided for here-
in.
( 2 ) Landowners , their resident children and
lessees on their lands may without a license take fish
from the waters therein , by angling from the bank
or shore only , and take wild animals and wild birds
therefrom subject to the further provisions of this
chapter .
( 3 ) Nonresidents attending and participating in
any annual state or national field trial need not obtain
a hunting license .
(4) The provisions of this chapter for the protec-
tion of wild animals shall not apply to any resident
landowner of this state who kills on his land any wild
animal that is causing damage to such land or to any
personal property located thereon , if such landowner
immediately reports the killing to a conservation of-
ficer or to the county sheriff , together with proof of
the damage caused by the animal , and at the same
time delivers the body of the animal to such officer or
sheriff . Any animals delivered to a conservation of-
ficer or sheriff under this subsection shall be dis-
posed of as provided by regulations of the Division
of Game and Fish .
§ 3. Section 150.180 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Rabbits , mussels , fish other than black bass ,
channel or fiddler cats under ten inches in length ,
sturgeon or hackleback , crappie , jack salmon or wall-
eyed pike or striped bass , sand pike or sauger , and
the pelts of fur -bearing animals may be bought and
sold , if they are in legal possession .
( 2 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 4 ) and
KRS 150.290 , no wild animal , wild bird or fish , other
than those named in subsection ( 1 ) , protected by this
chapter shall be bought or sold .

CHAPTER 124 229
(3) Except as provided in subsection ( 4 ) of this
section and KRS 150.290 , no person for himself or as
agent or employe shall transport or direct to be trans-
ported any wild birds , wild animals or fish protected
by this chapter , except that a person who has been
issued a license under this chapter and has the license
in his possession may transport anywhere as part of
his personal baggage a quantity of wild birds , wild
animals or fish not in excess of two days ' bag limit
at the time of transporting .
(4 ) This section shall not in any way interfere
with the buying, selling and transporting of the skins

Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as fol-

( 2 ) No person shall take fish or attempt to take

12. A new section of the statutes is created to

150.445 ( 1 ) No person shall take fish by the use

( 2 ) No person shall use , in taking fish , any line

§ 13. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 150.450 of the

(1 ) Minnows and crawfish to be used for bait for

nows in KRS 150.010 shall be immediately returned

14. Section 150.460 is amended to read as fol-

public waters any substance that injures , interferes
with , or causes the water to be unfit for the support

( 2 ) No person shall willfully and knowingly kill ,

of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are amended to

CHAPTER 125
(H. B. 222 )
AN ACT relating to police and fire departments for cities of the
fourth class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

238 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Subsection (1 ) of section 95.710 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) To be eligible for appointment as a member
of the police force or fire department of a city of the
fourth class a person must be a citizen of the state , a
resident of the county for at least six months , at least
twenty-four years of age , able to read and write the
English language intelligibly , and sober , moral and
sagacious . No person who has been convicted of a
felony shall be eligible for appointment . After ap-
pointment no member shall interfere in any election .
If it is possible to secure such a person , the appointee
shall have been a resident of the city for at least six
months in addition to the other required qualifica-
tions ; but should the city legislative body determine
a person meeting this added qualification cannot be
secured , a residence of six months in the county shall
be sufficient .
Passed and enrolled March 6 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 126
(H. B. 227)
AN ACT appropriating money from the General Fund for the
fiscal year ending June 30 , 1944 , supplementing an appropria-
tion to the Department of Agriculture , for Bang's Disease and
Bovine Tuberculosis , made by the General Assembly for such
fiscal year .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the Gen-
eral Fund for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1944 , to
the Department of Agriculture , for the payment of
indemnity for Bang's Disease and Bovine Tuber-
culosis and the ordinary recurring expenses involved
in paying such indemnity, to supplement an appro-
priation made by the 1942 General Assembly , the sum
of Five Thousand , Seven Hundred Dollars .

CHAPTER 128 239
§ 2. WHEREAS , the amount appropriated by the
1942 General Assembly for the purpose of paying in-
demnity for Bang's Disease and Bovine Tuberculosis
during the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1944 , has not
been sufficient to pay all lawful claims for cattle de-
stroyed on account of such diseases , an emergency is
declared to exist and this Act shall take effect upon
its passage and approval by the Governor.
Passed March 9 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
