Section 4. Registration of Absent Voters . It
shall be the duty of such local registration official ,
upon receipt of said application from the secretary
of state to : ( a ) If the applicant is found to be regis-
tered in the proper registration book , the said regis-
tration official shall mark the registration record of

CHAPTER 134 285
the applicant " absent voter " and immediately so ad-
vise the secretary of state ; ( b ) If the applicant is
found by the local registration official not to be regis-
tered in the registration record of the voting precinct
in which the applicant declares he is a resident , upon
determining that the applicant is entitled to register
under the laws of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , the
said registration official shall register such applicant
in the registration records in his office , mark the same
" absent voter " , and the same shall constitute suf-
ficient registration under the laws of this State .
Notice of such registration shall be given the secre-
tary of state .
The local registration official shall prepare a list
of the names of all persons who have applied for
absentee ballots under federal statute or under this
Act , and whose names he has registered on the records
of his office as an absentee voter . Such list shall be
delivered by the local registration official to the pre-
cinct election officers of each precinct at the same time
other election paraphernalia is delivered to them on
the morning of the election which shall be posted by
the precinct officers in a public place in the voting pre-
cinct where it may be inspected by any voter . This
list shall be entitled " List of Applicants for Absentee
Ballots ".
Section 5. Official Ballots and Booklets . The
Secretary of State shall cause to be prepared and
printed an appropriate number of official absentee
voters ballots . Such ballots shall provide for voting
for electors of President and Vice President of the
United States , United States Senators and Repre-
sentatives in Congress . Such ballots shall be uniform
in size and style of type , and the type and paper shall
conform generally to that used for the regular official
ballots of this State in special , general and primary
elections . Such ballots shall be printed in such form

286 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
as may be appropriate for carrying out the provisions
of this Act .
Section 6. Official Envelopes and Instructions for
Voters . (a ) The Secretary of State of Kentucky
shall also cause to be prepared and printed an appro-
priate number of official envelopes for use in connec-
tion with such absentee voters ballots . Each such
envelope shall be gummed , ready for sealing . Upon

stantially the following form the following :

CHAPTER 134 287

Such oath shall constitute prima facie evidence that
the voter is qualified to vote , unless the statements
contained in such oath indicate the contrary .
(b) The secretary of state shall also cause to be
prepared and printed an appropriate number of
copies of instructions for absent voters . The secre-
tary of state shall also cause to be prepared and
printed an appropriate number of envelopes for the
use of voters in returning official ballots and en-
velopes to the secretary of state .
Section 7. Transmission of Official Absentee
Voters Ballots , Booklets , Envelopes and Instruction .
The secretary of state shall transmit to every absent
voter , whose name is furnished to the secretary of
state by the local registration official , as soon as prac-
ticable after the receipt thereof, an official absentee
voters ballot , two envelopes , voting instructions , a
booklet containing instructions and explanations as
to casting the vote .
Section 8. Preparation of Ballot by "Absent
Voters " . When the " absent voter " receives such
ballot and said two envelopes , he shall go before some

288 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
civil officer authorized by law to administer an oath
and before him make and subscribe to the affidavit
on the face of the envelope and shall , in the presence
of such officer and no one else , mark the ballot so as
to indicate the way he desires to vote , but in such
manner as to prevent the officer from knowing how
the ballot is marked or how he has voted ; he shall
then and while in the presence of the officer fold the
ballot in such way as to conceal the markings made
thereon by him, and after the ballot has been so folded ,
he will enclose it in the inner envelope , then seal said
envelope and then enclose it in the return envelope ,
then seal and write his name across the face of that
envelope , and at once mail it to the Secretary of State
of Kentucky ; provided that if such " absent voter " is
in the military or naval service of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky or the United States , or attached to or
serving with any branch of the Armed Forces , he may
do the things herein required to be done instead of
before a civil officer , before any commissioned mili-
tary or naval officer of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky or the United States .
Section 9. Ballots - Custody- Delivery to Elec-
tion Officers . When the Secretary of State receives
an " absent voter's " ballot , he shall immediately for-
ward the same to the county court clerk of the proper
county who shall at once endorse on the envelopes the
day and hour he received the same , and all such ballots
received by him before the day on which an election is
held at which said ballot is to be voted , shall be trans-
mitted by him with the inner envelope unopened to
the election officers of the precinct or ward in which
said " absent voter " is an elector at the same time
the poll books and ballots for such election are de-
livered to them , and all such ballots received by him
on or after such election day, shall be kept by him un-
opened until thirty days after such election , when the
same shall , without being opened , be burned by the

CHAPTER 134 289
clerk in the presence of the judge and sheriff of his
county .
Section 10. Ballots-When and How Voted . The
election officers of any election shall , while the polls
are open , examine such " absent voter's ballots as
have been delivered to them . If , upon such examina-
tion it is found that the inner envelope is regular
and in form and condition provided by this Act , and
has not been opened but is properly sealed and the af-
fidavit on the face thereof is regular and in the form
provided by this Act, the clerk of such election shall
read aloud the name of such " absent voter " and after
so doing , he shall then open the envelope and remove
the ballot therefrom without unfolding it , and in such
way as not to expose the markings thereon or how the
" absent voter " has voted . The clerk shall write his
name on the back of the ballot and then hand it to one
of the judges, who shall write his name on the back
of the ballot and then place the ballot in the regular
ballot box . The two envelopes shall be preserved by
the election officers and treated in the same way as the
ballot stubs , provided that when the name of the
"absent voter " is read aloud by the clerk, the vote of
such " absent voter " may be challenged and the chal-
lenge thereto may be determined and the vote ac-
cepted or rejected by the officers of the election as
though the voter were present and voting in person .
Provided however , that if such envelopes and the
affidavit on the face of the inner envelope are regular ,
and substantially comply with the provisions of this
Act , they shall be considered as showing that the " ab-
sent voter " is prima facie entitled to vote , provided
that if the voter of an " absent voter " is rejected , the
clerk shall write on the ballot the word " rejected ” .
Section 11. Election Returns . The officers of the
election shall , in their election returns state the num-
ber of ballots sent to them by the county court clerk as

290 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ballots of absent voters , the number of such ballots
voted and the number thereof rejected .
Section 12. Commissioned Officers Empowered
to Administer Oaths and Affirmation . All commis-
sionered military or naval officers of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky or the United States are hereby
authorized and empowered to administer oaths and
affirmations for the purposes specified in this Act ;
and to every such oath or affirmation administered by
them , they shall subscribe their names and their of-
ficial designations .
Section 13. False Registration Application - Af-
fidavit-Penalty . Any person who willfully and
knowingly make a false application for the purpose
of registering under the provisions of this Act , or a
false affidavit for the purpose of voting under the
provisions thereof, when the same is either sworn
to or affirmed by him , shall be guilty of a felony , and
upon conviction , shall be confined in the penitentiary
not less than one nor more than five years .
Section 14. Failure to Comply with Official Duties
-Penalty . Any registration official or any election
officer who shall willfully refuse or neglect to perform
and do any of the duties required by him by this Act ,
or who shall in any manner willfully violate or abuse
any trust or duty imposed on him by this Act , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon conviction , shall
be fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than one thousand dollars .
Section 15. Unlawful Interference with Right of
Voter-Penalty . Any person who shall by threats ,
bribery , corrupt or arbitrary measure , offer or pay-
ment of reward , threat or infliction of punishment ,
interfere with any qualified voter or elector , in the
free exercise of his right to register or vote under
the provisions of this Act , shall be guilty of a misde-

CHAPTER 135 291
meanor and upon conviction , be fined not less than
one hundred dollars nor more than one thousand
dollars .
Section 16. Jurisdiction - Prosecution . Juris-
diction of any prosecution for a violation of any pro-
vision of this Act , wherever the violation occurred ,
whether within or without this State , is hereby vested
in the circuit court of the county of the Voter's resi-
dence .
Section 17. Whereas , many of the citizens of this
state are now in foreign lands defending our nation ,
and will be deprived of the right of representation in
our government unless provision is made to enable
them to cast their votes , an emergency is declared to
exist and this Act shall take effect upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 135
(H. B. 256 )
AN ACT relating to Motor Carriers .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 281.020 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
The provisions of this chapter do not apply to :
(1 ) Motor vehicles used for the transportation of
property for hire operating exclusively within the
limits of a city or within its suburban area .
( 2 ) Motor vehicles used as a school bus and while
engaged in the transportation of students , under the
supervision and control , and at the direction , of school
authorities .
(3) Motor vehicles being used exclusively for the
transportation of property belonging to a nonprofit

292 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cooperative association or its members , where the ve-
hicle is owned or leased exclusively by the association .
(4) Motor vehicles , regardless of ownership , used
exclusively :
( a ) For the transportation of agricultural and
dairy products (including fruit , livestock , meats
fertilizer , wood , lumber , cotton , products of grove or
orchard , poultry and eggs ) , while owned by the pro-
ducer of the products ( including landlord where the
relation of landlord and tenant or landlord and crop-
per is involved ) from the farm to a market , ware-
house , dairy or mill , or from one market , warehouse ,
dairy or mill to another market , warehouse , dairy or
mill ; or
(b ) For the transportation of agricultural and
dairy products , livestock , farm machinery , feed ,
fertilizer , and other materials and supplies essential
to farm operation , from market or shipping terminal
to farm ; or
(c) For both of the purposes described in para-
graphs ( a ) and (b ) of this subsection .
(5) Motor vehicles used as a church bus and while
engaged in the transportation of persons to and from
a church or place of worship or for other religious
work under the supervision and control , and at the
direction , of church authorities .
( 6 ) Motor vehicles used exclusively in transport-
ing materials , supplies and equipment for the con-
struction , reconstruction or maintenance of any public
highway , road or street in this state .
(7) Motor vehicles used for the exclusive trans-
portation of members of the armed forces on educa-
tional or recreational trips under the supervision and
control of and under the exclusive direction of mili-
tary authorities . Such vehicles , however , shall be
insured as other vehicles under this chapter . This

CHAPTER 137 293
exclusion shall be limited to the duration of the
present National Emergency .
2. Section 281.025 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is repealed .
Passed ,March 6 , enrolled March 8 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 137
(H. B. 260 )
AN ACT relating to State Parks .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That the Division of Parks in the Department of
Conservation is hereby authorized and it shall be its
duty immediately upon the passage of this Act to open
negotiations for the purpose of accepting as a gift to
The Commonwealth of Kentucky , the Francis Berry
house in Washington County , Kentucky , in which
NANCY HANKS was living at the time of her mar-

294 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
riage to Thomas Lincoln on June 12 , 1806 and to move
and erect the same in the Lincoln Homestead Park
at Springfield , Washington County, Kentucky , so as
to preserve it to posterity .
A sum not to exceed Forty-eight Hundred
($4,800.00 ) Dollars is hereby appropriated for the
purpose of removing , erecting and furnishing the
said house in said Lincoln Homestead State Park .
The said sum is to be paid out of the State Treasury
to the Division of Parks , over and above its regular
appropriation .
Passed March 1, enrolled March 8 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 138
(H. B. 262 )
AN ACT relating to airports in counties containing a city of the
first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 183.140 is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) In each county in which there is a city of the
first class there shall be a bi -partisan board composed.
of six members , known as the (name of city ) and
(name of county ) Air Board . The Board shall be a
body politic and corporate with the usual corporate
attributes , and in its corporate name may sue and be
sued , contract and be contracted with , and do all
things reasonable or necessary to effectively carry
out the work contemplated by KRS 183.140 to 183.210 .
§ 2. Section 183.150 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The purpose of the board shall be to establish and
maintain one or more airports and facilities to pro-
vide for transportation by air of passengers , prop-

CHAPTER 139 295
erty , express and mail , and to provide one or more
airports and facilities for the use of the Federal Gov-
ernment . The board shall have such powers as may
be necessary or desirable to promote aviation and the
development of facilities for air travel and trans-
portation .
Passed March 13 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 18 , 1944 .

(H. B. 267 )

Kentucky Revised Statutes , as officially adopted in 1942 , pro-

That Sub-section 2 of Section 26.330 of the Ken-

296 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 140
(H. B. 272 )
AN ACT relating to the duties and compensation of the County
Tax Commissioners , repealing, amending and re -enacting sub-
sections 2 , 3, 4, and 5 of Section 132.590 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
1. Subsections 2 , 3 , 4 and 5 of Section 132.590 of
Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed , amended
and re - enacted to read as follows :
(2 ) The compensation of the county tax commis-
sioner shall be computed on the basis of ten cents on
the one hundred dollars of the first million dollars
and two cents on each one hundred dollars of the
excess over one million dollars .
(4 ) No county tax commissioner shall receive for
his personal services more than five thousand dollars
during any year.
( 5 ) In counties in which the assessed value of
property exceeds fifteen million dollars , the county
tax commissioner shall be allowed by the state as com-
pensation to his deputies the sum of one thousand five
hundred dollars for each seven and one -half million
dollars of the property assessed in excess of fifteen
million dollars , but the total sum allowed to any
county tax commissioner as deputy hire shall not
exceed seven thousand five hundred dollars in any
county except in a county having a city of the first
class , which shall be governed by KRS 132.630 . In
any county assessing more than one hundred million
dollars in taxable property , the county shall allow and
pay to the county tax commissioner from its funds ,
for assistants , three thousand dollars . In any county
assessing more than thirty -five million dollars in tax-
able property , the county shall allow and pay to the

CHAPTER 141 297
county tax commissioner from its funds , for assist-
ants , one thousand five hundred dollars .
Passed and enrolled March 14 , approved March 18 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 141
(H. B. 274 )
AN ACT relating to water districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section I.-Chapter 74 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , being Sections 74.010 to 74.990 , both inclu-
sive , of Kentucky Revised Statutes , be amended and
supplemented as follows :
Any water district , created in the manner provided
in KRS 74.010 to 74.070 , both inclusive , may if the
commissioners of such water district deem it feasible ,
build , or acquire or enlarge a water system without
resort to the right to levy assessments for the cost of
such water system , as is provided in KRS 74.130 to
74.250 , both inclusive , and may obtain the funds with
which to build , acquire or enlarge such system by
the issuance of revenue bonds , payable solely from
the revenue to be derived from the operation of such
system .
In the event the commissioners shall decide to fi-
nance the cost of such construction , acquisition or
enlargement by the issuance of revenue bonds , se-
cured solely by the revenue of the system , the com-
mission shall note such decision by appropriate reso-
lution , and shall thereafter proceed under the
provisions of KRS 96.350 to 96.510 , both inclusive ,
and the water district and the commission shall have
the same powers and duties as a city of the second
to sixth class inclusive under the provisions of KRS
96.350 to 96.510 , both inclusive .

298 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
In the event such procedure is followed the com-
mission shall not observe the provisions of KRS
96.350 to 96.510 , both inclusive .
Section II .- If any district has previously issued
bonds secured entirely or partially by the right to
levy an assessment as provided by KRS 74.130 to
74.230 , both inclusive , and such bonds are redeemed
prior to maturity , bonds to refund same may be issued
secured solely by the revenue of said system in the
manner provided hereinabove .
Section III.- This amendment shall not repeal nor
reduce any existing rights or duties of a water dis-
trict , and the commissioners thereof, but shall con-
stitute an additional and alternate method of financ-
ing .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 18 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 142
(H. B. 276 )
AN ACT to enable any city to establish , maintain and use a
Public Works Cumulative Reserve Fund .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Any city is hereby authorized , through
its legislative body , to establish and maintain a Public
Works Cumulative Reserve Fund to provide money
for paying all or part of the cost of a long range pro-
gram of municipal public works ; into which fund may
be paid, transferred or credited any money raised by
taxes specified to be for the use and benefit of such
fund , together with any other money whether arising
from taxes , loans , gifts , grants , or otherwise , as to
which the legislative body of the city has or may here-
after have authority to and does order to be paid ,
transferred or credited to said fund .
Section 2. Any city , which pursuant to Section 1
hereof establishes a Public Works Cumulative Re-

CHAPTER 142 299
serve Fund , is hereby authorized to levy or impose ,
from time to time , for the use and benefit of the said
fund , any character of revenue tax which the said city
is now, or may hereafter have authority to levy or im-
pose ; and to designate as the purpose for which any
such tax is laid that the money raised thereby shall
be paid into and be used as a part of the city's Public
Works Cumulative Reserve Fund ; or within the dis-
cretion of its legislative body , the city in levying or
imposing such tax may designate that the proceeds
thereof shall go into the Public Works Cumulative
Reserve Fund to be used for a particular public work,
naming it .
Section 3. The uses to which such a Public Works
Cumulative Reserve Fund shall be devoted are to
acquire , construct , maintain , alter , repair , or improve
any municipal Public Works .
The term " Public Works " shall refer to lands , or
buildings and structures , or either , or both , and shall
include municipal buildings wherein any govern-
mental function of the municipality , or any depart-
ment , or agency thereof, may be located and carried
on ; schools , cultural or recreational buildings , cen-
ters , structures or lands ; bridges , viaducts , over-
passes , and underpasses ; streets , roads , highways ,
sidewalks , alleys ; sewers , incinerators , garbage dis-
posal plants and other public sanitary structures or
lands ; public housing projects ; public redevelopment
or reclaimation projects ; and public utilities ;—the
foregoing enumeration is to be construed as illustra-
tive rather than exclusive , and other types of public
buildings , structures or lands which appear useful or
necessary for municipal purposes shall be included in
the term " Public Works . "
In any city which is or may be authorized to act
jointly with a county in any governmental function ,
the term " Public Works " shall include any building ,

300 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
structure or land jointly improved and used by such
city and county . Nothing in this Act shall be con-
strued to authorize any city to acquire or construct
any public work which it would lack authority , apart
from this act, to acquire or construct if it had funds
available to do so.
Section 4. The legislative body of any city, which
establishes a Public Works Cumulative Reserve Fund
pursuant to this Act , may , from time to time , desig-
nate any one or more public works , as the term is de-
fined in Section 3, for which the fund , or parts thereof,
shall be used and expended , provided that the pro-
ceeds of any tax designated under Section 2 to be used
for a particular public work shall be used for that
purpose only . The legislative body of the city shall
determine when , and in what amount , the money in
the Public Works Cumulative Reserve shall be ex-
pended , and make provisions for its withdrawal from
the fund and provide for its expenditure by the city
proper , through any of its departments , or agencies ,
or by any independent agency , board , commission ,
or authority , to which the city has , or may have ,
authority to appropriate money for capital outlay .
Section 5. All monies paid into , transferred or
credited to a Public Works Cumulative Reserve Fund
shall accumulate in such fund until expended as herein
provided, and shall not , at any time , be subject to
lapse into any other fund or become a surplus avail-
able for, or which may be used for , any other purpose
than that herein specified .
Section 6. All money in any Public Works Cumu-
lative Reserve Fund , prior to the time of its expendi-
ture for some public works , may be invested by the
city under the direction and authority of such city
official , or board , as is designated by the city's legisla-
tive body to see to its investment . Such fund may be

CHAPTER 143 301
invested only in bonds and direct obligations of the
United States Government and the interest and in-
come from such investment shall be a part of the fund .
The nature of such investments shall be such as to
facilitate the realization of the cash proceeds thereof
as and when directed to be expended for public works ,
and so far as practical , maturity dates shall conform
to the expected date of utilization of the funds
invested .
Section 7. Whereas , at the conclusion of the War
there will be an economic dislocation created by the
termination of war contracts and the delay incident to
the conversion of industry to peace-time activities
which will result in widespread unemployment , dur-
ing which period the several cities can aid greatly in
taking up the slack of otherwise idle manpower on a
program of municipal public works , provided they
have funds then available , and it being imperative
that this act take effect immediately so that the cities
may commence to accumulate funds for such purposes ,
an emergency is declared to exist and this act shall
take effect upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 143
(H. B. 277 )
AN ACT relating to crimes and punishments .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 435.090 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Any person who unlawfully carnally knows a
female of and above twelve years of age against her
will or consent , or by force or while she is insensible ,

302 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall be punished by death , or by confinement in the
penitentiary for life without privilege of parole , or by
confinement in the penitentiary for life , or by confine-
ment in the pententiary for not less than ten years nor
more than twenty years .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 17 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 144
(H. B. 279 )
AN ACT to repeal , amend and reenact Section 199a - 11 , Supple-
ment , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1936 Revision ,
being Chapter 150 of the Acts of the 1938 General Assembly
and providing for the payment of funeral or burial benefits
in money ; providing further that any person regularly licensed
to engage in the undertaking business in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky may, upon complying with the provisions of this
Act, qualify to perform the provisions of any policy or contract
of insurance issued by any burial association ; providing for
the adoption of appropriate forms for the filing of a bond and
for annual reports by the Commissioner of Insurance ; provid-
ing that the designation of a particular undertaker or under-
taking concern or an undertaker or undertaking concern out
of a particular group by a policy of burial insurance shall be
unlawful ; providing that policies of burial associations shall
be non-transferable and non - assignable ; providing that this
Act shall not be applicable to policies of burial insurance in
existence at the time of the effective date of this Act and de-
claring the public policy of. the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
It shall be unlawful for any burial association or
any association engaged in the business of issuing con-
tracts , policies or agreements of burial insurance of
any kind or character whatsoever or any contracts of
a similar nature to issue or cause to be issued any
agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or guar-
antee or to have any by-laws which shall provide for
the payment of any funeral or burial benefits in mer-

CHAPTER 144 303
chandise or services , but all payments of benefits
thereunder must be made in money .
Any person , firm , corporation , partnership or un-
dertaking concern , duly and regularly licensed to en-
gage in the undertaking business in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky who may desire to perform the
provisions of any agreement , policy , contract , bond ,
assurance or guarantee issued by any burial associa-
tion or association of a similar nature authorized to do
business in the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall file
with the Commissioner of Insurance of the Common- ·
wealth of Kentucky a bond in an amount that is rea-
sonable to be fixed by the Commissioner of Insurance
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , which bond shall
be conditioned upon the faithful performance of the
agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or guar-
antee issued by any burial association or association
of a similar nature authorized to do business in the
Commonwealth of Kentucky to its members as ap-
proved by the Commissioner of Insurance of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
The Commissioner of Insurance shall adopt appro-
priate forms for the filing of the bond provided for
herein and shall, upon request , furnish said forms to
any person , firm , corporation , partnership or under-
taking concern , duly and regularly licensed to engage
in the undertaking business in the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , who may desire to qualify under the terms
of this Act .
From and after the effective date of this Act it shall
be unlawful for any agreement , policy , contract , bond ,
assurance , or guarantee or by-law of any burial as-
sociation or association of a similar nature to provide
for the payment of benefits or proceeds of any such
agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or guar-
antee , or any part thereof, to any designated or par-
ticular undertaker or undertaking concern or to any
particular or designated tradesman or business man

304 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or to any undertaker , undertaking concern , tradesman
or business man out of any particular or designated
group of undertakers , undertaking concerns , trades-
men or business men ; but any undertaker or under-
taking concern authorized to do business in this State
and who qualifies under the terms of this Act may
upon request of the personal representative or family
of a deceased member , or any other person having
charge of the funeral of any deceased member , per-
form the provisions of the agreement , policy , con-
tract , bond , assurance or guarantee and upon such
performance shall be entitled to receive the benefits
and proceeds provided for therein .
No agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or
guarantee of any burial association or association of
a similar nature authorized to do business in the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky shall be transferable or as-
signable nor shall the benefit or any part thereof be
transferrable or assignable .
It is hereby declared to be the public policy of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky that the designation of
particular undertakers or undertaking concerns or
groups of particular undertakers or undertaking con-
cerns by contracts of burial insurance constitutes un-
reasonable restraint of trade between undertakers
and is contrary to the public policy of the Common-
wealth .
The provisions of this Act shal not be applicable to
any agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or
guarantee in existence at the time of the effective date
of this Act .
The provisions of this Act are severable and if any
of its provisions shall be held unconstitutional the
decisions of the courts shall not affect or impair any
of the remaining provisions of this Act . It is hereby
declared as the legislative intent that this Act would
have been adopted had not such unconstitutional pro-
visions been included therein .

CHAPTER 145 305
All laws or parts of laws in conflict with this Act are
hereby repealed .
Passed March 3 , enrolled March 7 , approved March 24 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 145
(H. B. 281 )
AN ACT amending and re - enacting Section 431.240 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes relating to condemned criminals ; and
declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 431.240 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes of 1942 is amended and re -enacted , so that when
amended said statute shall provide as follows :
(1 ) Unless the execution is stayed by due process
of law or under authority of subsection ( 2 ) of this
section , the superintendent of the penitentiary or his
deputy shall proceed , on the day and at the place
named in the judgment of conviction , to cause the
condemned person to be electrocuted . The execution
shall take place before sunrise on the day designated
in the judgment .
( 2 ) If the condemned person is insane or pregnant
with child on the day designated for the execution , the
execution shall be suspended until the condemned is
restored to sanity or is delivered of child . The exe-
cution shall then take place under the warrant of the
Governor and at the time designated by him , unless
stayed by due process of law. If execution is sus-
pended on the ground of insanity , the Commissioner
of Welfare may , after an examination as provided in
Kentucky Revised Statutes , 202.380 , transfer the con-
demned person to one of the state institutions for the
treatment of the mentally afflicted , until such time as
he is restored to sanity.

306 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(3 ) If the condemned person escapes from custody
and is recaptured after the expiration of the date fixed
for the execution , the Governor , upon receiving writ-
ten notice of the recapture from the superintendent
of the penitentiary , shall send his warrant of execu-
tion to the superintendent by special messenger and
shall name therein the day of execution . The super-
intendent shall then proceed to the execution thereof
according to the provisions of KRS 431.220 to 431.270 .
Whereas , there are condemned prisoners now con-
fined in the state penitentiary for whose benefit it is
essential that this Act become applicable , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall take effect
upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed March 8 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 147
(H. B. 284 )
A BILL to provide for the supervision , regulation and control of
the intrastate transportation of persons and property by air-
craft for hire , to promote safety in the operation of said air-
craft , to preserve , foster, classify and coordinate intrastate

CHAPTER 147 307
aircraft transportation facilities , to insure adequate intrastate
aircraft transportation services , to give the Kentucky Aero-
nautics Commission jurisdiction and authority to fix , alter,
regulate and determine rates , fares , charges and practices of
intrastate aircraft common carriers and charter operators for
such purposes , and to require the observance thereof , to pre-
scribe the powers and duties of said Commission with refer-
ence thereto , to provide for appeals from the orders of said
Board , and to provide for the enforcement of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Definitions . ( a ) The term " common
carrier ' , when used in this Act , shall include all car-
riers for hire or compensation by air who operate , or
seek to operate , over fixed routes or between fixed ter-
mini within the State of Kentucky .
(b) The word " person " , when used in this Act ,
shall mean and include any individual , co - partner-
ship , association , corporation or other form of organ-
ization and their lessee , trustees , or receivers , ap-
pointed by any Court .
( c) The term " character operator " shall include
all persons not included in the definition of the term
common carrier , and specifically those persons who
transport persons or property in intrastate com-
merce for hire on unscheduled service and not be-
tween fixed points .
9(d ) The term " Commission " , when used in this
Act , shall mean and refer to the Kentucky Aeronautics
Commission .
(e) The term " Civil Aeronautics Authority " shall
mean that authority or board of the United States
Government empowered to regulate interestate com-
merce by aircraft .
Section 2. General Purposes of Act . It is hereby
declared to be the purposes and policy of the legisla-
ture in enacting this law to confer upon the Commis-

308 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sion the power and authority to make it its duty to
supervise and regulate the transportation of persons
and property for hire by aircraft operating in intra-
state commerce thru the air between termini located
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Section 3. Certificate of Public Convenience and
Necessity: Determination of Board . Any person seek-
ing to engage in the operation of a common carrier or
charter operator in intrastate commerce of persons .
or property by aircraft , shall first obtain a certificate
of convenience and necessity from the Commission in
accordance with the provisions of this Act . All cer-
tificates issued to common carriers shall automatical-
ly grant to such common carriers the right to conduct
charter operations that originate at a point on said
carrier's route to any other point in the State , and
return .
Any such certificate so issued shall be null and void
unless the person to whom said certificate is granted
shall, in good faith , begin and continue to operate air-
crafts under the right granted by same . Provided ,
however , if it appears from the application that the
applicant is engaged as a common carrier , as defined
in this Act , in the transportation of persons , property
or mail , and is operating on regular schedules over
the route or routes served at the time of the effective
date of this Act , the Commission shall forthwith issue
to said applicant a certificate of public convenience
and necessity authorizing said applicant to engage in
transportation by air of persons or property or both ,
in intra - state commerce between all of the points in
the state of Kentucky from which the applicant is
operating at the time of the effective date of this Act .
In the event there are more than one common carrier
operating over the same route or routes at the time of
the effective date of this Act , the certificate of public
convenience and necessity shall be granted by the

CHAPTER 147 309
Commission to the first common carrier which oper-
ated regular schedules over the route or routes in
question and which continues to operate regular
schedules until the date this Act becomes effective .
If it appears from the application that the appli-
cant is engaged as a common carrier by air for the
transportation of persons , property or mail pursuant
to a certificate or certificates of public convenience
and necessity issued by the Civil Aeronautics Author-
ity , the Civil Aeronautics Board , or any other Federal
Agency authorized to issue such certificates and said
certificate was issued before the effective date of this
Act , and that the applicant at said time is operating
between two or more points in the State of Kentucky
pursuant to the provisions of its said certificate or
certificates of public convenience and necessity , the
commission shall forthwith issue to applicant an in-
tra -state certificate of public convenience and neces-
sity authorizing the applicant to engage in transpor-
tation by air of persons or property or both , in intra-
state commerce between all of the points in the State
of Kentucky between which the applicant is author-
ized to operate under its said certificate or certificates
of public convenience and necessity issued by the Civil
Aeronautics Authority , the Civil Aeronautics Board ,
or any other such Federal Agency , before the effective
date of this Act .
Section 4. Same : Application , Contents , Fees ,
Insurance . Application for such certificate shall be
in writing , and shall contain such information as the
Commission may require . All applications for a cer-
tificate shall be in the form prescribed by the Commis-
sion by general rule and shall be accompanied by a
filing fee of $ 25.00 , proof of insurance and all other
things required by law and the rules and regulations
of the Commission . The Commission is empowered
to make enough charges annually for the use of air

310 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
routes in the State of Kentucky to pay the expenses
of said Commission for services rendered under this
Act , not to exceed , however , 2% of the gross receipts
from intra -state business of any common carrier op-
erating hereunder , and all of such charges in any one
year under this Act shall not exceed $25,000.00 .
Section 5. Same : Hearing , Notice to Interested
Parties , Right to Appear : Proceedings . Upon the
filing of an application for a certificate of public con-
venience and necessity to operate as a common car-
rier or charter operator in intrastate commerce , the
Commission shall fix a time and place for a hearing
thereon and shall cause notice of the filing of such ap-
plication and of the hearing thereon to be given by
mail not less than thirty ( 30 ) days , exclusive of the
date of mailing , before such hearing , addressed to
all holders of certificates and applicants therefor
whose operations , or proposed operations would be
effected by the granting of the proposed certificate ,
and upon any other person deemed by the Commis-
sion to have an interest in such proceeding , and said
notice shall likewise be given to the Mayors of all
Cities on said route . All interested persons shall
have the right to appear and take part in all proceed-
ings before the commission either in person or by
counsel . Hearings on such applications shall be con-
ducted in accordance with the rules of evidence and
law applicable to the trial of equity cases .
Section 6. Same : Determination by Commission ,
Orders of Commission; Rehearing . The Commission
is hereby vested with power and authority and it is
hereby made its duty upon the filing of an applica-
tion for a certificate of public convenience and neces-
sity to hear and determine under such reasonable
rules and regulations as it may promulgate whether
public convenience and necessity require the proposed

CHAPTER 147 311
service , no such hearings shall be had until thirty
(30 ) days after rules and regulations made and prom-
ulgated have been published and filed with the Sec-
retary of State of Kentucky . The Commission may
issue such certificate granting the application in whole
or in part and may impose such terms and conditions
as it may deem proper in the public interest . The
applicant for such certificate shall be entitled thereto
only upon showing by competent evidence that the
public convenience and necessity requires the pro-
posed service or operation . The Commission shall
receive and consider evidence with respect to other
forms of transportation , or the service , or facilities
thereof , in determining whether public convenience
and necessity requires the proposed service or opera-
tion . All certificates issued by the Commission shall
continue in force and effect until surrendered or re-
voked for cause . No certificate may be transferred
except upon proper application and hearing and upon
a finding by the Commission that such transfer is con-
sistent with the public interest . No certificate shall
confer any proprietary , property , or exclusive rights
in the use of any air space , airport , or other air -navi-
gation facility .
No certificate shall required for the transpor-
tation of United States mail unless passengers or
freight are transported . The jurisdiction of the
Commission shall extend only to common carriers
and charter operators engaged in, or who seek to
engage in , intrastate commerce . Orders of the Board
shall be served upon all parties to said proceedings
by the Commission and shall become effective thirty
( 30 ) days after such service . Any of said parties.
to any such hearing , may upon proper petition be
granted a rehearing. If any rehearing is granted
within thirty ( 30 ) days after the issuance of an
order, then such order shall not become effective until

312 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
thirty ( 30 ) days after the determination of the Com-
mission following said rehearing proceedings .
Section 7. Rights ; Schedules , Publications and
filing ; Public Inspection ; Powers of Commission . All
common carriers subject to this Act shall before en-
gaging in business file with the Commission and keep
open to public inspection at its offices and terminals
schedules showing all rates , fares and charges for
transportation of passengers , or property between
different points on its route or routes , and also be-
tween points on its own route and on the routes of
other common carriers , when a through route and
joint rate have been established . No intrastate com-
mon aircraft carrier shall accept or receive any
person or property for transportation until the re-
quirements of this Act have been complied with and
no such carrier shall charge , demand , collect , or re-
ceive , a greater or less or different remuneration for
such transportation , or for any service in connection
therewith than the rates , fares and charges which
have been legally established and filed with the Com-
mission ; nor shall any such carrier refund or remit
in any manner or by any device , any portion of the
rates , fares and charges so established when service
is rendered in accordance with published tariffs . The
Commission is vested with the power and authority
to supervise , regulate and fix, alter and determine
just , fair , reasonable and sufficient rates , fares ,
charges and classifications . No order of the Commis-
sion effecting rates , fares and charges shall issue
except after notice and hearing on the same and any
interested party may appear and take part in any
such proceedings .
Section 8. (a ) The Commission is empowered
to perform such acts , to conduct such investigations ,
to issue and amend such orders , and to make and
amend such general or specific rules , regulations and

CHAPTER 147 313
·
procedure pursuant to and consistent with the pro-
visions of this Act as it shall deem necessary to carry
out such provisions and to exercise and perform its
powers and duties under this Act , provided that such
rules and regulations shall not be inconsistent with
the rules and regulations of the Civil Aeronautics
Authority . All rules and regulations made by said
commission under this Act shall be filed with the
Secretary of State of Kentucky .
(b) The Commission is empowered to confer
with or to hold joint hearings with any other state
or Federal agency in connection with any matter
arising under this act , and to avail itself of the co-
operation , services , records and facilities of such
agencies , State or Federal , as fully as may be prac-
ticable in the administration and enforcement of
this chapter .
(c) No certificate shall be issued until there has
been filed with the Commission a good and sufficient
indemnity bond or insurance policy issued by some
surety or insurance company or other insurance
carrier duly authorized to transact business as such
within this State , which shall provide such terms ,
conditions and provisions and in such penal sums in
such a maximum amount , if any , as the Commission
may deem necessary for the reasonable protection.
of the patrons and of the public in the collection of
damages for which the carrier may be liable by
reason of the operation under such certificate , carrier
may be liable by reason of the operation under such
certificate , provided that the requirements of the
Commission shall not exceed those of the Civil
Aeronautics Authority .
(d ) The Commission shall , beginning in 1946 ,
publish from year to year maps showing the air routes
and areas of all airlines and air areas in use at said
time in Kentucky , and for which applications are

314 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY

as it may see
fitpertaining

Section 12. Every person coming within the
provisions of this Act and every officer , agent or
employe of any holder of any certificate issued under
this Act and every other person who violates or
causes , aids or abets any violation of this Act , or who
violates , causes , aids or abets any violation of any
order, rule or regulation lawfully issued pursuant
to the authority granted by this Act shall , upon con-
viction , be punished by a fine of not more than five
hundred dollars ($ 500.00 ) . The prosecution may be
had in the circuit court of any county in which the
offense or any part thereof shall have occurred .
Section 13. Appeals . (a ) The applicant or the
holder of any certificate shall have the right of
an appeal from any action of the Commission by
filing within forty days after the rendition of the
order of the Commission a petition of appeal with
the Clerk of the Franklin Circuit Court . Such peti-
tion shall state completely the grounds upon which
the review is sought , and shall assign all errors re-
lied on and shall be accompanied by a copy of the
record certified by the Commission , or an abstract
thereof , if agreed to by all parties to the hearing
before said Commission .
(b) The person appealing shall furnish copies
of the petition to each person who is a party to the
hearing before 'such Commission .
(c ) Summons shall be issued upon the petition
directing the adverse party or parties to file answer
within fifteen days after service thereof .
(d) No new or additional evidence may be in-
troduced in the Circuit Court except as to fraud or
misconduct of some person engaged in the adminis-
tration of this Act , or affecting the order , ruling or

316 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
award, but the court shall otherwise hear the case
upon the certified record or abstract thereof, and
shall dispose of the case in a summary manner , its
review being limited to determining whether or not :
( 1 ) The Commission acted without or in excess
of its power ;
(2) The order, decree or award was secured by
fraud ;
(3 ) The order , decision or award is not in con-
formity with provisions of the Act ; or
(4 ) The finding of facts in issue is not sup-
ported by any substantial evidence .
(e) The Commission and each party shall have
the right to appeal in such review proceedings . The
court shall enter judgment confirming , modifying or
setting aside the order, decision or award , or in its
discretion remanding the case to the Commission for
proceedings in conformity with direction of the court .
( f) The court may advance a judgment , and
upon a sufficient showing of facts , remand the cause
to said Commission .
(g) Any party may appeal from the decision of
the Circuit Court to the Court of Appeals of Ken-
tucky. Such appeals from the order of the Commis-
sion or to the Court of Appeals shall have precedence
over other cases pending .
Section 14. Severing Clause. If any section ,
sub- section , clause , sentence or phrase of this Act is
for any reason held to be unconstitutional or invalid ,
such decision shall not affect the validity of the re-
maining portions of this Act . The legislature hereby
declares that it would have passed this Act and each
section , sub- section , sentence , clause or phrase there-
of irrespective of the fact that any one or more of

CHAPTER 149 317
the sections , sub- sections , sentences , clauses or
phrases be declared unconstitutional .
Section 15 .

CHAPTER 148
(H. B. 289 )
AN ACT amending Section 433.400 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 , relating to Railroad property .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 433.400 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
of 1942 is amended to read as follows :
Any person , other than an employee of the rail-
road company , in the usual and necessary discharge
of his duty , who tampers with a railroad car or loco-
motive , or who shall take or remove the waste or
packing from any journal box of any railroad car
or locomotive , or who disconnects or disables the air
hose, or turns or disables the anglecock connecting
the air line on any train or car, or uncouples any
train shall upon conviction be fined not less than one
hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars ,
or imprisoned for not less than one nor more than
six months , or both .
Passed March 13 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 18 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 149
(H. B. 292 )
AN ACT amending 7.020 , 7.030 , 7.040 , and 7.060 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes of 1942 .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

318 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 1. Section 7.020 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Legislative Council shall consist of eight
Senators and eight Representatives . The eight Sena-
tors shall be appointed by the Lieutenant Governor ,
and the eight Representatives shall be appointed by
the Speaker of the House of Representatives , at each
regular session of the General Assembly . The
Majority and Minority Floor Leads in the Senate
shall each submit a list of ten names to the Lieutenant
Governor , and the Majority and Minority Floor
Leads in the House shall each submit a list of ten
names to the Speaker of the House , and the members
of the Council shall be appointed from said lists .
The lists shall be submitted at least ten days before
the close of the regular session of the General As-
sembly . The Lieutenant Governor shall be Ex-
Officio Member and Chairman of the Legislative
Council , and the Speaker of the House of Represen-
tatives shall be Ex -Officio Member and Vice - Chair-
man . The party representation of the Council shall
be in proportion generally to the relative members
of the different political parties in each house of the
General Assembly ; but in no event shall the majority
party be represented by more than five -eights of the
members appointed from the House of Representa-
tives , and by more than five eighths of the members
appointed by the Senate .
( 2 ) The appointed members shall hold office until
the next regular session of the regular Assembly, or
until their successors are appointed , except that if
any House or Senate member appointed by the
Council is not re -elected to their branch of the General
Assembly, his term of office shall automatically end
on the 15th day of November succeeding his election .
The Lieutenant Governor and Speaker of the House

CHAPTER 149 319
shall continue to be Ex- Officio members of the Council
until their successors are elected and qualified .
Section 2. Section 7.030 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 is amended to read as follows :
A vacancy of the House appointees may be filled
by the Speaker , and a vacancy of the Senate appoin-
tees may be filled by the Lieutenant Governor ,
whether or not the General Assembly is in session
and without confirmation by either House . Vacancies
arising by reason of a member of the Council not
being re -elected as provided in Sub -section ( 2 ) Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes , 7.020 , shall be filled from
the list of those persons who will constitute the
membership of the respective Houses of the General
Assembly at its next regular session , whether such
persons are newly elected , re -elected , or holdover
members . In filling vacancies , the party representa-
tion required by KRS 7.202 shall be maintained .
Section 3. Section 7.060 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 is amended to read as follows :
Each officer , board , commission or department of
the State government , including the University of
Kentucky and the State Colleges , and each officer ,
board , commission , or department of any local gov-
ernment shall make such studies for the Council as
it may require and as can be made within the limits
of its appropriation in the preparation of a pro-
gram of legislation or in regard to any matters of
state -wide public importance within the jurisdiction
of the Legislative Branch .
Section 4. Subsection ( 1 ) of section 7.040 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as
follows :
( 1 ) The Council shall meet on the second Monday
in September immediately preceding each regular
session of the General Assembly , and shall remain in
session for such period of time as may be necessary

320 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to complete the performance of its duties , except
that the session may be adjourned for such periods
of time as will not interfere with the completion of
the duties of the Council before the convening of the
General Assembly , and except that the Council shall
not remain in session for more than sixty days . Spe-
cial meetings of the Council may be held at any time
between the regular sessions of the General Assem-
bly , but the total number of days the Council may be
in session between regular sessions of the General
Assembly, including both regular and special meet-
ings , shall not exceed sixty days . Special meetings
may be called by the chairman, and upon the written
request of five members of the Council the chairman
shall call a special meeting .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 18 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 153
(H. B. 301 )
AN ACT to require the enrichment of bread and flour by the
addition of certain vitamins and minerals and to prescribe the
methods of enrichment ; and to fix penalties for violation of
same .
WHEREAS there exists a widespread deficiency of certain ip-
gredients in foods necessary to the health and well - being of
the people , it is necessary and advisable to protect so far as
may be possible the health of the people of this State against
such deficiency by providing for the addition of such necessary
ingredients to certain kinds of flour and bread . In the ac-
complishment of such purposes it is necessary and advisable
to promote uniformity in the laws applicable to both interstate
and intrastate sales of such foods , and to that end , to conform
to the definitions and standards of identity and the labeling
requirements for such foods now or hereafter promulgated by
federal authorities pursuant to the provisions of the Federal
Food , Drug and Cosmetic Act .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 153 325
SECTION 1. As used in this Act , unless the con-
text otherwise requires :
(a) The term " flour " includes and shall be
limited to the foods defined as ( 1 ) flour , white flour ,
wheat flour , plain flour , ( 2 ) bromated flour , ( 3 ) self-
rising flour , self - rising white flour , self-rising wheat
flour , and ( 4 ) phosphated flour , phosphated white
flour and phosphated wheat flour , in the definitions
and standards of identity promulgated by the Federal
Security Agency under date of May 26 , 1941 ( Volume
6, Federal Register , pages 2574 to 2582 inclusive ) , or
as they may be amended , but does not include special
flours not used for bread , roll , bun or biscuit baking ,
such as specialty cake , pancake and pastry flours .
(b) The term " enriched " , as applied to flour,
means the addition to flour of the vitamins and other
nutritional ingredients necessary to make it conform
to the definition and standard of identity of enriched
flour, or enriched bromated flour , or enriched self-
rising flour, as the case may be , as fixed and estab-
lished by order of the Federal Security Agency on
July 1 , 1943 (Vol . 8 Federal Register pages 9115-
9116 ) or as the same may be from time to time
amended or modified .
( c ) The term " bread" includes and shall be
limited to the foods commonly known and described
as white bread , white rolls and white buns .
(d ) The term " Department " means the Depart-
ment of Health .
SECTION 2. No person shall manufacture , mix ,
compound , sell or offer for sale in this State , for
human consumption , any flour unless the same is en-
riched . This prohibition shall not apply to flour sold
to bakers or other commercial secondary processors
if, prior to or simultaneously with delivery , the pur-
chaser furnishes to the seller a certificate of intent
in such form as the Department shall by regulation

326 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
prescribe certifying that such flour will be used only
in the production of flour or bread enriched to meet
the requirements of this Act or in the manufacture
of products other than as stated in the certificate .
SECTION 3. No person shall manufacture , bake ,
sell or offer for sale , in this State , for human con-
sumption , any bread unless the same contains the
vitamins and other ingredients necessary to make it
conform to the definition and standard of identity ,
then in effect , of enriched bread or enriched white
bread , as fixed and established by order of the Fed-
eral Security Agency, or other appropriate federal
agency or officer , pursuant to the provisions of the
Federal Food , Drug and Cosmetic Act . If , during
any period , no such order of any such Federal agency
or officer fixing and establishing a definition and
standard of identity for enriched bread is in effect ,
no person during any such period shall manufacture ,
bake , sell or offer for sale in this State , for human
consumption , any bread unless the flour used therein
has been enriched , or, to the extent that flour is used
therein, there is added , in the mixing of the dough ,
vitamins and other nutritional ingredients sufficient
to enrich the bread to the levels that would have ob-
tained if such flour had been enriched .
SECTION 4. No person shall sell or offer for sale ,
in this State , for human consumption , any flour or
wrapped bread meeting the requirements of Sections
2 and 3 of this Act which fails to conform to the label-
ing requirements of the Federal Food , Drug and Cos-
metic Act, and the regulations promulgated there-
under , with respect to such product when introduced
in interstate commerce .
SECTION 5. ( a ) The Department shall inforce
the provisions of this Act , and shall make , amend , or
rescind rules , regulations , and orders for the efficient
enforcement of this Act .

CHAPTER 153 327
(b) In the event of a finding by the Department
that there is an existing or imminent shortage of any
ingredient required by Sections 2 or 3 of this Act ,
and that because of such shortage the sale and dis-
tribution of flour or bread may be substantially im-
peded by the enforcement of this Act , the Department
shall issue an order , to be effective immediately upon
issuance , permitting the omission of such ingredient
from flour and bread , and , if the Department finds
it necessary or appropriate , excepting such foods
from the labeling requirements imposed by this Act ,
all until the further order of the Department . When-
ever the Department finds that such shortage no
longer exists , it shall issue an order to become effec-
tive not less than thirty ( 30 ) days after publication-
tion thereof , revoking such previous order . Undis-
posed floor stocks of flour on hand at the effective
date of such revocation order , or flour manufactured
prior to such effective date for sale in this State ,
which could be lawfully sold in this State except for
such revocation order , may be lawfully sold and dis-
posed of subsequent to the effective date of such re-
vocation order . Any such finding as to the existence
or imminence of any such shortage , or the cessation
thereof, may be made by the Department without
any hearing , on the basis of an order of , or factual
information supplied by the appropriate federal
agency or officer . In the absence of any such order
or factual information , the Department on its own
motion may , and , upon receiving the sworn statement
of any person subject to this Act that such a shortage
exists or is imminent or has ceased , shall , within
twenty ( 20 ) days thereafter , hold a public hearing
with respect thereto , at which any interested person
may present evidence , and any such finding by the
Department may be based upon the evidence so pre-
sented . The Department shall publish notice of any
hearing at least ten ( 10 ) days prior thereto .

328 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(c) All orders , rules , and regulations adopted by
the Department pursuant to this Act shall be pub-
lished in the manner hereinafter prescribed , and ,
within the limits specified by this Act , shall become
effective upon such date as the Department shall fix.
(d ) Whenever under this Act publication of any
notice , order , rule or regulation is required , such
publication shall be made at least once in at least one
daily newspaper of general circulation printed and
published in this State .
( e ) The Department is authorized to take samples
for analysis and to conduct examinations and investi-
gations for the purposes of this Act , through any
officers or employees of the Department ; and all such
officers and employees shall have authority to enter ,
at reasonable times , any factory , mill , warehouse ,
shop or establishment where flour or bread are manu-
factured , processed , packed , sold , or held , or any
vehicle being used for the transportation thereof , and
to inspect any such place or vehicle and any flour or
bread therein, and all pertinent equipment , materials ,
containers and labeling.
SECTION 6. Any person who violates any of the
provisions of this Act , or the orders , rules or regula-
lations promulgated by the Department under au-
thority thereof, shall , for each offense , be fined not
more than one hundred dollars , or imprisoned for not
more than thirty days , or both .
SECTION 7. This Act shall take effect six months
after the expiration of the National Emergency which
was declared to exist by proclamation of the Presi-
dent of the United States on May 27 , 1941 .
Passed March 14 , enrolled March 15 , approved March 18 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 154 329
CHAPTER 154
(H. B. 305 )
AN ACT relating to alcoholic beverages .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Subsection ( 9 ) of section 241.010 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is repealed .
§ 2. Subsection ( 10 ) of section 241.010 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as
follows :
( 10 ) " Commissioner ' means the Commissioner of
Alcoholic Beverage Control .
§ 3. Subsection ( 13 ) of section 241.010 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as
follows :
( 13 ) " Department " means the Department of
Alcoholic Beverage Control .
4. A new section of the statutes is created to
be numbered KRS 241.015 and to read as follows :
There is created a Department of Alcoholic Bever-
age Control , which shall constitute a statutory admin-
istrative department of the state government within
the meaning of Chapter 12 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes . The department shall consist of the Com-
missioner of Alcoholic Beverage Control and the
Alcoholic Beverage Control Board . The commis-
sioner shall head the department , shall be its
executive officer , and shall have charge of the admin-
istration of the department and perform all functions
of the department not specifically assigned to the
board . The Governor shall appoint as commissioner
a person with administrative experience in the field
of alcoholic beverage control . The commissioner shall
be appointed for a term of four years and shall re-
ceive a salary of five thousand dollars per annum .

330 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
§ 5. Section 241.020 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The department shall administer statutes re-
lating to , and regulate traffic in, alcoholic beverages ,
except that the collection of license fees and taxes
shall be administered by the Department of Revenue .
(2) A Distilled Spirits Unit , under the super-
vision of the board , shall administer the laws in rela-
tion to traffic in distilled spirits and wine .
(3 ) A Malt Beverage Unit , under the supervision
of the board, shall administer the laws in relation to
traffic in malt beverages .
§ 6. Section 241.030 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The Alcoholic Beverage Control Board shall con-
sist of the Commissioner of Alcoholic Beverage
Control and two persons appointed by the Governor ,
who shall be persons with administrative experience
in the field of alcoholic beverage control and who
shall serve for terms of four years each . One of such
persons shall serve as Administrator of the Distilled
Spirits Unit , and the other shall serve as Administra-
tor of the Malt Beverage Unit , as indicated by the
Governor in making the appointments . The com-
missioner shall be chairman of the board . The two
administrators shall each receive a salary of four
thousand five hundred dollars per annum .
$ 7. Sections 241.050 and 241.070 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes are repealed .
§ 8. Subsection ( 1 ) of section 241.080 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is repealed .
9. Section 241.100 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) No member of the board or employe of the
department shall have any interest in any premises
or business where alcoholic beverages are manufac-

CHAPTER 154 331
tured , stored or sold . Nor shall he receive any com-
mission or profit from any person applying for or
receiving any license or permit .
(2 ) No person shall be disqualified under this
section solely by reason of his membership in a club .
(3 ) If a member of the board is disqualified or
fails to give bond and take the oath of office , the
Governor shall fill the vacancy by appointment for
the period during which that member of the board
remains disqualified or fails to qualify .

qualify a person under KRS 241.100 from acting as

licensee to pay an excise tax or any part thereof, or
any penalties imposed by or under the provisions of
any statutes , ordinances or Acts of Congress relative
to taxation , or for a violation of any rules or regula-
tions of the Department of Revenue made in pur-
suance thereof .
(6 ) Revocation of any license or permit provided
in KRS 243.060 , 243.070 , 243.600 and 243.610 , or
granted under any Act of Congress relative to the
regulation of the manufacture , sale and transporta-
tion of alcoholic beverages . Any license issued under
KRS 243.020 to 243.670 must be revoked or suspended
in the case of sale of alcoholic beverages by the
licensee at a price in excess of the price set by Federal
or state regulations .

Statutes is amended to read as follows :
A state administrator or a person designated by
him may , on his own initiative or on the complaint

license under KRS 243.020 to 243.670 . Revocation
shall be had only upon five days ' notice to the licensee ,

338 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and an opportunity shall be given him to be heard .
All such hearings shall be before the board .
§ 21. Section 243.550 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Hearings upon appeals from orders of a county
administrator or a city administrator , a determina-
tion of a state administrator , or upon proceedings for
revocation or suspension shall be held by the board.
The board may , at its discretion , hold the hearing in
Frankfort , or in the county where the licensed
premises , or the premises to be licensed , are located .
The board shall control the introduction of evidence
and shall hear all arguments of the parties or their
counsel as it or he deems necessary or appropriate .
The procedure on such hearings shall be summary
and in accordance with the regulations of the board .
A record of the orders appealed from or the charges
filed , of the evidence heard and of all steps taken , shall
be kept by the board . Decisions shall be made and
orders entered only upon the vote of a majority of the
board.

Wholesalers of distilled spirits and wine may de-
duct from the amount of the tax collected under

to cover expenses incident to the reporting and re-
mitting of those taxes .
§ 23. Section 243.870 of the Kentucky Revised

(1 ) Any person or persons owning any distilled

Prohibition and upon which all Federal taxes have

tucky Consumer's Tax or any other excise taxes , and

the proprietor of any free warehouse or of any dis-

sumption and are not to be withdrawn for purposes

§ 24. Paragraph ( b ) of subsection ( 2 ) of section

§ 25. Subsection ( 2 ) of section 244.120 of the

§ 26. Section 244.150 of the Kentucky Revised

( 1 ) Each licensee under KRS 243.020 to 243.670
shall keep and maintain upon the licensed premises

volved in the manufacture or sale of alcoholic bever-
ages , in the manner required by regulations of the
board and the Department of Revenue . Such books

for inspection by the board and by the Department
of Revenue .
(2) The commissioner shall require common car-
riers to provide information in such form as he deems
wise respecting all shipments of alcoholic beverages
to , from , or between persons in Kentucky .

clusive right and power to establish the hours and

$ 28 . Subsection ( 1 ) of section 244.480 of the

31. A new section of the statutes is created to

CHAPTER 154 343
$§ 33. Section 243.230 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Licenses to sell distilled spirits and wine by
the drink for consumption on the premises may be
issued only for premises located within cities of the
first , second or third class , or elsewhere in counties
containing a city of the first , second or third class if
those counties maintain an adequate police force un-
der KRS 70.540 to 70.570 and KRS 70.150 to 70.170 .
(2 ) Licenses to sell distilled spirits or wine by
the package may be issued only for premises located
within incorporated cities , or elsewhere in counties
containing a city of the first , second or third class if
those counties maintain an adequate police force un-
der KRS 70.540 to 70.570 and KRS 70.150 to 70.170 .
(2 ) Notwithstanding subsection ( 2 ) , the board
may , after a field investigation , issue a license to sell
distilled spirits and wine by the package at premises
not located within any city if :
(a) Substantial aggregations of population would
otherwise not have reasonable access to a licensed

(b) The premises to be licensed under this subsec-

spirits and wine by the package and shall not be used
in any manner , in connection with a dance hall , road-
house , restaurant , store or any other commercial en-
terprise , except as a drug store in which a registered
pharmacist is employed ; and
(c ) The part of any premises to be licensed under
this subsection which is available to the public shall
not , except in the case of a drug store , exceed one
hundred square feet of floor space and shall not con-
tain any chairs , benches , stools or similar furniture

(4 ) No retail package or drink license for the

344 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
premises used as or in connection with the operation´
of any business in which a substantial part of the
commercial transaction consists of selling at retail
staple groceries or gasoline and lubricating oil .
§ 34. Section 243.280 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) A malt beverage Class A retail license shall

cludes any retail establishment commonly known as

(2 ) Malt beverages Class B retail licenses shall
be issued only for premises located within an incor-
porated city or elsewhere in counties containing a city
of the first , second or third class if those counties

70.150 to 70.170 and KRS 70.540 to 70.570 . Provided ,
however , that no malt beverage Class B retail license
shall be issued for any premises used as or in con-
nection with the operation of a grocery store as herein
defined .
(3 ) Notwithstanding subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) , the
board may , after a field investigation , issue licenses
for the sale of malt beverages at premises not located
within any incorporated city if :
( a) Substantial aggregations of population would
otherwise not have reasonable access to a licensed
vendor .
