CHAPTER 187
(H. R. 32 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION establishing a Youth Guidance Com-
mission ; providing for its organization and membership ; defin-
ing its duties and powers ; and providing for its report and
recommendations .
WHEREAS , this nation is now in a National Emergency , and
WHEREAS , juvenile delinquency appears to be increasing for
the state and nation , and

412 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , thoughtful and capable citizens have expressed their
belief that the problems of youth should be studied and means
sought for their solution , and
WHEREAS , it is vital to the welfare of the future generations
of our state and nation that the guidance of our youth to be
given special attention ; NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is established a Kentucky Youth Guid-
ance Commission to be composed of nine members ap-
pointed by the Governor , one from each Congressional
District of the State . At least three of the members
shall be appointed from the teaching profession , at
least three from religious and welfare workers , and
the other members shall be so selected as to obtain the
services of persons competent and interested in the
education and welfare of youth . The Governor shall
designate one member as chairman and another as
vice chairman . The commission shall elect a secre-
tary . The members of the commission shall serve
without pay and shall have the cooperation and as-
sistance of all state , county , and city officials in the
conduct of their studies .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of the Kentucky Youth
Guidance Commission to make a survey of the entire
field of youth welfare in the Commonwealth , of char-
acter education in the schools , of the character-
building organizations and of the administration and
results of present laws for the prevention of juvenile
delinquency , and to make a report of its findings to
the Governor thirty days prior to the next regular
session of the General Assembly . The commission
shall have power to summon witnesses and to compel
their attendance , and shall have such incidental
powers as may be necessary and proper in the per-
formance of its duties .
Passed March 11 , enrolled March 13 , approved March 17 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 188
(H. R. 33 )
JOINT RESOLUTION for the benefit of Mrs. Gertie Gresham ,
widow of Carlie S. Gresham , who lost his life in the service of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , Carlie S. Gresham was an attendant at the Western
State Hospital at Hopkinsville , Kentucky , and while on duty ,
on the 24th day of November , 1942 , was, without any improper
action or conduct upon his part , beaten by an inmate and sus-
tained injuries which resulted in his death on the 1st day of
December , 1942 ; and
WHEREAS , he left a widow , Mrs. Gertie Gresham , and a son six-
teen years of age ; and
WHEREAS , said Carlie S. Gresham had no estate and his said
widow and child were left without property and without suf-
ficient means for the payment of his burial expenses and with-
out any means except such salary or wages as the widow was
and may be able to earn ; Now , Therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
There is hereby appropriated from the General
Fund of the Commonwealth , the sum of one thousand
dollars ( $1,000.00 ) , and the Department of Finance
shall draw its warrant upon the Treasurer for that
amount in favor of Mrs. Gertie Gresham .
Passed March 10 , enrolled March 14 , 1944 , not approved .

CHAPTER 193
(H. R. 79 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION proposing an Amendment to the Con-
stitution of the United States relative to taxes on incomes , in-
heritances and gifts .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That application be and it hereby is made to the
Congress of the United States of America to call a

CHAPTER 193 419
convention for the purpose of proposing the following
article as an amendment to the Constitution of the
United States :
ARTICLE-
Section 1. The sixteenth article of amendment to
the Constitution of the United States is hereby re-
pealed .
Section 2. The Congress shall have power to lay
and collect taxes on incomes , from whatever source
derived , without apportionment among the several
states , and without regard to any census or enumer-
ation : Provided that in no case shall the maximum
rate of tax exceed 25 per centum .
Section 3. The maximum rate of any tax , duty , or
excise which Congress may lay and collect with re-
spect to the devolution or transfer of property , or
any interest therein , upon or in contemplation of or
intended to take effect in possession or enjoyment at
or after death , or by way of gift , shall in no case
exceed 25 per centum .
Section 4. The limitations upon the rates of said
taxes contained in Sections 2 and 3 shall , however , be
subject to the qualification that in the event of a war
in which the United States is engaged creating a grave
national emergency requiring such action to avoid na-
tional disaster, the Congress by a vote of three -fourths
of each house may for a period not exceeding one
year increase beyond the limits above prescribed the
maximum rate of any such tax upon income subse-
quently accruing or received or with respect to sub-
sequent devolution or transfers of property , with like
power, while the United States is actively engaged in
such war , to repeat such action as often as such emer-
gency may require .
Section 5. Sections 1 and 2 shall take effect at mid-
night on the 31st day of December following the ratifi-
cation of this article . Nothing contained in this arti-

420 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cle shall effect the power of the United States after
said date to collect any tax on incomes for any period
ending on or prior to said 31st day of December laid
in accordance with the terms of any law then in effect .
Section 6. Section 3 shall take effect at midnight
on the last day of the sixth month following the rati-
fication of this article . Nothing contained in this
article shall affect the power of the United States to
collect any tax on any devolution or transfer occurring
prior to the taking effect of Section 3 , laid in accord-
ance with the terms of any law then in effect .
and be it further
RESOLVED , that the Congress of the United
States be , and it hereby is , requested to provide as
the mode of ratification that said amendment shall be
valid to all intents and purposes , as part of the Con-
stitution of the United States , when ratified by the
legislatures of three -fourths of the several states ; and
be it further
RESOLVED , that the Secretary of State be , and he
hereby is , directed to send a duly certified copy of this
resolution to the Senate of the United States and one
to the House of Representatives in the Congress of
the United States .
Passed and enrolled March 15 , approved March 20 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 194
(H. R. 5 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing an allowance for postage ,
telephone , stationery , supplies and stenographic work , to each
member of the present General Assembly , and the Lieutenant-
Governor .
WHEREAS the proper representation of the citizens of this Com-
monwealth requires that the members of the General Assem-
bly keep in constant touch with the citizens of the districts
which such members represent , by correspondence and by
telephone , and

CHAPTER 194 421
WHEREAS no provision has heretofore been made to enable
members of the General Assembly to convey messages by tele-
phone and by correspondence to the citizens whom they repre-
sent or to make adequate inquiry of such citizens as to their
desires with regard to proposal legislation , and
WHEREAS to make the funds available for such purpose will
enable the performance of a public service which has not pre-
viously been performed , NOW THEREFORE
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Each member of the present General Assem-
bly , shall be entitled to the sum of three hundred dol-
lars , and the Lieutenant Governor and Speaker of
the House shall be entitled to the sum of five hundred
dollars , as an allowance for postage , telephone , sta-
tionery , supplies and stenographic work . Such allow-
ances shall be paid at the beginning of the session , and
shall be in lieu of the allowance made by KRS 6.220 .
§ 2. Such allowances shall be paid by the State
Treasury out of the General Funds , upon warrant of
the Department of Finance issued on the requisition
of the Clerks of the respective Houses .
WHEREAS it is necessary that the allowances pro-
vided for by this resolution be made forthwith in order
to properly expedite the business of the present Gen-
eral Assembly , an emergency is declared to exist and
this resolution shall be effective from and after its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed January 28 , enrolled February 1, vetoed February 9 ,
passed over veto February 9 , 1944 .

OF THE •
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE

CHAPTER 1
(S. B. 3)
AN ACT appropriating money for the operation , maintenance ,
support and functioning of the State Government of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , including each of the various
offices , departments , boards , commissions , institutions , and
subdivisions of the State Government , and the purchase of
record books as provided by Section 28.130 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes of 1942 , judiciary and court costs , Con-
federate pensions, the Frankfort Cemetery , Jefferson , Kenton
and Harlan County fees , and for defraying the expenses of
all other State obligations for ( a ) the fiscal years ending June
30, 1945 , and June 30 , 1946 , ( b ) the fiscal years of the Depart-
ment of Highways ending March 31 , 1945 , and March 31 ,
1946 ; designating the sources and funds from which said
appropriations are made , and providing for the establishment
of certain revolving funds specifically mentioned and the
investment of certain funds ; providing for money refunds ;
prescribing certain powers and duties of the Commissioner
of Finance , the Governor , the State Treasurer , and other
public officials , institutions , boards , commissions and agen-
cies with respect thereto ; authorizing and empowering the
Commissioner of Finance with the approval of the Governor
to limit, restrict and supervise expenditures of all money

424 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
appropriated herein and hereafter by the General Assembly
in the first Extraordinary Session of 1944 , and to reduce or
adjust each of the appropriations in the manner herein pro-
vided ; providing for certain appropriations to be limited to
specific purposes and barring the use of appropriations for
certain purposes ; repealing all blanket and continuing ap-
propriations not provided for in this Act and appropriations
made by any previous Act or Acts of the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and repealing all laws
or parts of laws in conflict with any of the provisions of this
Act ; enacting each section and each sub- section as a separate
or specific appropriation , and declaring an emergency to exist .
WHEREAS , the fiscal operations of the state government are so
interdependent and interrelated that appropriations for any
particular purpose or agency , or class or purposes or agencies ,
cannot be made without inevitably affecting the appropria-
tions for other agencies and purposes , by reason of the fact
that all appropriations for general governmental purposes
are payable from the same Treasury Fund ; and
WHEREAS , the interrelation and interdependence of appro-
priations are illustrated by the fact that the apppropriations
made by this Act for educational purposes and agencies
represent an appropriation of approximately sixty -five per-
cent of the revenues anticipated to be received into the
General Expenditure Fund during the fiscal years 1944-1945
and 1945-1946 , leaving only thirty - five percent of such reve-
nues for the operations of all other state functions , and such
appropriations for educational purposes and agencies , when
added to the appropriations for other agencies which would
be carried over by operation of section 45.120 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes for such fiscal years if no appropria-
tions should be made by this General Assembly for such other
agencies , would cause the total appropriations to exceed the
total revenues , with the result that the financial affairs of the
state would be placed on an unsound basis and the budgetary
system jeopardized ; and
WHEREAS , appropriations for educational purposes or agen-
cies , or for any other single purpose or agency , cannot be
made without first ascertaining and determining the bud-
getary requirements of other agencies , in order to insure the
availability of funds to meet the appropriation for such single
purpose or agency ,
NOW THEREFORE , the General Assembly hereby expressly
finds that the proclamation of the Governor calling this extra-

CHAPTER 1 425
ordinary session , for the purpose of making appropriations
for educational purposes and agencies , necessarily embraces
the adjusting and fixing of the entire budgetary plan so as
to enable the appropriations for educational purposes and
agencies to be met according to a sound fiscal policy , and the
entire fiscal operations of the state maintained with available
funds properly allocated among the various agencies and
purposes according to needs and the best welfare of the
Commonwealth , and
NOW THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
There is hereby appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund for the fiscal year beginning July
1 , 1944 , and ending June 30 , 1945 , and for the fiscal
year beginning July 1 , 1945 , and ending June 30 , 1946 ,
and the following sums for the following officers ,
departments , boards , commissions , institutions , and
subdivisions of the State Government , printing of
County Record Books , Judiciary and Court Costs ,
Confederate Pensions , Frankfort Cemetery , Jeffer-
son , Kenton and Harlan County Fees and any and all
other agencies of the State Government of the Com-

Chapters 12, 42 and 45 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes , for which the specific appropriation is herein

prove no budget or salary schedule for a local board

aries is reduced below that of the school year 1943-

for an amount which would be required to pay the
number of teachers reduced and the decline in local
revenue that would have been used for teaching serv-
ices had no decline in local revenue occurred .

ernment Bonds directed to be purchased out of the

Act shall be cashed or sold except as and for the uses

after a proclamation is issued by the President of the

shall not have been expended or encumbered during
the aforesaid biennium may be expended or encum-
bered at any time during such extended period for the
uses and purposes and subject to the restrictions and
limitations herein provided .
No part of this appropriation may be transferred
for use or used for any other uses or purposes than

propriation may not be reduced by allotment or other-

PART FOUR
1 .
All receipts , fees , and/or revenue , regardless of
whether heretofore specified in this Act , of every de-
partment , board , commission , institution , agency ,
division , or subdivision of the State Government shall
be covered into the State Treasury promptly , as pro-
vided by statute , through the Department of Finance
and placed to the credit of the General Expenditure
Fund , excepting those revenues which are specifically
allocated to the State Road Fund or the Revolving
Fund which shall be credited to their respective funds
as provided for in this Act . The fees , receipts , and/or
revenue of every officer , department , division , sub-
division , commission , board, institution , or agency
of the State Government shall be covered into the
State Treasury promptly , as provided by statute , and
placed to the credit of the General Expenditure Fund
and shall be used to defray the expenses of the State
Government and the appropriations made from the
General Expenditure Fund in this Act , unless other-
wise specifically appropriated to one of the profes-
sional boards , commissions , institutions , agencies , or
subdivisions of the State Government for Revolving

466 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Fund purposes ; such agencies shall be only those
that are listed in this Act as having been approved
for Revolving Fund Accounts .
2.
The appropriations herein made for the ordinary
recurring expenses and extraordinary expense and
capital outlay for any officer , department , board com-
mission, institution , and subdivision of the State
Government of the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall
not be available for expenditure until allotted as now
provided by law , and such appropriations as speci-
fically made in this Act are hereby declared to be
maximum and conditional appropriations except
when the Governor determines an emergency exists
in any department , board , commission , institution , or
subdivision of the State Government requiring an
additional allotment of funds to be made from the
General Emergency Fund hereby placed at his dis-
posal and made a specific appropriation to be used
by him to meet existing emergencies so determined
by him . This appropriation is designated under
section 4 " EXECUTIVE DEPARTMENT " Item
(d) 1 , Part One of this Act .
Whereas , a state of war exists in the United States ,
and the effect upon the finances of the Commonwealth
can not be ascertained at this time , and since the actual
needs of each officer , department , board, commission ,
institution , or subdivision of the State Government
can only be anticipated and not finally determined by
the Legislature , and since the total amount of revenue
accruing to the General Expenditure Fund from
which these appropriations are hereby made can only
be anticipated through existing laws and those that
may hereafter be enacted , it is the purpose and inten-
tion of this Legislature that the State expenditures
for each fiscal year covered in this Act , exclusive of
appropriations out of the State Road Fund , and ex-

CHAPTER 1 467
clusive of revolving fund accounts , shall not exceed an
amount equal to the amount of revenues collected and
received into the treasury , and credited to the General
Expenditure Fund , during the same fiscal year , and
that there is appropriated out of the General Expendi-
ture Fund for each fiscal year to meet the appropria-
tions made by this Act , only such an amount as does
not exceed the revenues actually collected and re-
ceived into the General Fund during such year ,
exclusive of any unencumbered balance existing at
the close of business of the preceding fiscal year .
However , in view of the present emergency which may
materially affect revenue collections , there is hereby
appropriated out of the unencumbered cash balance
in the General Expenditure Fund as of the close of
business on June 30 , 1944 , the sum of $3,319,050.00 ,
which shall constitute an Emergency Expenditure
Fund . The amount so appropriated shall be credited
to such Emergency Expenditure Fund by the State
Treasurer on July 1 , 1944 , and shall be held sacred and
inviolate in such fund except for use as hereinafter
provided . In the event it appears at any time during
the fiscal year 1944-1945 that the revenues that will
be received during that year will not be sufficient to
meet the appropriations made by this Act for such
year, then and in that event the Commissioner of
Finance , with the advice of the Commissioner of
Revenue and the approval of the Governor , may allot
any part or all of the Emergency Expenditure Fund ,
to the extent of such deficiency of revenue , but not to
exceed $1,659,525.00 , to the various budget units to
which appropriations have been made , such allot-
ment , in the event it shall be insufficient to meet all
appropriations in full , to be prorated on an equal
basis . If, after so using any portion or all of said
$1,659,525.00 during such fiscal year , it develops that
the revenues ultimately collected and received into the

468 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
State Treasury during such fiscal year and credited
to the General Expenditure Fund would have been
sufficient to have made unnecessary the use of part
or all of such Emergency Expenditure Fund , than an
amount equal to the amount so used from such fund
shall be replaced to the credit of the Emergency Ex-
penditure Fund , out of such revenues . During the
fiscal year 1945-1946 , such sum as stands to the credit
of the Emergency Expenditure Fund , including such
sum as may have been replaced to the credit of the
fund as above provided , shall be held sacred and
inviolate except that if it appears , at any time during
such year , that the revenues that will be received
during the year will not be sufficient to meet the ap-
propriations made by this Act for such years , the
Commissioner of Finance , with the advice of the
Commissioner of Revenue and with the approval of
the Governor , may allot any part or all of such Emer-
gency Expenditure Fund to the extent of such antici-
pated deficiency of revenues , to the various budget
units to which appropriations have been made , such
allotments to be prorated on an equal basis if insuffi-
cient to meet all appropriations in full .
It is the intention of the General Assembly that the
Emergency Expenditure Fund established under the
preceding paragraph of this Act shall be used for no
other purpose than to meet deficiences in revenue , as
set forth in said preceding paragraph , and that an
amount of cash equal to the amount appropriated to
such fund shall be earmarked for such fund as of
July 1 , 1944. However , it is intended that advance-
ments may be made out of the monies to the credit of
such fund for necessary operating capital preliminary
to meeting the general budgetary requirements of
either or both of the fiscal years 1944-1945 and 1945-
1946 , such advancements to be repaid to the fund as

CHAPTER 1 469
.
soon as incoming revenues enable it to be done .
Nothing in the preceding paragraph of this Act is
intended to apply to the appropriations for capital
outlay made in item (k ) of section 26 of Part One of
this Act .
As the conditions cannot be finally determined until
after the adjournment of this session of the Legisla-
ture, and as the needs of each officer , department ,
board , commission , institution or subdivision of the
State Government and the total revenues to be re-
ceived into the General Fund cannot be fully deter-
mined at this time it is the intention and the purpose
of this Legislature that the Commissioner of Finance ,
with the advice of the Commissioner of Revenue and
the approval of the Governor of the Commonwealth ,
be and he is hereby authorized , empowered and di-
rected to ascertain and determine the needs of each
officer , department , board , commission , institution ,
or subdivision of the State Government and to ascer-
tain the total revenues anticipated to be received into
the General Expenditure Fund of the Commonwealth ,
and upon the ascertainment and determination of
said facts , he, the Commissioner of Finance in the
manner set forth and as provided by general laws , is
hereby authorized , empowered and directed by the
Legislature to prevent expenditures in excess of the
amounts made available by this Act for the purpose
of meeting appropriations , by equitably reducing ,
according to the need , without discrimination , the ap-
propriations herein made to any officer , department ,
board , commission , institution , or subdivision of the
State Government ; provided that the power hereby
vested shall not permit any reduction of the appro-
priation of any officer , department , board , commis-
sion , institution or subdivision of the State Govern-
ment that will actually impair the necessary constitu-
tional functions of government .

470 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
3 .
To carry out the purpose of this Act for the ensuing
biennium , the Commissioner of Finance is hereby
empowered and granted the authority to make allot-
´ments and/or reallotments from appropriations made
to the various officers , departments , boards , commis .
sions , institutions , and subdivisions of the State
Government , so as to prevent an overdraft , or a deficit
in any fiscal year for which appropriations are herein
made , and to prevent the maximum appropriation
made to any officer , department , board , commission ,
institution , or subdivision of the State Government
from being exceeded .
4 .
Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any
appropriation for the fiscal year ending June 30 ,
1944 , and nothing in this Act is to be construed to
amend or alter the provisions of Chapter 42 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes pertaining to the duties
and powers of the Commissioner of Finance .
Section 47.030 to 47.100 , inclusive , of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes of 1942 , are repealed , and all con-
tinuing appropriations in existence at the time of this
Act are hereby discontinued and repealed .
WHEREAS , the first fiscal year for which ap-
propriations are made by this Act will commence on
July 1 , 1944 , and it is essential that the appropria-
tions made by this Act become effective at the begin-
ning of such fiscal year , NOW THEREFORE ,
An emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
take effect from and after its passage and approval
by the Governor , or from and after such time as this
Act becomes a law without the approval of the
Governor .
Passed and enrolled June 9 , approved June 12 , 1944 .

Acts of the General Assembly of 1936 , the sum of One
Hundred Dollars ( $ 100.00 ) per diem , effective begin-
ning with the first day of the 1944 Extraordinary
Session , for each body , for the duration of the 1944
Extraordinary Session .
Sec . 2. That the aforesaid amount shall be pro
rated among the aforesaid constitutional officers and
employees , according to the per diem set forth in
Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly of
1936 , and shall be by the Department of Finance
charged to the account of the aforenamed officers and
employees of the Senate and the House of Repre-
sentatives on this basis .
Sec . 3. That the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk
of the House of Representatives are directed to issue
vouchers to the Commissioner of Finance , who shall
draw his warrant in favor of the Clerk of the Senate
and the Clerk of the House of Representatives for
the abovementioned amounts , and that the Clerk of
the Senate and the Clerk of the House of Representa-
tives are further directed to distribute the additional
compensation to the abovementioned officers and em-
ployees as provided in Chapter 88 of the Acts of the
General Assembly of 1936 .
Whereas , it is necessary that this additional com-
pensation be received by the officers and employees
of the 1944 Extraordinary Session in order to expe-
dite properly the business of the Senate and the
House of Representatives , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this resolution shall be effective
from and after its passage and approval by the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Passed and enrolled June 1, approved June 12 , 1944.

CHAPTER 5 473
CHAPTER 4
(H. R. 2 -A)
A JOINT RESOLUTION appropriating money for the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the fiscal
year of 1943-1944 , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the
General Expenditure Fund for the payment of sala-
ries and other necessary expenses of the General As-
sembly for the fiscal year 1943-1944 the sum of One
Hundred Thousand Dollars ( $ 100,000.00 ) .
Section 2. Whereas, the funds heretofore appro-
priated for the 1944 General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky are exhausted , an emergency
is declared to exist and this resolution shall become
effective immediately upon its passage and approval
by the Governor.
Passed May 26 , enrolled May 31 , approved June 1, 1944 .
CHAPTER 5
(H. R. 9 -A )
RESOLUTION to pay Mrs. Sarah Stephens Moore the per diem
allowance of her deceased husband , the late L. D. Moore .
Whereas , the late L. D. Moore was elected to the House of
Representatives of this General Assembly at the general elec-
tion in November , 1943 , from the Whitley County legislative
district , and
Whereas , the said L. D. Moore was elected for a term of two
years and being unable to serve in a portion of the special
extraordinary necessary session which is now convened , and
Whereas , if he had been permitted to serve in the present Spe-
cial Extraordinary Session of the General Assembly he would
have received as per diem the sum of $ 10.00 for each legisla-
tive day of said Special Extraordinary Session as a member
thereof , and
Whereas , it has heretofore been the custom and precedent set
by the General Assembly to allow the widow or the depend-

474 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ents of the deceased member of the General Assembly his
per diem or salary that he would have received had he at-
tended the session , and
Whereas , the deceased spent considerable time and money in
his campaign for nomination and election as aforesaid , and
Whereas , his widow Mrs. Sarah Stephens Moore , is now obli-
gated for and will be compelled to pay certain debts and
obligations created by the said L. D. Moore , much of which
was created while and due to his making the race for mem-
ber of the General Assembly as aforesaid , and
Whereas , the said widow , Mrs. Sarah Stephens Moore , needs an
allowance for her support and maintenance .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
That there is hereby appropriated out of the special
extra legislative session fund an amount equal to
$10.00 for each and every legislative day of said ses-
sion , up to and including the date of sine die adjourn-
ment , to be paid to Mrs. Sarah Stephens Moore ,
widow of L. D. Moore , and the Commissioner of
Finance is hereby directed to draw his warrant upon
the Treasurer in favor of the said Mrs. Sarah
Stephens Moore in an amount equal to the payment of
$10.00 per diem for each and every legislative day
of said Special Extraordinary Session of the Gen-
eral Assembly.
Passed, enrolled and approved June 12 , 1944 .

CHAPTER 1 .
(S. B. 1 )
AN ACT appropriating money for the operation , maintenance ,
support and functioning of the State Government of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , including each of the various offices ,
departments , boards , commissions , institutions , and sub-
divisions of the State Government , and the purchase of record
books as provided by Section 28.130 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes of 1942 , judiciary and court costs , Confederate Pen-
sions , the Frankfort Cemetery , Jefferson , Kenton and Harlan
County fees , and for defraying the expenses of all other State
obligations for ( a ) the fiscal years ending June 30 , 1945 , and
June 30, 1946 , ( b ) the fiscal years of the Department of High-
ways ending March 31 , 1945 , and March 31 , 1946 ; designating
the sources and funds from which said appropriations are
made , and providing for the establishment of certain revolv-
ing funds specifically mentioned and the investment of cer-
tain funds ; providing for money refunds ; prescribing certain
powers and duties of the Commissioner of Finance , the Gov-
ernor , the State Treasurer , and other public officials , institu-
tions , boards , commissions and agencies with respect thereto ;
authorizing and empowering the Commissioner of Finance
with the approval of the Governor to limit , restrict and super-
vise expenditures of all money appropriated herein and here-

after by the General Assembly in the Second Extraordinary
Session of 1944 , and to reduce or adjust each of the appro-
priations in the manner herein provided ; providing for cer-
tain appropriations to be limited to specific purposes and
barring the use of appropriations for certain purposes ; re-
pealing all blanket and continuing appropriations not pro-
vided for in this Act and appropriations made by any previous
Act or Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , and repealing all laws or parts of laws in con-
flict with any of the provisions of this Act ; enacting each
section and each sub -section as a separate or specific appro-
priation , and declaring an emergency to exist .

(k) Capital Outlays . There hereby is appro-
priated out of the cash balance which will be in the
State Treasury to the credit of the General Expendi-
ture Fund at the end of the present biennium ending
June 30 , 1944 , the sum of $5,300,000.00 for the follow-
ing uses and purposes , to -wit :
( 1 ) For acquiring lands , the construction of build-
ings , the reconstruction of buildings , the purchase and
installation of equipment and furnishings for the
buildings so constructed or reconstructed , which
equipment and furnishings are of a permanent nature ,
for the following institutions , to -wit : The Western
State Hospital , at Hopkinsville ; Central State Hos-
pital , at Lakeland ; Eastern State Hospital , at Lex-
ington ; Kentucky Feeble - Minded Institute , at Frank-
fort ; House of Reform , at Greendale ; School for the
Deaf , at Danville ; School for the Blind, at Louisville ;
a War Memorial Field House at the University of
Kentucky , Lexington ; the Hazelwood Sanatorium ;
and State Tuberculosis Sanatoriums to be established
as provided in House Bill No. 147 of the 1944 Regular
Session of the General Assembly . Of the $5,300 ,-
000.00 herein appropriated , not less than $1,500,000.00
thereof shall be spent for the construction , equipment
and furnishing of State Tuberculosis Sanatoriums .
The $ 5,300,000.00 herein appropriated shall be in-
vested in United States Government Bonds on or be-
fore the 10th day of July , 1944 , and said appropriation
shall take precedence over any other appropriation

CHAPTER 1 507
or encumbrance made or authorized by this Act and
shall constitute a withdrawal as of July 1 , 1944 , of
$5,300,000.00 cash which will be in the State Treasury
at the close of the current fiscal year which will end
June 30 , 1944. All interest which accrues on the
Bonds purchased as herein directed shall be held , used
and expended for the same purpose and only for the
purposes for which the principal of said Bonds may
be used .
It shall be the duty of the Commissioner of Finance
and the State Treasurer to invest said $5,300,000.00 in
United States Government Bonds , and it shall be the
duty of the State Treasurer to safely hold and keep
said Bonds for the uses and purposes herein provided .
The Governor , with the advice of the Commissioner
of Finance , the Commissioner of Revenue , the Com-
missioner of Welfare , and the Treasurer , may con-
tract for capital improvements for any of the institu-
tions hereinabove specified , and shall make direct ap-
proval of all expenditures from the appropriation
herein made . Each of such contracts shall be awarded
to the lowest and best bidder pursuant to competitive
bids ; and the Commissioner of Finance and the State
Treasurer shall, from time to time , cash or sell a suf-
ficient amount of the United States Government
Bonds directed to be purchased out of the appropria-
tion herein made to pay the estimates on such con-
tracts awarded as herein provided , as and when such
estimates become due .
None of the Bonds purchased as provided in this
Act shall be cashed or sold except as and for the uses
and purposes herein provided . Any part of this ap-
propriation not expended during the biennial period
beginning July 1 , 1944 , and ending June 30 , 1946 , shall
not lapse , but shall continue until twenty -four months
after a proclamation is issued by the President of the
United States terminating the present National

508 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Emergency . Any balance of this appropriation which
shall not have been expended or encumbered during
the aforesaid biennium may be expended or encum-
bered at any time during such extended period for
the uses and purposes and subject to the restrictions
and limitations herein provided .
No part of this appropriation may be transferred
for use or used for any other uses or purposes than
those hereinabove expressly provided ; and this ap-
propriation may not be reduced by allotment or other-
wise , except by expenditures made therefrom as here-
inabove expressly provided .
PART TWO
REVOLVING , TRUST AND AGENCY FUNDS
There is hereby appropriated to the various pro-
fessional boards or other boards , commissions , in-
stitutions , agencies or subdivisions of the State Gov-
ernment for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1945 ,
and for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1946 , all of
the fees (which include fees for board and room ,
athletics , student activities ) and rentals , admittances ,
sales , licenses collected by law , contributions , gifts ,
subventions , and other miscellaneous receipts pro-
duced by any of the following professional boards , or
other boards , commissions , institutions , agencies , and
subdivisions of the State Government , except as
otherwise specifically provided , for the use and bene-
fit of each of the below-named professional boards ,
or other boards , commissions , institutions , agencies
and subdivisions of the State Government , the re-
ceipts which are received by each of the respective
professional boards , or other boards , commissions , in-
stitutions , agencies or subdivisions of the State Gov-
ernment which receipts are placed to the credit of a
Revolving Fund out of which shall be established

CHAPTER 1 509
separate Revolving Fund Account for the use and
benefit of each separate professional board or other
boards , commissions , institutions , agencies and/or
subdivisions of the State Government . Withdrawal
from the said Revolving Fund by each of the said pro-
fessional boards , or other boards , commissions , insti-
tutions , agencies , and/or subdivisions of the State
Government may be made when and if needed for
betterment and ordinary recurring expenses of oper-
ation properly approved by the Department of
Finance , Division of Accounts and Control , and said
requisitions shall not be approved unless there is to
the credit of the Revolving Fund of such profes-
sional board, or other board , commission , institution ,
agency and/or subdivision of the State Government ,
a free and unencumbered sum equal to the amount of
the requisitions . The total amount any agency may
requisition from the Revolving Fund Account through
the Department of Finance , shall not during the
fiscal year ending June 30 , 1945 , nor during the fiscal
year ending June 30 , 1946 , exceed the amount placed
to the credit of the Revolving Fund for such year , out
of which has been established a Revolving Fund Ac-
count for each separate agency during such fiscal
year, plus any balance which said professional board ,
or other board , commission , institution , agency
and/or subdivision of the State Government may
have had forwarded from the preceding year , on or
before September 30 of the then current fiscal year ,
by the Department of Finance .

PART THREE
DEPARTMENT OF HIGHWAYS
State Road Fund . There shall be established( 1 )
for the purpose of paying all cost of operation and
maintenance of the State Highway Department and
for carrying on its activities for the fiscal year begin-
ning April 1 , 1944 , and ending March 31 , 1945 , and
for the fiscal year beginning April 1 , 1945 , and ending
March 31 , 1946 , a State Road Fund ; and there is here-
by appropriated to the State Road Fund all the funds
now realized out of the State revenues , authorized
now by law or that may hereafter be authorized by
law , or any additional or other revenues that may be

CHAPTER 1 515
imposed by law for the benefit of public roads and all
funds realized from taxes now collected by law or that
may hereafter be imposed or collected by law for the
benefit of said department and its activities . Such
receipts and revenues shall constitute the State Road
Fund .
There is hereby appropriated to the State Highway
Department all monies received and placed to the
credit of the State Road Fund during the fiscal years
ending March 31 , 1945 , and March 31 , 1946 , not other-
wise appropriated in this Act , for use and benefit of
the State Road System in construction , maintenance ,
and repair of roads , and for all the activities and
duties of said Department , as prescribed by law .
(2) Division of Administration . There is hereby
appropriated out of the State Road Fund for each
of the fiscal years ending March 31 , 1945 , and March
31 , 1946 , for ordinary recurring administrative ex-
penses of operation , including the cost of printing
Treasury checks for the department , the sum of
$600,000.00 .
(3) Division of Rural Highways . There is here-
by appropriated out of the State Road Fund for each
of the fiscal years ending March 31 , 1945 , and March
31 , 1946 , to be used by the Division of Rural High-
ways , for the improvement , reconstruction and main-
tenance of county roads and bridges which have not
been accepted by the State Highway Commission for
maintenance , said appropriation to be expended in
accordance with KRS 179.410 to 179.430 , the sum of
$2,000,000.00 . (Of this amount , not more than 10 per-
cent may be used for Administrative purposes for
Rural Highway Division in the Highway Department .
When the funds hereby appropriated have been allo-
cated to the respective counties as provided by KRS
179.410 , any unexpended balance remaining to the
credit of any county from appropriations of previous

516 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
years shall be carried forward and added to the allo-
cation of the current year for each county .

and March 31 , 1946 , for the ordinary recurring ex-

PART FOUR
1.

partment, board , commission , institution , agency , di-
vision , or subdivision of the State Government shall
be covered into the State Treasury promptly , as pro-
vided by statute , through the Department of Finance
and placed to the credit of the General Expenditure

CHAPTER 1 517
Fund, excepting those revenues which are specifically
allocated to the State Road Fund or the Revolving
Fund which shall be credited to their respective funds
as provided for in this Act . The fees , receipts , and/or
revenue of every officer , department , division , sub-
division , commission , board , institution , or agency of
the State Government shall be covered into the State
Treasury promptly , as provided by statute , and
placed to the credit of the General Expenditure Fund
and shall be used to defray the expenses of the State
Government and the appropriations made from the
General Expenditure Fund in this Act , unless other-
wise specifically appropriated to one of the profes-
sional boards , commissions , institutions , agencies , or
subdivisions of the State Government for Revolving
Fund purposes ; such agencies shall be only those that
are listed in this Act as having been approved for
Revolving Fund Accounts .
2 .
The appropriations herein made for the ordinary
recurring expenses and extraordinary expense and
capital outlay for any officer , department , board , com-
mission , institution , and subdivision of the State Gov-
ernment of the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall not
be available for expenditure until allotted as now pro-
vided by law, and such appropriations as specifically
made in this Act are hereby declared to be maximum
and conditional appropriations except when the Gov-
ernor determines an emergency exists in any depart-
ment , board, commission , institution , or subdivision
of the State Government requiring an additional allot-
ment of funds to be made from the General Emer-
gency Fund hereby placed at his disposal and made
a specific appropriation to be used by him to meet ex-
isting emergencies so determined by him . This ap-
propriation is designated under section 4 , " EXECU-

518 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
TIVE DEPARTMENT " , Item ( d ) 1 , Part One of
this Act .
Whereas , a state of war exists in the United States ,
and the effect upon the finances of the Commonwealth
can not be ascertained at this time , and since the actual
needs of each officer , department , board , commission ,
institution , or subdivision of the State Government
can only be anticipated and not finally determined by
the Legislature , and since the total amount of revenue
accruing to the General Expenditure Fund from
which these appropriations are hereby made can only
be anticipated through existing laws and those that
may hereafter be enacted , it is the purpose and inten-
tion of this Legislature that the State expenditures
for each fiscal year covered in this Act , exclusive of
appropriations out of the State Road Fund , and ex-
clusive of revolving fund accounts , shall not exceed
an amount equal to the amount of revenues collected
and received into the treasury , and credited to the
General Expenditure Fund , during the same fiscal
year , and that there is appropriated out of the Gen-
eral Expenditure Fund for each fiscal year to meet
the appropriations made by this Act, only such an
amount as does not exceed the revenues actually col-
lected and received into the General Fund during such
year , exclusive of any unencumbered balance existing
at the close of business of the preceding fiscal year .
However , in view of the present emergency which
may materially affect revenue collections , there is
hereby appropriated out of the unencumbered cash
balance in the General Expenditure Fund as of the
close of business on June 30, 1944 , the sum of $ 3,319 ,-
050.00 , which shall constitute an Emergency Expendi-
ture Fund . The amount so appropriated shall be
credited to such Emergency Expenditure Fund by the
State Treasurer on July 1 , 1944 , and shall be held
sacred and inviolate in such fund except for use as

CHAPTER 1 519
hereinafter provided . In the event it appears at any
time during the fiscal year 1944-1945 that the revenues
that will be received during that year will not be
sufficient to meet the appropriations made by this Act
for such year , then and in that event the Commission-
er of Finance , with the advice of the Commissioner of
Revenue and the approval of the Governor , may allot
any part or all of the Emergency Expenditure Fund ,
to the extent of such deficiency of revenue , but not to
exceed $ 1,659,525.00 , to the various budget units to
which appropriations have been made , such allotment ,
in the event it shall be insufficient to meet all appro-
priations in full , to be prorated on an equal basis . If ,
after so using any portion or all of said $ 1,659,525.00
during such fiscal year , it develops that the revenues
ultimately collected and received into the State Treas-
ury during such fiscal year and credited to the Gen-
eral Expenditure Fund would have been sufficient to
have made unnecessary the use of part or all of such
Emergency Expenditure Fund , then an amount equal
to the amount so used from such fund shall be replaced
to the credit of the Emergency Expenditure Fund , out
of such revenues . During the fiscal year 1945-1946 ,
such sum as stands to the credit of the Emergency
Expenditure Fund , including such sum as may have
been replaced to the credit of the fund as above pro-
vided , shall be held sacred and inviolate , except that
if it appears , at any time during such year , that the
revenues that will be received during the year will not
be sufficient to meet the appropriations made by this
Act for such year , the Commissioner of Finance , with
the advice of the Commissioner of Revenue and with
the approval of the Governor , may allot any part or
all of such Emergency Expenditure Fund , to the ex-
tent of such anticipated deficiency of revenues , to the
various budget units to which appropriations have

520 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
been made , such allotments to be prorated on an equal
basis if insufficient to meet all appropriations in full.
It is the intention of the General Assembly that
the Emergency Expenditure Fund established under
the preceding paragraph of this Act shall be used for
no other purpose than to meet deficiencies in revenue ,
as set forth in said preceding paragraph , and that an
amount of cash equal to the amount appropriated to
such fund shall be earmarked for such fund as of July
1 , 1944. However , it is intended that advancements
may be made out of the monies to the credit of such
fund for necessary operating capital preliminary to
meeting the general budgetary requirements of either
or both of the fiscal years 1944-1945 and 1945-1946 ,
such advancements to be repaid to the fund as soon as
incoming revenues enable it to be done . Nothing in
the preceding paragraph of this Act is intended to ap-
ply to the appropriations for capital outlay made in
item (k) of section 26 of Part One of this Act .
As the conditions cannot be finally determined un-
til after the adjournment of this session of the Legis-
lature , and as the needs of each officer , department ,
board , commission , institution or subdivision of the
State Government and the total revenues to be re-
ceived into the General Fund cannot be fully deter-
mined at this time it is the intention and the purpose
of this Legislature that the Commissioner of Finance ,
with the advice of the Commissioner of Revenue and
the approval of the Governor of the Commonwealth ,
be and he is hereby authorized , empowered and di-
rected to ascertain and determine the needs of each
officer , department , board , commission , institution , or
subdivision of the State Government and to ascertain
the total revenues anticipated to be received into the
General Expenditure Fund of the Commonwealth ,
and upon the ascertainment and determination of said
facts , he, the Commissioner of Finance , in the manner

CHAPTER 1 521
set forth and as provided by general laws , is hereby
authorized , empowered and directed by the Legisla-
ture to prevent expenditures in excess of the amounts
made available by this Act for the purpose of meeting
appropriations , by equitably reducing , according to
the need, without discrimination , the appropriations
herein made to any officer , department , board , com-
mission , institution , or subdivision of the State Gov-
ernment ; provided that the power hereby vested shall
not permit any reduction of the appropriation of any
officer , department , board , commission , institution or
subdivision of the State Government that will actually
impair the necessary constitutional functions of gov-
ernment .
3.
•
To carry out the purpose of this Act for the ensuing
biennium , the Commissioner of Finance is hereby em-
powered and granted the authority to make allotments
and/or reallotments from appropriations made to the
various officers , departments , boards , commissions ,
institutions , and subdivisions of the State Govern-
ment , so as to prevent an overdraft , or a deficit in any
fiscal year for which appropriations are herein made ,
and to prevent the maximum appropriation made to
any officer , department , board, commission , institu-
tion , or subdivision of the State Government from
being exceeded .
Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any
appropriation for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1944 ,
and nothing in this Act is to be construed to amend
or alter the provisions of Chapter 42 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes pertaining to the duties and powers
of the Commissioner of Finance .
Section 47.030 to 47.100 , inclusive , of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes of 1942 , are repealed , and all con-
tinuing appropriations in existence at the time of this
Act are hereby discontinued and repealed .

522 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , the fiscal year for which appropria-
tions are made by this Act will commence on July 1 ,
1944 , and it is essential that the appropriations made
by this Act become effective at the beginning of such
fiscal year , NOW , THEREFORE ,
An emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
take effect from and after its passage and approval
by the Governor , or from and after such time as this
Act becomes a law without the approval of the Gov-
ernor .
Passed, enrolled and approved June 16 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 2
( S. R. 6. )
JOINT RESOLUTION appropriating additional compensation
for officers and employees of Senate and House .
WHEREAS , the General Assembly deems the compensation of
the officers and employees of the Senate and the House of
Representatives , as provided for in Chapter 88 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1936 , inadequate for the proper
functioning of the business of the Senate and the House dur-
ing the Second 1944 Extraordinary Session,
NOW , THEREFORE , Be it Resolved by the Gen-
eral Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. That there is hereby appropriated from
the Legislative Session Fund as additional compensa-
tion for the following officers and employees of the
Senate and the House of Representatives : Assistant
Clerk , Enrolling Clerk , Sergeant at Arms , Door-
keeper , Cloakroomkeeper , Janitor and Pages , to that
compensation as provided for in Chapter 88 of the
Acts of the General Assembly of 1936 , the sum of One
Hundred Dollars ( $ 100.00 ) per diem , effective begin-
ning with the first day of the Second 1944 Extraordi-
nary Session , for each body , for the duration of the
Second 1944 Extraordinary Session .
Sec . 2. That the aforesaid amount shall be pro
rated among the aforenamed constitutional officers

CHAPTER 3 523
and employees , according to the per diem set forth in
Chapter 88 of the Acts of the General Assembly of
1936 , and shall be by the Department of Finance
charged to the account of the aforenamed officers and
employees of the Senate and the House of Repre-
sentatives on this basis .
Sec . 3. That the Clerk of the Senate and the Clerk
of the House of Representatives are directed to issue
vouchers to the Commissioner of Finance , who shall
draw his warrant in favor of the Clerk of the Senate
and the Clerk of the House of Representatives for the
above mentioned amounts , and that the Clerk of the
Senate and the Clerk of the House of Representatives
are further directed to distribute the additional com-
pensation to the above mentioned officers and em-
ployees as provided in Chapter 88 of the Acts of the
General Assembly of 1936 .
Whereas , it is necessary that this additional com-
pensation be received by the officers and employees of
the Second 1944 Extraordinary Session in order to
expedite properly the business of the Senate and the
House of Representatives , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this resolution shall be effective
from and after its passage and approval by the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Passed , enrolled and approved June 16 , 1944 .
CHAPTER 3
(S. R. 7)
A JOINT RESOLUTION appropriating money for the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the fiscal
year of 1943-1944 , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. There is hereby appropriated out of
the General Expenditure Fund for the payment of
Salaries and other necessary expenses of the General

524 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Assembly for the fiscal year 1943-1944 the sum of
Twenty Thousand Dollars ( $20,000.00 ) .
Section 2. Whereas , the funds heretofore appro-
priated for the 1944 General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky are exhausted , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this resolution shall
become effective immediately upon its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Passed , enrolled and approved June 16 , 1944 .

An ACT relating to individuals exempt from poll tax .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

222 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1 ) All citizens of this state who are sixty-five
years of age or older are exempt from all poll taxes .
(2 ) Any honorably discharged soldier, sailor or
marine who has served ninety days or more in

into service , and who is totally disabled as evidenced
by pension certificate or award of compensation , is
exempt from all poll taxes .
